observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the
(i) Municipal Treasurer to (a) update the RPTAR/Taxpayer’s Index Card yearly and subsequently prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year; (b) provide and maintain records of delinquent taxpayers and their respective tax dues and intensify tax collection efforts on outstanding RPT and SET receivables;
(ii) Municipal Accountant to prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers required in Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and
(iii) Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to (a) reconcile their records to come up with the correct balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and their corresponding reciprocal accounts; and (b) adhere to the provisions of IPSAS 23 and MNGAS for LGUs regarding the proper treatment and presentation of advance RPT and SET payments.

b) DPC and Accounting Office maintain SCs and SLCs, respectively, and reconcile the two records periodically for proper monitoring of stocks and supplies; and
c) Inventory Committee together with the DPC (i) conduct semestral physical count of inventory items and (ii) prepare and submit RPCI to the Audit Team.

14. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the HRMO issue written policies and guidelines on the hiring of JOs as a guide in drafting their contracts and in evaluating their accomplishments.

observations.recommendation:
15. Prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit to the Audit Team after the same has been approved by the Municipal Mayor but before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities;
16. Start immediately, and complete during the year, the physical count of all PPEs of the Municipality, including road networks, in accordance with the procedures and schedule in the PIP as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and other relevant issuance; and
17. Prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCLRN, and submit the same to the Accountant for reconciliation and to the Municipal Audit Team for verification.
18. We also recommended that the Property Custodian prepare and maintain Property Cards for each class of PPE, and LRNPC for all roads and its components, respectively.
19. The Municipal Accountant: Update the PPELC for each class of PPE, and LRNLC for each local road, and reconcile these with the RPCPPE and RPCLRN, respectively; Start the cleansing of PPE accounts by following the procedures in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and based on the RPCPPE and RPCLRN.

observations.reason: Financial Incapacity of the LGU

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledger for each special account in the General Fund and prepare the corresponding financial statements to form part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance, as required under Sections 105, 107 and 110, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

observations.reason: SLs are yet to be maintained for each special account in the General Fund.

observations.recommendation:
1.15 We recommended and management agreed to comply with the following
audit recommendations:
(a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer (i) conduct regular
reconciliation of their respective records for the timely settlement of any
23

discrepancies between the Cash-in-Bank account balance as recorded
per GL and cashbook; and (ii) immediately resolve discrepancies noted
above between the records of the Accounting and Treasury Offices.
(b) Municipal Accountant (i) ensure that all Bank Reconciliation Statements
(BRS) are prepared accurately, supported by complete and valid
documentation, and reflect balances sourced from the updated GL and
SLs; (ii) promptly record all identified book reconciling items listed in
the BRS to reflect the true cash position of the Municipality; (iii) update
the SLs and reconcile the discrepancy noted between the balances in the
SLs and those reported in the financial statements; and (iv) record the
reversion of the stale check to the Cash-In-Bank account and recognize
the corresponding payables.

1.16 We further recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to
consider hiring additional qualified personnel for the Accounting Office to fill out
the vacancies left by former staff and address the current manpower shortage. This
will help ensure the timely updating of the General and Subsidiary Ledgers, the
accurate and reliable preparation of financial reports, and enhanced compliance
with applicable accounting and audit requirements.

2.12 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to (i) issue demand
letters to concerned AOs for them to liquidate their cash advances by submitting
complete and valid settlement documents or refunding applicable portion of the cash
advances, as necessary, (ii) establish a thorough monitoring of cash advances
granted to different AOs by making a schedule indicating when these cash advances
are due for liquidation, and (iii) reconcile regularly the GL and Progress Report and
settle the discrepancy amounting to ₱961,819.09.

3.19 We recommended and management agreed to comply with the following
recommendations:
(a) Municipal Treasurer (i) update the RPTAR/Taxpayer’s Index Cardy yearly; (ii)
furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with a certified list of
taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year as basis for the set-up
of RPT and SET receivables; and (iii) establish and maintain a record of all delinquent
taxpayers and their respective tax dues from prior years to improve monitoring and
facilitate collections.
(b) Municipal Accountant (i) adjust the negative balance of the Real Property Tax
(RPT) receivable account through appropriate correcting entries; (ii) coordinate
closely with the MTO to obtain the list of delinquent taxpayers and prepare/maintain
subsidiary ledgers, in accordance with Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and
(iii) make the necessary adjusting/correcting entries on the erroneous recognition of
advance RPT and SET payments; and (iv) ensure that accounting procedures in
recording RPT transactions are consistent with the provisions of IPSAS 23 and NGAS
Manual for LGUs; and
(c) Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile their respective records to arrive
at accurate and consistent balances of RPT and SET Receivables, including their
corresponding reciprocal accounts.

4.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the
necessary adjusting entries for the derecognition of the Prepaid Insurance account
amounting to ₱1,065,297.75, with the corresponding adjustment to Accumulated
Surplus/(Deficit).

5.20 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
(a) formally establish an Inventory Committee to (i) conduct a complete and
timely physical count of all inventory items in accordance with Section
124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and (ii) prepare and submit the
corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI)
within the prescribed deadlines and ensure that it accurately reflects
only the inventory items physically on hand at year-end;
(b) require all offices with custody of inventories to (i) designate a Property
Custodian responsible for maintaining the required Stock Cards (SCs)
for each inventory item; (ii) implement a standardized and complete
inventory documentation system that tracks receipts, issuances,
consumption, and balances;; and (iii) prepare and submit monthly
Requisition Issue Slips (RIS) and Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) reflecting the movement and balances of inventory items;
(c) instruct the DPC to consolidate the submitted RISs and SSMIs and
transmit them to the Accounting Office promptly to facilitate timely
and accurate recording of inventory transactions in the books of
accounts;
(d) direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) stop the practice of recognizing
inventory purchases as outright expenses and instead record them
through the appropriate inventory accounts, making necessary year-end
adjustments for remaining stocks, and (ii) prepare and maintain the
Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) and ensure the accuracy and
34

completeness of the related accounting records; and
(e) instruct the Accounting Office and DPC to coordinate and conduct
regular reconciliation of their respective inventory records to ensure
proper tracking and accountability of supplies and materials.

6.5 We recommended and management agreed to comply with the following
recommendations:
(a) Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian (i) prepare and maintain
the required accounting and property records and reports for all PPE
accounts; and (ii) reconcile their respective PPE records.
(b) Inventory Committee (i) conduct complete physical count of PPE and
submit RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to
Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and (ii) implement the
procedures prescribed on one-time cleansing of PPE account balances in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
(c) Property Custodian and Disposal Committee prepare and submit the
IIRUP together with the necessary supporting documents for the
disposal of unserviceable PPE items to prevent further loss and
deterioration of the same.

7.12 We recommended and management agreed to comply with the following
recommendations:
(a) Accounting Office (i) maintain detailed subsidiary ledgers for all CIP
accounts, including complete project information (e.g., project name,
location, date started, expected and actual completion dates, and amount
incurred); (ii) maintain and regularly update the CIPLC for all ongoing
construction projects of the Municipality to ensure efficient asset
management and enhance financial reporting accuracy; and (iii) moving
forward, ensure timely transfer of completed projects to the appropriate
PPE or infrastructure account in compliance with the NGAS Manual for
LGUs and COA guidelines.
(b) Engineering Office (i) submit to the Accounting Office the latest status
report on infrastructure projects; and (ii) henceforth regularly monitor
and report on the status of ongoing projects.

8.8 We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to (i) revise the SLs of
the Due to NGAs account; and (ii) strictly conform to the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015, in the recording of transactions.

8.9 We reiterated our recommendation, and management agreed to return the
unused balance of fund transfers to the source agency and national treasury in
compliance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994
and DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.

9.10 We recommended and management agreed to comply with the following
recommendations:
(a) Municipal Accountant account for the employee leave benefits such that
a liability is recognized as they are accrued;
(b) Municipal Budget Officer to establish an Employee Benefits Reserve
Fund to fund for all the leave benefits owed to employees; and
(c) Municipal Human Resource Management Officer have a collaborative
effort with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer for
the strict adherence to Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing
Book V of E.O. No. 292 on the accounting and funding of leave benefits.

10.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed as Special Disbursing Officer of CF
and Municipal Accountant, within seven (7) days from the release of the check,
submit the DVs for CA for confidential activities/projects with complete required
supporting documents to the COA ATL concerned in a sealed envelope with a
visible label “CONFIDENTIAL – For the COA ATL Only” and with a covering
transmittal letter enumerating the submissions.

10.9 We further recommended and the LCE agreed to continue to observe
compliance with the submission to ICFAU of CF liquidation reports supported with
documentary requirements.

11.16 We recommended and management agreed to comply with the following
recommendations:
(a) LCE (i) adhere to Section 171 of R.A. No. 7160, which grants the
exclusive power and authority to assess business taxes to the Municipal
Treasurer; and (ii) formally incorporate the use of the Presumptive
Income Level Assessment Approach (PILAA) into the Municipal
Revenue Code to support its continued application and establish clear
procedures with appropriate safeguards for its use;
(b) Municipal Treasurer (i) continue enforcing compliance with the Municipal
Revenue Code to ensure the correct collection of business taxes for the
benefit of the Municipality; and (ii) strictly require all business
establishments to submit sworn statements of gross
sales/receipts and certified Income Tax Returns (ITRs) annually, as
mandated by law; and
(c) Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer,
implement the business tax withholding policy on future payments to
suppliers, ensuring proper withholding and application in accordance
with the Municipal Revenue Code.

13.17 We recommended and management agreed to comply with the following
recommendations:
(a) MDRRMO (i) ensure that all proposed PPAs are properly planned and
aligned with eligible disaster risk reduction and management activities as
prescribed by law and (ii) submit a written explanation establishing the
direct relevance of the noted activities to disaster prevention and
mitigation, in accordance with the provisions of JMC No. 2013-1, to
justify the eligibility of their funding under the LDRRMF;
(b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) (i) strictly adhere to the appropriate
procurement modalities as prescribed under R.A. No. 9184, such as
Small Value Procurement (SVP) or Public Bidding, depending on the
nature and amount of the procurement and (ii) ensure compliance with
the documentary requirements required by the law; and
(c) Municipal Accountant (i) establish and transfer the unexpended balance
of LDRRMF from CYs 2020-2024 to special trust fund account in
accordance with Section 21 of RA No. 10121; (ii) submit the lacking
documentary requirements of subject transactions discussed in
paragraph 1.7; and (iii) ensure that all expenses charged against the
LDRRMF are fully documented with the required supporting papers
prior to effecting payment, in accordance with pertinent laws and COA
Circulars and (iv) submit to the Audit Team the DVs pertaining to the
noted transactions above.

15.5 We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to (i) immediately
submit the DVs with their supporting documents pertaining to the noted
transactions; and (ii) strictly comply with the submission of DVs and supporting
documents within the reglementary period as prescribed by COA Circular No.
2009-006.

16.15 We recommended and management agreed to comply with the following
recommendations:
(a) LSB (i) prioritize the preparation and approval of the SEF budget in a
timely manner to provide uninterrupted support for the operation and
maintenance of public schools within the municipality (ii) ensure the
future distribution of the copy of the SEF Budget to the Municipal
Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer be
promptly accomplished and shall be supported with proper transmittal
records from the concerned departments; and (iii) comply with
requirements of the JC on the submission of quarterly reports and
annual reports on SEF utilization to the relevant agencies within
prescribed timeline.
(b) LCE instruct personnels to promptly update the postings of the SEF
Utilization Report in the three (3) conspicuous places to ensure greater
public accessibility of SEF utilization information.
(c) Bids and Awards Committee and Municipal Accountant (i) adhere to the
requirements of the 2016 revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and (ii) submit
the lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team to prove the
propriety and regularity of disbursement transactions noted.

17.5 We recommended and management agreed to (i) continue to observe the
rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR;
and (ii) design a more effective strategy that will allow a timelier recording of
transactions to facilitate the reconciliation between actual taxes withheld and
remitted.

18.7 We recommended and management agreed to (i) continue to withhold and
remit the loan amortization and mandatory contributions to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG,
and PhilHealth; (ii) identify the reason that caused the over remittances made to the
GSIS and PhilHealth and adjust the books when warranted; and (iii) exercise
caution by thoroughly examining employees’ index cards/records to avoid payments
in excess of the due amount.

1.14. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
d. coordinate with the Inventory Committee, Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Engineer in facilitating the conduct of inventory of all local roads
within the LGU’s jurisdiction;
e. maintain subsidiary records on the roads and road components for each road
network;
f. discontinue assigning residual values in computing depreciation, and make
appropriate adjustments thereon to correct the understatement of the
Accumulated Depreciation and Government Equity accounts.
1.15. We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with information on the complete
description and segregated cost components of each road project.
1.16. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
c. Independently maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map and the
Local Road Network Property Cards; and
d. Render the Report on Local Road Network at year-end.

2.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting
entries to correct recording errors in the affected accounts from the General Fund
and Trust Fund books, to properly account for the transfer of the unexpended
LDRRM Fund balances. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant shall record and
make the transfer of any unexpended balance of LDRRMF (QRF & MOOE) and its
corresponding cash, from the General Fund to Trust Fund, at year-end.

3.11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the members of the
Municipal Planning Development Council, through the MPD Coordinator, expedite
the implementation of prior and current years’ unimplemented development PPAs.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
(a) Strictly adhere to the provisions on the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances set forth under COA Circular No. 97-002;
(b) Stop the practice of approving additional CAs to officers and employees whose previous CA has not been liquidated; and
(c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) examine and investigate the unaccounted balance of the Advances to Officers and Employees account and make necessary adjustments; and (ii) revise the Progress Report on CAs that will present data on a per CA basis indicating the necessary details such as the detailed purpose and date of the CA.
observations.reason: (a) Reiterated in Part II of this Report; (b) Implemented; (c)(i) Reiterated in Part II of this Report; (c)(ii) Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We commended Management for the efficient collection performance for business taxes.
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to
(a) adhere to Section 171 of R.A. No. 7160 which grants the exclusive power and authority to assess business taxes to the Municipal Treasurer;
(b) direct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) continue to comply and enforce the Municipal Revenue Code so that correct business tax will accrue for the benefit of the Municipality and (ii) require the submission of a sworn statement of the business’ gross sales/receipt and copy of income tax return to ensure that the basis declared by the taxpayers are reliable and accurate and
(c) require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to implement the business tax withholding policy on future money payments to suppliers with business offices in La Paz in accordance with the Municipal Revenue Code.
observations.reason: (a) Reiterated in Part II of this Report; (b)(i) Implemented; (b)(ii) Reiterated in Part II of this Report; (c) Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a) strictly adhere to Item No. 6.1.5 of the Joint Circular by limiting the cash advance for CF to a three-month requirement; and
b) require the Municipal Accountant to: i) submit all DVs for CF cash advances to the COA ATL within seven (7) working days from the release of check with complete supporting documents; and ii) submit immediately to the Audit Team the documents evidencing the 2/3 votes of LPOC for the approval of POP and release of confidential fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the
(a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer regularly reconcile their records for the timely settlement of the discrepancies between the Cash-in-Bank account balance as recorded per GL and cashbook;
(b) Municipal Accountant (i) continuously prepare and submit the required monthly BRS; (ii) cause the immediate recording of book reconciling items identified in the BRS to present the accurate financial position of the Agency; (iii) request records from the Treasury Office to effect closing in the GL the bank accounts that are no longer existing; (iv) record the reversion of the stale check to the Cash-In-Bank account and
(c) Municipal Treasurer (i) strictly observe the provision of GAAM and LTOM on the issuance of official receipts in acknowledgment of any payment received and (ii) ensure that all collections are accounted for at all times.
observations.reason: (a) Reiterated in Part II of this Report; (b)(i) Reiterated in Part II of this Report; (b)(ii) Reiterated in Part II of this Report; (b)(iii) Reiterated in Part II of this Report; (b)(iv) Reiterated in Part II of this Report; (c)(i) Implemented; (c)(ii) Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to
(i) fully implement the use of the HGDG and PIMME tools to measure the actual cost attributed to gender-responsiveness and know if the existing mechanism responds to the basic 5% requirement of the law; and
(ii) continue its endeavor in the proper monitoring and effective implementation of its GAD PPAs.
observations.reason: (i) Reiterated in Part II of this Report; (ii) Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management, headed by the Local Chief Executive (LCE), agreed to
(i) continue to ensure optimal use of the DF so that socio-economic programs and projects that are beneficial to the LGU’s constituents are timely implemented; and
(ii) comply with the rules on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF for eligible projects pursuant to Item 3.2.5 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Bids and Awards Committee and concerned officials of the Municipality (i) comply with the requirements under the 2016 revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and (ii) submit the lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team to prove the propriety and regularity of disbursement transactions noted.
Lastly, we recommended and the Management agreed that BAC Secretariat and concerned officials submit copies of contracts entered into by the Municipality and/or issued POs within five days from the execution thereof in consonance with COA Circular No. 2009-01.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and LCE agreed to direct the
(a) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and concerned officials of the agency to strictly follow the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and services and comply with the procedures and documentary requirements required by the aforementioned law; and
(b) Municipal Accountant to (i) transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF from CYs 2020-2023 to special trust fund account in accordance with Section 21 of RA No. 10121; (ii) submit the lacking documentary requirements of subject transactions discussed in paragraph 1.7; and (iii) ensure that all expenses charged under the LDRRMF be fully documented with the required supporting papers before effecting payment.
We further recommended and the Management agreed to submit the revised AIP and the resolution and declaration of state of calamity as required by NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.reason: (a) Reiterated in Part II of this Report; (b)(i) Reiterated in Part II of this Report; (b)(ii) Reiterated in Part II of this Report; (b)(iii) Reiterated in Part II of this Report; Further recommendation: Implemented

observations.recommendation:

We also recommended and the Management agreed to return the unused balance of fund transfers to the source agency and national treasury in compliance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.
Further, we recommended and the Management agreed to utilize the financial assistance received from various government agencies to their intended purposes to realize the benefits that can be derived thereon.

observations.recommendation:

(i) harmonize the SWMP with the annual budgets of the Municipality;
(ii) oversee the implementation and completion of programmed SWM projects and interventions to ensure that the intended outcomes are fully achieved; and
(iii) submit a comprehensive Accomplishment Report with information on the appropriations, funding source, expenditures, target/goals and accomplishments.

(i) remit the HDMF premiums within the prescribed period as provided in the HDMF Circular No. 275;
(ii) properly monitor the remittances of premiums to avoid overpayment; and that
(iii) Municipal Accountant provide documents and/or justify the adjustment made at year-end.

(i) continue to comply with BIR RMO No. 23-2014 and other BIR rules and regulations pertaining to withholding of taxes; and
(ii) provide documents and/or justify the adjustment made at year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to
(i) remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period provided in the National Health Insurance Act; and that
(ii) Municipal Accountant provide documents and/or justify the adjustment made at year-end.
observations.reason: (i) Implemented; (ii) (no additional reason stated)

observations.recommendation:
We recommend and Management agreed to
(i) remit the GSIS premiums and loan repayments within the period prescribed in Section 14 of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 8291; and that
(ii) Municipal Accountant provide documents and/or justify the adjustment made at year-end.
observations.reason: (i) Implemented; (ii) (no additional reason stated)

observations.recommendation:
1. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary correcting journal entries in the General Fund and Trust Fund books for the cash transfer made in 2024 for the unexpended 2023 LDRRM Fund.
2. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant revert back to the General Fund the unutilized LDRRM Fund of more than five years in the Trust Fund.
3. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant should record the transfer of unexpended balance of LDRRMF (QRF & MOOE) from the General Fund to Trust Fund at yearend, and also record the reversion of unutilized LDRRMF of more than five years in the Trust Fund to the General Fund.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented
Reverted thru JEV No. 200-2024-06-183
Implemented
Not Reiterated in this Audit Report. Please refer to Observation No. 2 of Part II Implemented

observations.recommendation: 4. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record progress payments on civil works contracts to the appropriate construction in progress (CIP) accounts during the construction period, and close only such CIP account to its proper asset account upon its completion.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 6. We recommended that the Municipal Planning Officer, together with the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant facilitate the reversion of the unexpended balances of completed and non-moving PPAs in the 20% Development Fund (DF).
observations.managementAction: Reverted per Approriation Ordinance No. 1 Series of 2024 dated August 12, 2024.

observations.recommendation: 7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the focal person of the Municipal Peace and Order Council, obtain the PNP Certification containing the concrete details of the peace and order situation pertaining to the peace and order situation when allocating Confidential Funds from the budget for Peace and Order Programs to justify the need for such allocation; and include the said PNP Certification as an integral part of the Municipal Peace and Order Public Safety Plan.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2025 the Chief of Police made a budget proposal, the Municipal Mayor, together with the focal person of the Municipal Peace and Order Council, obtain the PNP Certification pertaining to the peace and order situation of the Municipality. As of 3rd quarter of 2024, no disbursement was made on the Confidential Fund.

observations.recommendation: 8. We recommended that the LGU require all contractors to comply with the submission of the CSHP for the protection and welfare of the workers and the public. Moreover, proceed with contract signing only when all documents that are required as part of the contract, particularly the CSHP, are complete and submitted to the BAC.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 10. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the assistance of the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan, the reversion of ₱783,607.58 to make the funds available for other development projects upon its subsequent enactment.
observations.managementAction: Reverted thru Approriation Ordinance No. 1 Series of 2024 dated August 12, 2024.

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor include, under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP, a list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, and to implement the planned priority projects in a timely manner, in order to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Included in the approved LDRRMFIP for 2024.

observations.recommendation: 21. Post the NOA and NTP in the PhilGEPS website within three (3) and fifteen (15) calendar days from issuance, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. The BAC Secretariat already made a printout of the posting of NOA and NTP in the PhilGEPS.

observations.recommendation: 25. Designate a Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Officer who will monitor the lessees’ compliance of the terms and conditions of the contract of lease, policies, rules and regulations promulgated for their observance, and assist in declaring vacant stalls of violators, if the personnel/officer in charge is not designated.
observations.managementAction: Issued EO No. 1 Series of 2012 "Creating a MEEDO (Economic Enterprise and Development Office) To Oversee, Manage and Superintend the Operation of the New Public Market) To Oversee, Manage and Superintend the Operation of the New Public Market but failed to designate a personnel/officer in charge.
observations.reason: Failed to designate a personnel/officer in charge.

observations.recommendation: 26. Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the books based on the documents secured by management.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were already made by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: 28. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should require the Municipal Treasurer as well as the Revenue Collection Clerk to strictly adhere the provisions of Sections 69 of PD 1445 and Section 32 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Vol. 1 on the remittance/deposit of collections and to stop the practices of not depositing collections intact daily or the next banking day.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. Deposits are already made on a daily basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant file with COA the request for authority to derecognize the Construction in Progress – Infrastructure Asset account from the books in order that the Statement of Financial Position of the LGU will present fair and reliable balances.
observations.reason: According to Management, they are still in the process of completing the needed documents in filing the said request for authority with COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the assistance of the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan, the reversion of ₱783,607.58 to make the funds available for other development projects upon its subsequent enactment.
observations.reason: According to Management, the offices involved have yet to consolidate the said amounts, and have yet to agree on the needed priority project (s) for its presentation to MDC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to include, under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP, a list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, and to implement the planned priority projects in a timely manner, in order to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the Municipality.
observations.reason: According to Management, the MDRRMC has yet to deliberate on the matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor seek assistance from the Provincial Government, as well as the BLGF, on the reassessment of real properties, as well as the tax remedies to be availed of pertaining to the delinquent taxpayers, and develop their own strategies to intensify the collection of real property taxes.
observations.reason: According to Management, some of the amounts cannot be collected anymore as it has already been delinquent for more than five (5) years, in which, according to RA 7160, the taxpayer involved cannot be anymore compelled to pay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the heads of departments and operating units, most especially the HRMO, assess the agency-wide personnel needs and create a staffing pattern for the LGU, which shall be the basis in the hiring of Contract of Service and Job Order personnel.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the HRMO issue written policies and guidelines on the hiring of JOs as a guide in drafting their contracts and in evaluating their accomplishments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the Inventory Committee to:

Prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit to the Audit Team after the same has been approved by the Municipal Mayor but before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities;

Start immediately, and complete during the year, the physical count of all PPEs of the Municipality, including road networks, in accordance with the procedures and schedule in the PIP as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and other relevant issuance; and

Prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCLRN, and submit the same to the Accountant for reconciliation and to the Audit Team for verification.

We also recommended that the Property Custodian prepare and maintain Property Cards for each class of PPE, and LRNPC for all roads and its components, based on the RPCPPE and RPCLRN, respectively.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Update the PPELC for each class of PPE, and LRNLC for each local road, and reconcile these with the RPCPPE and RPCLRN, respectively; and

Start the cleansing of PPE accounts by following the procedures in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and based on the RPCPPE and RPCLRN.
observations.reason: According to Management, the Inventory Committee did not convene and has not yet prepared the Physical Inventory Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary documents on the dormant receivable accounts with evidences that the same could no longer be collected.  Likewise, management should file with COA, the request for write-off  of dormant accounts, duly supported by evidences showing efforts made to collect the accounts, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: Difficulty in gathering the supporting documents required in filing the request for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board prioritize the construction of the MRF to hasten the closure of the open dumpsite and to ensure the protection of public health.
observations.reason: Difficulty in transporting materials due to poor access road.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Inventory Committee undertake an annual inventory of all insurable property of the agency and submit the report to GSIS and furnish a copy to the COA Auditor on or before October 31 of ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: Inventory of PPEs to be made and inclusion of insurance premiums in Annual Budget.
observations.reason: Not all insurable PPEs were provided with budget

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Designate a Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Officer who will monitor the lessees’ compliance of the terms and conditions of the contract of lease, polices, rules and regulations promulgated for their observance, and assist in declaring vacant the stalls of violators, if any.
observations.managementAction: Issued EO No. 1 Series of 2012 “Creating a MEEDO (Economic Enterprise and Development Office) To Oversee, Manage and Superintend the Operation of the New Public Market but failed to designate a personnel/officer in charge.
observations.reason: For Proposal to SB

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the books based on the supporting documents secured by management.
observations.reason: Waiting for the additional documents needed for the adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledger for each special account in the General Fund and prepare the corresponding financial statements to form part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance, as required under Sections 105, 107 and 110, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.reason:
Lack of accounting personnel who will take charge in the recording of financial transactions of the Economic Enterprise.
Only subsidiary ledgers were maintained. The budget of each economic enterprise is still incorporated in the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should require the Municipal Treasurer as well as the Revenue Collection Clerk to strictly adhere the provisions of Sections 69 of PD 1445 and Section 32 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Vol. 1 on the remittance/deposit of collections and to stop the practices of not depositing collections intact daily or the next banking day.
observations.reason: Limited out of town travel due to COVID 19.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the current portion of the loans payable.
observations.managementAction: Management’s action plan was for the report to be adjusted.
observations.reason: The Management already identified the current portion of the Loans Payable, yet the total outstanding balance of the loans was still recorded as noncurrent as at yearend.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to reconcile its records with the source agency’s records in order to reflect the correct balance in the financial statements of the financial position.
observations.managementAction: Management’s action plan was for the books to be adjusted.
observations.reason: Although adjustments were made, there was still a variance noted between the Due to LGUs account versus the confirmed balance from the source agency. This is reiterated in the CY 2023 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to submit the Sangguniang approval on the disbursements of the lumpsum appropriation for appropriate audit action.
observations.managementAction: Submit the Sangguniang approval on the disbursements of the lumpsum appropriation.
observations.reason: Review of the CY 2023 Annual Budget revealed that the appropriation for the FMR under 20% LDF was still in lumpsum. This is reiterated in the CY 2023 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a. The Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the Accounting Office records and avail the one-time cleansing of non-existing / missing PPE items granted to government agencies to ensure that reported PPE balances are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability;
b. The GSO to dispose of the unserviceable properties immediately and upon application, be inspected and appraised by the concerned Appraisal Committee; and
c. The GSO to inform and submit documents to the accounting department upon disposal of the unserviceable properties so that necessary adjusting entries be taken for the derecognition of the unserviceable properties.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to complete the reconciliation and avail the one-time cleansing; GSO to dispose of the unserviceable properties; GSO to inform and submit documents to the accounting department upon disposal.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee only submitted a list of all PPEs for the application of one-time cleansing. Still for Further verification.

observations.recommendation:
1.13 We recommended that Management:
 Instruct the Inventory Committee of the MLGU to:
 Conduct yearly and complete physical count and inventory of PPE. See to it that all columns in the RPCPPE shall contain the required important details on PPE. For the unserviceable /damaged property and equipment, use the template – Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP); and
 Henceforth, furnish a copy of the RPCPPE to the General Services Office and Accounting Office for reconciliation with their PPE records. Submit also a copy to the Audit Team for verification not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS.
 For discrepancy/ies that would be noted between actual physical count of PPE vis updated GSO and Accounting PPE records, investigate the cause and prepare corresponding report. Also, instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare separate subsidiary ledger for such difference/s; and
 For the unserviceable/damaged property and equipment, the Property and Accounting Units comply with the prescribed procedures in COA Circular No. 89-296 on the disposal of unserviceable PPE.

2.11 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate:
 Analyze further the CIP – Infrastructure Assets account comprising the CY
2014 balance amounting to ₱9,397,576.35, afterwards, prepare the
necessary adjusting entry;
 Verify the noted differences in the above presented tables and prepare
necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the accounts;
 Submit the lacking Disbursement Vouchers and Supporting Documents for
proper verification of recorded transactions;
 Record the completed projects to their proper Asset accounts. Maintain
subsidiary ledger in order to easily account these assets in case of
physical inventory;
 Draw adjusting entry for the depreciation expenses recognized for the
Multi-purpose Building (RHU) located at Mudeng, La Paz; and
 Maintain subsidiary ledger per CIP asset per project for ready reference
and to support balances in the financial statements.

3.11 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate:
 Trace back and establish existence and determine nature of the accounts.
For receivables, measures which include sending demand letters to debtors
reminding them of their overdue obligations should be made. Only when it is
definitely determined said accounts to be uncollectible that a request for
authority to write-off the accounts together with all the necessary documents
be submitted before the Commission;
 Analyze the accounts and subsequently adjust as necessary. Prepare the
Fund Utilization Reports and or coordinate with the source agency for the
closing of the accounts; and
 For future transactions, maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers
in accordance with Section 4(e) of the NGAS to provide ready and complete
information on the accounts and to attain fair presentation of financial
statements.

4.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate make necessary
adjustments to present fairly the financial statements. Henceforth, take extra
care in recording the RPT and SET Receivable basing from the list of taxpayers
and amount due and collectible provided by the Municipal Treasurer.

5.11 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate:
 Record the funds received to their proper account. Also, subsidiary
ledgers should be maintained in order to easily account the funds received
from NGAs which were not yet implemented;
 Submit the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents together
with the SORD of the transactions relating to the deductions made to Due
to NGAs account amounting to ₱579,928.00 for further verification; and
 Analyze the noted transactions in paragraphs 5.8 and 5.9. Prepare
corresponding FUR/SORD and submit the same to the source agency/ies.
For those with FUR/SORDs which were already received by the source
agencies, draw adjusting entries to close the account Due to NGAs to
Government Equity account. The received FUR/SORDs be attached to the
JEV for verification.

6.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate made the
necessary adjusting entry for the noted under-recorded depreciation
expenses. Henceforth, properly compute and record Depreciation Expenses
to present fairly the book values of PPE.

7.11 We recommended that the –
 OIC Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-designate submit
photocopy of cashbooks and complete bank statements/snapshot of
General Fund for December 2022 in order to fully verify and reconcile the
Cash in Bank balances of the MLGU;
 Municipal Accountant-designate reconcile the cash in bank ledger
balances not only with the bank balances but also with the cashbooks to
establish correct balance of the account. Also, effect the noted accounting
differences discussed in paragraphs 7.5 to 7.8; and
 Municipal Accountant-designate and OIC Municipal Treasurer reconcile
their records at least quarterly to thresh out discrepancies and adjust their
records as necessary, to fairly present balances.

8.10 We recommended and Management agreed to:
 Require the OIC Municipal Treasurer to update and submit a copy of the
cashbooks to the Audit Team for verification;
 Require the OIC Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant - designate
to reconcile entries in the Cashbook and General Ledger to bring the
correct balance of Cash-Local Treasury account or undeposited
collections and afterwards demand the Municipal Treasurer to deposit
intact the full amount to the AGDB; and
 Ensure that at least on a quarterly basis, the OIC Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant-designate reconcile their records in compliance
with Item C, Section 181 of GAAM Volume I and Section 59 of Book 2,
Chapter 3 of Updated LTOM, to establish accurate balance of Cash-Local
Treasury account.

9.12 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate:
 Analyze further the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds
accounts, trace back differences and the nature of the accounts; and
prepare the necessary adjusting entries to bring the correct balances of
the accounts;
 Maintain subsidiary ledger for reciprocal accounts to support the balances
and provide ready information on the nature of accounts; and
 Henceforth, in the preparation of consolidated year-end financial
statements, prepare necessary elimination entry for intra-agency
receivables and payables and include disclosures thereof in the Notes to
Financial Statements in consonance with IPSAS 6 and IPSAS 1. for fair
presentation of financial statements.

10.7 We recommended the Management to instruct the:
 Municipal Budget Officer to comply with the requirements of properly
accomplishing the Obligation Requests for all expenditures. Ensure that the
Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations be maintained to
properly monitor the budget of the agency and serve as the basis to
prepare the Statement of Appropriations, Allotment, Obligations and
Balances. A copy of the registries be submitted to the Audit Team for
verification; and
 Municipal Accountant-designate do not process claims without the required
certification as to the availability of appropriation including the specific cost
centers or function/program/project where expenses will be charged.

11.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate immediately
submit to the auditor the Disbursement Vouchers and Liquidation
Documents of Cash Advances for verification.

12.9 We recommended and Management agreed to:
 Instruct the Municipal Accountant-designate and the OIC Municipal
Treasurer to see to it that Disbursement Vouchers, Payrolls, Official
Receipts, and other Financial Reports be submitted within the prescribed
reglementary period for timely examination of accounts and
communication/ correction of possible errors; and
 Municipal Accountant – designate to ensure the submission of accounts
and financial statements within the prescribed periods pursuant to Section
347 of Republic Act No. 7160, Section 70 of NGAS for LGUs, Item 4 of
COA Circular No. 2010-01 and Paragraph 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-
006. Monthly accounts should be submitted along with the journals and
financial statements and trial balances to facilitate audit and verification.

13.8 We recommended that Management:
 Submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents tabulated under
paragraph 13.2 for further verification; and
 Henceforth, see to it that guidelines on fund transfers received from other
local and national government agencies be adhered to ensure attainment
of its objectives and to facilitate judicious verification.

14.5 We recommended that Management, henceforth, cause the submission to
the Audit Team the duly accomplished and signed AAPSI, within 60 days
from the receipt of the Annual Audit Report of the LGU pursuant to the
General Provision of the GAA, to enable more effective evaluation and
validation of actions taken on prior year’s audit recommendations.

15.9 We recommended and Management agreed to:
 Revise the Revenue Code and incorporate specific provisions for the
collection of Building Permit Fees in compliance with Section 208 of PD
No. 1096;
 Account for separately the 15% share of building permit fees and other
charges, and maintain a Trust Liability account and thereafter, the Building
Official shall prepare a Program of Work detailing the manner of
utilization of the 15% share to be held under trust, to be approved by the
Local Chief Executive, and to be submitted to the DPWH Secretary thru
the NCBDO; and
 Direct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasury Office to
look into collections on Building Permit Fees and other charges from CY
2013 to present to address the communication from DPWH Central
Office, and make the necessary adjustments/remittance therein, and
henceforth, strictly enforce the full remittance of 5% DPWH Share within
the prescribed period.

16.7 We recommended and Management agreed to submit the following verifiable
supporting documents to enable proper evaluation:
 Acknowledgement Receipts showing the address and signature of the
beneficiary /recipient as well as the description, amount and quantity of
goods being received; and
 Photograph documentation of the response aid and operation of the
Municipality relative to the above expenditures, properly captioned and
dated.

17.5 We recommended and Management agreed to submit the following
verifiable documentations to enable effective evaluation on the above
program of the MLGU:
 Provide the lacking information per recipient, as tabulated under Item
17.3; and
 Submit Photograph documentations of the aid to solo parents and
assistance affected by Earthquake of the Municipality relative to the
above expenditures, properly captioned and dated.

18.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate submit:
 Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing LCE to enter into such
Contract of Service/Job Orders;
 Certified copies of Contract of Services/Job Order contracts from the
Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) of the 34 personnel; and
 Henceforth, see to it job order payments be completely documented to
Explanation the noted discrepancy between the prescribed rate in the
Contract of Service and the amount paid based on payroll of the 12
personnel.

19.8 We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to instruct the
OIC Municipal Treasurer to pay disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00
through checks direct to the payee in compliance with Sections 2.2 and 4.3.2
of COA Circular No. 97-002.

20.14 We recommended that the BAC Chairperson:
 Submit to the audit team the documentary evidence enumerated under
Items 20.5 to 20.10, to support the validity of the award of the contracts
and eventually the payments thereof; and
 Always ensure that all contracts be supported with complete
documentations required under RA No. 9184 and its IRR and other
relevant guidelines on procurement.

21.4 We recommended that the BAC Chairperson:
 Submit to the Audit Team the lacking Notice to Proceed of the two
perfected contracts for further verification; and
 Ensure the submission of government contracts within the
reglementary period sets forth in Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-
001 for a systematic and effective review process to come up with
timely and relevant audit results; and

22.9 We recommended that Management:
 Submit the Official Receipt, Surety Bond with specific ICC for the above
releases of retention money, if any; and
 For future releases, require the contractor to submit project specific ICC
along with the posted Surety Bond, to further protect the interest of the
Municipality.

23.7 We recommended that Management submit a copy of Solid Waste
Management (SWM) Accomplishment Report to verify the efforts of the MLGU
in supporting the objectives of National Solid Waste Management Framework.

24.5 We reiterated our recommendation in Observation No. 11 that the
Municipal Accountant-designate submit to the auditor the Disbursement
Vouchers and Liquidation Documents of Cash Advances for verification.

25.11 We recommended that Management instruct the:
 Sangguniang Bayan Secretary and Municipal Budget Officer to submit
the SB Resolutions for the amendments made in the original Municipal
Development Plan; and
 Municipal Accountant to incorporate breakdown of expenditures in the
20% Utilization Report to facilitate easier verification of the reported
actual disbursements.

26.14 We recommended that the:
 Municipal Accountant-designate and LDRRM Officer incorporate
breakdown of expenditures in the LDRRMF Utilization Report to
facilitate easier verification of the reported actual disbursement;
 Municipal Accountant-designate and requesting offices submit the
lacking documents and or duly accomplish the attached supporting
papers as discussed in the foregoing observations to establish the
propriety and validity of the transactions; and
 SB Secretary or Municipal Budget Officer submit the necessary
issuances for the amendments made in the original LDRRMF
Investment Plan for verification.

27.11 We recommended that the Chairperson, GAD Focal Point System,
 See to it that GAD AR for CY 2023 together with the following accompanying
reports be submitted to the assigned Audit Team within five (5) working days
from the end of the January of the preceding year pursuant to Item V of COA
Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014, and Section 4.5.C.8 of PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-
NEDA JMC No. 2016-01:
 brief summary of the reported program or project;
 copies of reported policy issuances; and
 actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and
recommendations; and
 Together with the Municipal Accountant, incorporate breakdown of
expenditures in the GAD Accomplishment to facilitate easier verification
of the reported actual disbursements.

28.7 We recommended that the MSWDO:
 Together with the Municipal Accountant-designate, incorporate
breakdown of expenditures in the 1% SC and PWD Accomplishment
Report to facilitate easier verification of the reported actual
disbursement; and
 See to it that formulated plans, programs, and projects intended to
address the concerns of Senior Citizens and Persons with Disability
be accordingly implemented. In case a PPA would no longer feasible
or another PPA seems more important and responsive, initiate the
enactment of SB Resolution to support validity and legality of the
transactions.

29.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate and MSWDO
incorporate the breakdown of expenditures in the LCPC Accomplishment
Report to facilitate easier verification of the reported actual disbursement.

30.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate:
 Submit the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for the
remittances of mandatory obligations for verification; and
 Trace/ locate and analyze the transactions recorded in the said
accounts, and subsequently prepare adjusting entry as necessary.
Otherwise, communicate to the different National Government
Agencies or Financial Institutions concerned for the
settlement/reversion of the said accounts.

31.9 We reiterated our recommendation that the OIC Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant -designate:
 Deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its
purchases and contracts as mandated by laws, rules, and regulations
to avoid impositions of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue;
and
 Prevent paying/disbursing through cash or reimbursement in the
procurement of goods and services. Relative to this, we reiterated our
recommendation that Management instruct the OIC Municipal
Treasurer to pay disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00 through checks
direct to the payee pursuant to Sections 2.2 and 4.3.2 of COA Circular
No. 97-002.

32.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate:
 Analyze the noted accounting errors and draw necessary adjusting
entries, then submit the disbursement vouchers and supporting
documents to include Alphalists, to the audit team for further
verification;
 Look into the abnormal balances and determine the cause. Draw
adjusting entry to correct the balances, if necessary. If found over-
remittances, coordinate with the BIR for possible offsetting;
 Ensure that the amount to be remitted to BIR is the amount actually
withheld and deducted from the supplier/contractor to avoid
unnecessary over-remittances; and
 Record the withholdings and remittances carefully to avoid
misrepresentations in the financial statements, attach the journal entry
voucher together with the necessary supporting documents to verify
the entries in the general journal.

33.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate always see to
it that remittances to BIR be supported with Monthly Alpha list of Payees
(MAP) pursuant to Item B.4 of RMC No. 23-2012 and Section 2 of Revenue
Regulations (RR) No. 1-2014, to avoid possible imposition of penalty by the
BIR. The MAP stamped received by BIR be attached to the DV or JEV to
form integral part of its supporting documents.

1.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
(a) Strictly adhere to the provisions on the granting, utilization, and
liquidation of cash advances set forth under COA Circular No. 97-002;
(b) Stop the practice of approving additional CAs to officers and employees
whose previous CA has not been liquidated; and
(c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) examine and investigate the
unaccounted balance of the Advances to Officers and Employees account
and make necessary adjustments; and (ii) revise the Progress Report on
CAs that will present data on a per CA basis indicating the necessary
details such as the detailed purpose and date of the CA.

3.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed to: a) strictly adhere to Item No.
6.1.5 of the Joint Circular by limiting the cash advance for CF to a three-month
requirement; and b) require the Municipal Accountant to: i) submit all DVs for CF
cash advances to the COA ATL within seven (7) working days from the release of
check with complete supporting documents; and ii) submit immediately to the Audit
Team the documents evidencing the 2/3 votes of LPOC for the approval of POP and
release of confidential fund.

4.15. We recommended and the Management agreed that the (a) Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer (i) regularly reconcile their records for the
timely settlement of the discrepancies between the Cash-in-Bank account balance as
recorded per GL and cashbook; (b) Municipal Accountant (i) continuously prepare
and submit the required monthly BRS; (ii) cause the immediate recording of book
reconciling items identified in the BRS to present the accurate financial position of
the Agency; (iii) request records from the Treasury Office to effect closing in the GL
the bank accounts that are no longer existing; (iv) record the reversion of the stale
check to the Cash-In-Bank account and (c) Municipal Treasurer (i) strictly observe
the provision of GAAM and LTOM on the issuance of official receipts in
acknowledgment of any payment received and (ii) ensure that all collections are
accounted for at all times.

5.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the GAD Focal
Point System (GFPS) to (i) fully implement the use of the HGDG and PIMME tools
to measure the actual cost attributed to gender-responsiveness and know if the
existing mechanism responds to the basic 5% requirement of the law; and (ii)
continue its endeavor in the proper monitoring and effective implementation of its
GAD PPAs.

6.5 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Accounting Office i)
immediately submit the DVs with their supporting documents pertaining to the noted
transactions; and ii) strictly comply with the submission of DVs and supporting
documents within the reglementary period as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-
006.

7.16 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (i) Municipal Treasurer
to (a) update the RPTAR/Taxpayer’s Index Card yearly and subsequently prepare
and furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of
taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis
of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year; (b)
provide and maintain records of delinquent taxpayers and their respective tax dues
and intensify tax collection efforts on outstanding RPT and SET receivables; (ii)
Municipal Accountant to prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers required in
Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and (iii) Municipal Treasurer and
Accountant to (a) reconcile their records to come up with the correct balances of the
RPT/SET Receivables and their corresponding reciprocal accounts; and (b) adhere
to the provisions of IPSAS 23 and MNGAS for LGUs regarding the proper treatment
and presentation of advance RPT and SET payments.

9.8 We recommended and the Management agreed that Municipal Accountant
and DPC to (i) prepare and maintain the required accounting and property records
and reports for all PPE accounts; and (ii) reconcile their respective PPE records.
9.9 We also recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Inventory
Committee to (i) conduct complete physical count of PPE and submit RPCPPE not
later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs; and
(ii) complete the procedures prescribed on one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

11.17 We recommended and LCE agreed to direct the (a) Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) and concerned officials of the agency to strictly follow the
provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and services and
comply with the procedures and documentary requirements required by the
aforementioned law; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) transfer the unexpended
balance of LDRRMF from CYs 2020-2023 to special trust fund account in accordance
with Section 21 of RA No. 10121; (ii) submit the lacking documentary requirements
of subject transactions discussed in paragraph 1.7; and (iii) ensure that all expenses
charged under the LDRRMF be fully documented with the required supporting
papers before effecting payment.
11.18 We further recommended and the Management agreed to submit the revised
AIP and the resolution and declaration of state of calamity as required by NDRRMC,
DBM, and DILG JMC 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

12.11 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to properly account the funds received from different government agencies and
private entities in accordance with Section 2 and 10, Volume 3 of the MNGAS for
LGUs.
12.12 We also recommended and the Management agreed to return the unused
balance of fund transfers to the source agency and national treasury in compliance
with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and DBM LBC
No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.
12.13 Further, we recommended and the Management agreed to utilize the financial
assistance received from various government agencies to their intended purposes to
realize the benefits that can be derived thereon.

13.12 We recommended and the Management agreed that the SWM Board, with
MENRO as lead office, (i) harmonize the SWMP with the annual budgets of the
Municipality; (ii) oversee the implementation and completion of programmed SWM
projects and interventions to ensure that the intended outcomes are fully achieved;
and (iii) submit a comprehensive Accomplishment Report with information on the
appropriations, funding source, expenditures, target/goals and accomplishments.

14.11 We recommended and LCE agreed to instruct (a) all concerned officials and
personnel (i) to strictly adhere to RA 9184 and its Revised IRR; and (ii) to submit the
lacking documents to the Audit Team for evaluation and examination and observe
stringent compliance on the submission of documentary requirements of
transactions; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly all claims
presented for payment prior to approval to ensure that only properly and completely
documented transactions/disbursement are paid.

15.3 We recommended and the Management agreed to (i) remit the HDMF
premiums within the prescribed period as provided in the HDMF Circular No. 275;
(ii) properly monitor the remittances of premiums to avoid overpayment; and that
(iii) Municipal Accountant provide documents and/or justify the adjustment made at
year-end.

16.4 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant (i) continue to comply with BIR RMO No. 23-2014 and other BIR rules
and regulations pertaining to withholding of taxes; and (ii) provide documents and/or
justify the adjustment made at year-end.

17.4 We recommended and the Management agreed to (i) remit the PHIC
premiums within the prescribed period provided in the National Health Insurance
Act; and that (ii) Municipal Accountant provide documents and/or justify the
adjustment made at year-end.

18.4 We recommend and Management agreed to (i) remit the GSIS premiums and
loan repayments within the period prescribed in Section 14 of the revised IRR of R.A.
No. 8291; and that (ii) Municipal Accountant provide documents and/or justify the
adjustment made at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record progress payments on civil works contracts to the appropriate construction in progress (CIP) accounts during the construction period, and close only such CIP account to its proper asset account upon its completion.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer confer with the bank pertaining to its bank balances on all its bank accounts, and accordingly, adjust the LGU’s books of accounts, particularly in Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, together with the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant facilitate the reversion of the unexpended balances of completed and non-moving PPAs in the 20% Development Fund (DF).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the focal person of the Municipal Peace and Order Council, obtain the PNP Certification containing the concrete details of the peace and order situation in the Municipality, when allocating Confidential Funds from the budget for Peace and Order Programs to justify the need for such allocation; and include the said PNP Certification as an integral part of the Municipal Peace and Order Public Safety Plan.

We recommended that the LGU require all contractors to comply with the submission of the CSHP for the protection and welfare of the workers and the public. Moreover, proceed with contract signing only when all documents that are required as part of the contract, particularly the CSHP, are complete and submitted to the BAC.

1.8 We recommended and the Management agreed for:
a. The Engineering Office to submit a report of all completed projects
with attached Certificate of Completion to the Accounting Office for
recording of these projects to the appropriate PPE accounts; and
b. The Accounting Unit to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to
reclassify the completed projects from the CIP to the proper PPE
accounts and recognize the computed depreciation thereof.

2.12 We recommended and the Management agreed for the:

a. Municipal Treasurer to only include released checks in the Monthly
Report of Check Issued for submission to the Accounting Office; and
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to scrutinize the nature
and terms of the dormant bank accounts and communicate with the
respective source agencies to determine the appropriate course of
action in order to avoid possible risk of loss of rights and ownership.

3.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to reconcile its records
with the source agency’s records in order to reflect the correct balance in
the financial statements of the financial position.

4.10 We recommended and the Management agreed to secure a separate SB
appropriation ordinance in cases of lumpsum appropriations containing
generic terms, and henceforth, instruct the concerned offices to diligently
plan and review identified PPAs and discourage approval of items
containing generic terms, otherwise, seek proper approval from the
Sanggunian, to preclude transactions relative to these lumpsum
appropriations from being suspended or disallowed in audit.

5.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to implement projects,
programs and activities in accordance with the approved program of works
and properly monitor the obligations and disbursements covered in an
appropriation for labor, materials, and other items to ensure reliability and
propriety of the project implementation.

6.17 We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a. Open a separate bank account under the Trust Fund books for the
transfer of the unexpended balance of the QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE
and ensure appropriate and complete reporting of the unutilized
LDRRMF and the applicable disclosures in the financial statements;

b. Investigate the ₱9,696,000.25 excess amount transferred to the
LDRRMF bank account in CY 2022 and take appropriate actions with
regard to the remaining balance to ascertain fair presentation of the
related accounts;

c. Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer the transfer
of the ₱2,052,101.16 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund
and shall be made available for other social services;

d. Transfer the total actual unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF
and LDRRMF-MOOE amounting to ₱3,937,526.64 to the LDRRMF bank
account under the Trust Fund Books, recognize the remaining balance
of ₱624,311.35 and make the necessary adjustments in the General
Fund and Trust Fund books; and

e. Fund, out of the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE,
programs, projects and activities identified by the MDRRMO and
incorporate it in the LDRRMFIP and AIP.

7.5 We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal
Accountant to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF
and LDRRMF-MOOE from previous years to the Special Trust Fund and
make the necessary adjustments in the General Fund and Trust Fund
books.

8.12 We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a. Ensure that expenses charged under the LDRRMF or any other
government funds shall be supported with complete and appropriate
documentation;

b. Submit to COA a satisfactory explanation and the legal basis on the
payment of traveling expenses in the total amount of ₱201,080.00,
otherwise the amount should be refunded; and

c. Ensure that the utilization of the LDRRMF is in accordance with the
pertinent laws, rules, and regulations to prevent possible invalidity of
the transactions.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the: (a) members of the Local Finance Committee (LFC) continue to ensure that the required appropriation for local DF is always observed; (b) Municipal Development Council (MDC) continue to comply the guidelines mandated by DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-1 in the utilization of the 20% DF; and (c) BAC Secretariat/concerned officials: (i) submit copies of contracts entered into by the Municipality and/or issued POs within five (5) days from execution thereof in consonance with COA Circular No. 2009-01; (ii) comply with the requirements under the 2016 revised IRRs of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001; and (iii) submit the lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team to prove the propriety and regularity of disbursement transactions noted above.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the (a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer (i) explain the discrepancies in their respective records and prioritize the reconciliation of the same; and (ii) record the reversion of the stale check to the Cash-In-Bank account; and b) Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the required monthly BRS.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the a) Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a certified list of taxpayers indicating the amount due and collectible for the year; and b) Municipal Accountant, i) upon receipt of the list, to set up immediately the RPT/SET Receivable by preparing the corresponding JEV; and ii) prepare and maintain GLs and SLs for all the accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (i) direct the Accounting Office to comply with the provision of the MNGAS on the proper recognition of procured supplies so as not to misstate financial reports; (ii) instruct the Accounting Office and GSO to maintain SLCs and SCs and observe periodic reconciliation of the two records for proper monitoring of agency assets; and (iii) compel the GSO together with the Inventory Committee to conduct semestral physical count and prepare and submit RPCI thereafter to the Audit Team.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct (a) the DPC, together with the Inventory Committee, to (i) make a complete report of RPCPPE and provide the details of each PPE item in the conduct of physical count; and (ii) allot time to ask for the PPE records maintained by the previous property custodian; (b) the Municipal Accountant and DPC to prepare and maintain the required records and reports of all PPE accounts; and (c) the Municipal Accountant to allot time and exert effort to look for the previous PPE records in order to provide a reliable provision for depreciation of PPE.

We recommended and the Management agreed to comply with the documentary requirements of R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised IRR and COA Circular No. 2012-001 and submit to the Audit Team the noted DV and the lacking documents stated above for further evaluation of the project.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct: (i) the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the DVs pertaining to paragraph 7.5; (ii) the BAC and GSO to comply with the requirement of Section 7 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the preparation of APP and Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) and indicate the mode of procurement to be adopted; (iii) the Municipal Accountant and BAC submit the required documents such OSS, SPA, Secretary’s Certificate, or Board/Partnership Resolution, as the case may be, evidencing the authority of the supplier’s purported representative to act on behalf of his/her principal and ensure compliance of the same in future procurement contracts; and (iv) the BAC and concerned officials of the agency to strictly observe the procedures in the procurement of goods prescribed by the 2016 Revised IRRs of R.A. No. 9184 and comply with the documentary requirements required by the aforementioned law.
We recommended and the Management agreed to comply with the documentary requirements of R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised IRR and COA Circular No. 2012-001 and submit the lacking documents stated above.

We reiterated our audit recommendation and the Management agreed that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) prioritize the adoption of the HGDG and PIMME tools to measure the actual cost attributed to gender-responsiveness. We further recommended and the GAD Focal Point agreed to continue their endeavor in the proper monitoring and effective implementation of GAD PPAs.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Accounting Office i) immediately submit the DVs with their supporting documents pertaining to the noted transactions; and ii) strictly comply with the submission of DVs and supporting documents within the reglementary period as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006.
We recommended and the Management agreed to fully comply with the obligation under the GSIS Act of 1997 requiring the periodic remittances of GSIS premiums and loan amortizations.
We recommended and the Management agreed to comply with its obligations under HDMF Circular No. 275 on monthly withholding and remittance of premiums and loan repayments.
We recommended and the Management agreed to remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period as provided in the National Health Insurance Act.
We recommended and the Management agreed to remit on time the monthly withholding of taxes to the BIR to avoid penalties and surcharges.
