We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to submit a report on the completed RAY 2 projects to the Municipal Accountant as basis in adjusting the balances of the affected accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts to remove the liability of the LGU over the funds received from DILG.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer furnished the Municipal Accountant a copy of the Certificate of Completion on the said projects.

Adjusting entries were made accordingly by the Municipal Accountant in the Trust Fund books under JEV Nos. 2023-12-358 and 359.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify and reclassify/close the items of PPEs in the LGU’s books of accounts that cost below the new capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00 to the appropriate accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reflect the change in accounting policy in the CY 2023 financial statements by restating the amounts from prior periods of affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entry was made by the Municipal Accountant in the General Fund books under JEV No. 2023-12-2745.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare the Disbursement Voucher, in favor of the Bureau of Treasury, for the return of the balances of the downloaded funds from the national government under Trust Fund totaling ₱57,255.09; and


revert the balances in the General Fund totaling ₱629,249.73 and inform the Municipal Budget Officer of such reversion after confirming with the Municipal Treasurer the availability of the corresponding cash.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality disbursed the amount under Check no. 4061984 dated 8/11/23 under Trust Fund, and recorded under JEV No. 2023-08-224

Reverted the balances and recorded in the General Fund books under JEV No. 2023-12-2741

observations.recommendation:
We also recommended that, in subsequent procurement, the Supply Officer:

issue a pre-numbered approved Requisition and Issue Slip upon release of any kind of supplies and materials to the end-users; and

prepare the weekly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) duly supported with pre-numbered RIS issued and Distribution Lists prepared by end-user departments, and submit the same thereafter to the Municipal Accountant to reduce his accountability.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the appropriate Inventory account all supplies and materials upon purchase, except those purchased out of the petty cash fund, and record the related expense only when supported with the SSMI accompanied with pre-numbered RISs and Distribution Lists from the end-users for inventories held for distribution.

observations.reason: According to Management, the BAC still failed to post the NOA and NTP within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor sign a Memorandum of Agreement with any government agency when all the necessary information is completely incorporated therein, specifically the duration of the implementation of the program to set the parameters in the program implementation.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant:

coordinate with the Program Coordinator on the submission to the Audit Team of the needed documents to validate the implementation of the program; and

prepare and submit immediately the FUR to DOLE, copy furnished the Audit Team, upon receipt of the lacking documents.

We recommend that in future transactions, the MDRRMC, through the MDRRMO, incorporate in the annual/revise LDRRMF Investment Plan and the Annual/Supplemental Investment Plan, all programs, projects and activities to be charged to LDRRMF, including subsequent changes thereon, duly supported with proper authority through Resolutions from MDRRMC, MDC and the Sangguniang Bayan.

We further recommend that the Municipal Mayor authorize, and the Municipal Budget and Municipal Accountant process payments, for the utilization of the LDRRMF only for PPAs which are included in the LDRRMF Investment Plan and integrated in the AIP to ensure that the transactions are duly authorized.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant:

Require the suppliers of the transactions in nos. 2 to 5 in Table 2, to pay the retention money or post a special bank guarantee as warranty security for the goods delivered; and

Require Warranty Security in future procurements of the LGU, before payment is made, to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Health Officer and Municipal Accountant submit the following supporting documents to validate the eligibility and entitlement of the claimants, and to support the completeness, validity and authenticity of the payments.

Pre-determined list of the frontline HRHs and HWs, including the BHWs prepared by the Local Health Board and validated by the Department of Health authorized representative;
Duly accomplished time record of recipients of COVID-19 AHDP and SRA; and
Accomplishment Report for the Job Order and BHWs personnel.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant require the persons concerned to refund the amount received by the JOs totaling ₱27,727.26.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer submit an explanation, justification or any form of certification from the department head concerned that the savings it generated from an item of appropriation is no longer needed because the work, activity or purpose by which it was authorized has been satisfactorily completed, discontinued or abandoned.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor approves realignment of savings of an item of appropriation to augment another item of appropriation ONLY when supported with justification or certification from the official concerned as to the source of such savings.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant obligate and utilize the COVID-19 Pandemic Fund only for its specific purpose, and charge administrative expenses to the proper appropriation of the respective offices/departments of the Municipality.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee follow strictly the rules and regulations set forth in the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184, pertaining to the alternative mode of procurement of Shopping to avoid audit disallowances.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to adopt the appropriate mode of procurement for POL products, and resort only to alternative methods of procurement for highly exceptional cases or if warranted by attending circumstances, and see to it the amount does not exceed the thresholds as prescribed in Annex H of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer only include released checks in the Monthly Report of Check Issued for submission to the Accounting Office; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to record the transaction only upon the receipt of the RCI from the Treasury Office.
observations.managementAction: Management’s action plan was for the Treasury Office to submit to Accounting the Monthly Report of Check Issued for released checks only.
observations.reason:
Unreleased checks as at yearend were still included in the RCI. This is reiterated in the CY 2023 AAR.
The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjusting entries to revert back unreleased checks to Cash in Bank as at yearend.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjustments to recognize the effects of foreign exchange rates pertaining to the procurement of various heavy equipment through LCs and ensure that the affected accounts are fairly presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management’s action plan was for paid the books to be adjusted.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers and the pertinent supporting documents, otherwise the subject transactions will be issued with Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance, whichever is applicable.
observations.managementAction: Management’s action plan was for the disbursement vouchers to be submitted.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant submitted the disbursement vouchers and the pertinent supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a. Bids and Awards Committee to fast track the bidding process; and
b. Engineering Office to rigidly monitor the implementation of the projects for funds to be optimally utilized.
observations.managementAction: Bids and Awards Committee to fast track the bidding process; Engineering Office to rigidly monitor the implementation of the projects for funds to be optimally utilized.
observations.reason: The Bids and Awards Committee fast tracked the bidding process. Implementation of FMR projects cannot be relied upon due to deficiencies noted in CY 2023 Audit, included in the CY 2023 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a. Ensure proper matching of expenses against appropriations and compliance to the pertinent provisions of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160;
b. Transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE to the Trust Fund - Proper account and make the necessary adjustments in the General Fund and Trust Fund books; and
c. Maintain subsidiary ledgers for transfers of agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer, receipts of NDRRMF by transferring agency, DRRMF from other LGUs by LGU and other sources by donor as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Ensure proper matching of expenses against appropriations and compliance to the pertinent provisions of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160; Transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE to the Trust Fund Books; Maintain subsidiary ledgers for transfers of agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer, receipts of NDRRMF by transferring agency, DRRMF from other LGUs by LGU and other sources by donor.
observations.reason: Only the unexpended portion of CY 2023 was transferred to the Trust Fund books. The remaining balance of unexpended LDRRMF from previous years was not yet transferred. This is reiterated in the CY 2023 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a. Submit a satisfactory explanation on resorting payment through LCs without adhering to the provisions set forth in Appendix 16 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184;
b. Submit the pertinent supporting documents of the procurement in compliance with Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445; and
c. Ensure strict compliance to procurement-related rules and circulars to prevent possible invalidity of the procurement of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Submit a satisfactory explanation; Submit the pertinent supporting documents of the procurement; Ensure strict compliance to procurement-related rules and circulars.
observations.reason: The Municipal Government submitted explanations and supporting documents for the procurement; however, audit of procurement-related transactions in CY 2023 disclosed that there were still deficiencies noted as to the Management’s compliance to the pertinent rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a. The inventory custodians in the RHU and LPMH, and GSO to maintain Stock Cards, and for the Inventory Committee to conduct timely physical count of inventories and submit RPCI in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the Manual of NGAS for LGUs on Inventory;
b. The RHU and the LPMH to properly organize and store their inventories to prevent spoilage or deterioration and avoid stock expiry, overstocking or stock-outs; and
c. The Municipal Accountant to record the financial transactions using the appropriate Inventory accounts in accordance with the Chart of Accounts prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: Inventory custodians to maintain Stock Cards and Inventory Committee to conduct timely physical count and submit RPCI; RHU and LPMH to properly organize and store inventories; Municipal Accountant to record transactions using appropriate Inventory accounts.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed for the MDRRMO to submit the required monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Audit Team through the LDRRMC and LDC within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO to submit the required monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Audit Team through the LDRRMC and LDC within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.managementAction: The necessary information such as description and cost segregation of road components for the local road networks have not been provided yet to the Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund by implementing projects in the nature of investment or capital expenditures benefits as envisioned in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of DILG and DBM.
observations.managementAction: There were still non-capitalizable expenditures charged to 20% LDF in CY 2021. This audit observation is reiterated in CY 2021 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, together with other municipal officials, follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 regarding the “one-time cleansing of PPE Account Balances” in order that  derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs will be undertaken.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report.  Please refer to Observation No. 2 of Part II

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record subsequent procurement of supplies and materials as an asset under the appropriate Inventory account upon purchase, and to recognize its related expenses only when supported with the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued from the end-users and Supply Officer with attached pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slips.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report.  Please refer to Observation No. 3 of Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to draw a journal entry voucher (JEV) to reclassify the completed projects still recorded in the CIP account, to its appropriate asset account, as well as reclassify the assets recorded in the Other Land Improvements account, also to its appropriate asset account.  Likewise, require the concerned officers to strictly observe the accounting and reporting requirements for the local road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.  The Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee that will handle the annual physical count of property plant and equipment as well as the preparation of the Inventory Report or the Report on the Physical Count of PPE.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report. Please refer to Observation No. 1 & 2 of Part II.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require:

The MDRRMO and other offices involved in the implementation of PPAs under LDRRMF to have close coordination to achieve completion targets indicated in the AIP;
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report. Please refer to Observation No. 6 of Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary documents on the dormant receivable accounts with evidences that the same could no longer be collected.  Likewise, management should file with COA, the request for write-off  of dormant accounts, duly supported by evidences showing efforts made to collect the accounts, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: Difficulty in gathering the supporting documents required in filing the request for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board prioritize the construction of the MRF to hasten the closure of the open dumpsite and to ensure the protection of public health.
observations.reason: Difficulty in transporting materials due to poor access road.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management file the request for authority to write-off dormant/ accounts totaling P173,105.40 to the COA Audit Team pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Request the PSA thru the Office of the Local Civil Registrar if they can assist the LGU in gathering supporting documents like death certificate of some of the accountable officers.
observations.reason: Difficulty in gathering the supporting documents required in filing the request for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Inventory Committee undertake an annual inventory of all insurable property of the agency and submit the report to GSIS and furnish a copy to the COA Auditor on or before October 31 of ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: Inventory of PPEs to be made and inclusion of insurance premiums in Annual Budget.
observations.reason: Not all insurable PPEs were provided with budget

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Designate a Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Officer who will monitor the lessees’ compliance of the terms and conditions of the contract of lease, polices, rules and regulations promulgated for their observance, and assist in declaring vacant the stalls of violators, if any.
observations.managementAction: Issued EO No. 1 Series of 2012 “Creating a MEEDO (Economic Enterprise and Development Office) To Oversee, Manage and Superintend the Operation of the New Public Market but failed to designate a personnel/officer in charge.
observations.reason: For Proposal to SB

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Record all purchases of supplies and materials in an Inventory Account both in the Accounting Office and in the General Services Office. As such, the General Services Office is responsible in the safe keeping and issuance of these supplies and materials by requiring the end-users to fill up a Requisition and Issue Slip duly approved by the approving official, as well as the preparation and maintenance of a Supplies Ledger Card for each type of supplies.
 
Enjoin all department heads to submit to the General Services Office their usage of the various supplies and materials thru the Requisition and Issue Slip, so that the latter can prepare the required Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued monthly which will become the basis for recording the expenses in the books and direct the General Services Office to properly maintain the supplies cards/stock cards.

Require the Inventory Team to conduct Inventory Supplies and Materials semestrally and to submit the corresponding report using the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories.   Instruct the Accounting Office to regularly reconcile this Inventory Report submitted by the General Services Office as against their own record and to verify discrepancies in the process and to adjust records accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Corrective entry has been made for Supplies and Spare Parts Inventory in the previous years. Only Accountable Forms was carried in inventory.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report. Please refer to Observation No. 3 of Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledger for each special account in the General Fund and prepare the corresponding financial statements to form part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance, as required under Sections 105, 107 and 110, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.reason:
Lack of accounting personnel who will take charge in the recording of financial transactions of the Economic Enterprise.
Only subsidiary ledgers were maintained. The budget of each economic enterprise is still incorporated in the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should require the Municipal Treasurer as well as the Revenue Collection Clerk to strictly adhere the provisions of Sections 69 of PD 1445 and Section 32 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Vol. 1 on the remittance/deposit of collections and to stop the practices of not depositing collections intact daily or the next banking day.
observations.reason: Limited out of town travel due to COVID 19.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
 Exert more effort to establish existence and determine nature of the accounts. For receivables, measures which include sending demand letters to debtors reminding them of their overdue obligations should be made. Only when it is definitely determined said accounts to be uncollectible that a request for authority to write-off the accounts together with all the necessary documents be submitted before the Commission;
 Analyze the accounts and subsequently make adjustments as necessary. Prepare the Fund Utilizations Reports and or coordinate with the source agency for the closing of the accounts; and
 For future transactions, maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers in accordance with Section 4(e) of the NGAS to provide ready and complete information on the accounts and to attain fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the audit recommendations and committed to affect necessary adjustments in 2022 Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Discussed in Part II, Observation No. 5

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 
 Analyze the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, trace back differences and the nature of the accounts; and
 Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to bring the correct balances of the accounts; and
 Maintain subsidiary ledger for reciprocal accounts and include disclosure in the Notes to FS for fair presentation of Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant traced back documents and taken up adjusting entries to bring the correct balances of the accounts this 2022.
observations.reason: Discussed in Part II, Observation No. 6

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
 Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit the aforementioned DVs and liquidation documents totaling ₱357,343.10. Henceforth, see to it that Disbursement Vouchers, Payrolls, Official Receipts and other financial reports be submitted within the prescribed reglementary period for timely examination of accounts and communication/ correction of possible errors; and
 Municipal Accountant ensure the submission of accounts and financial statements within the prescribed periods pursuant to Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160, Section 70 of NGAS for LGUs, Item 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-01 and Paragraph 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006. Monthly accounts should be submitted along with the journals and financial statements and trial balances to facilitate audit and verification.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office and Treasury Office committed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Discussed in Part II, Observation No. 10

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to pay disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00 through checks direct to the payee in compliance with Sections 2.2 and 4.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Management replied that they are doing its best to avoid cash payments exceeding ₱15,000.00.
observations.reason: Discussed in Part II, Observation No. 13

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
 Require the conduct of materials testing for Four infrastructure projects to uncover any possible inferior construction materials used or components supplied by the contractor so as to ensure the quality of projects; and that the results be required before the payment of the contractor’s accomplishments; and
 Ensure that the provision on the conduct of material testing and the submission of the test results as a requirement of final payment be incorporated in the Special Condition of the Contract.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to comply with the audit recommendations moving forward.
observations.reason: Discussed in Part II, Observation No. 16

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant include the required disclosures on DRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements to come up with more informative financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No additional disclosures for CY 2021 were included in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace/ locate and analyze the transactions recorded in the said accounts, and subsequently prepare adjusting entry as necessary. Otherwise, communicate to the different National Government Agencies or Financial Institutions concerned for the settlement/reversion of the said accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to submit working papers and other documents as bases for her adjusting entries this 2022. For the GSIS, the Management explained the system issue and is coordinating with GSIS.
observations.reason: Discussed in Part II, Observation No. 27

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer as withholding agents, to deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases and services as mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid impositions of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer replied that they are doing their best efforts to withhold and remit VAT/NVAT/EWT to all purchases of goods and services of the MLGU-La Paz.
observations.reason: Discussed in Part II, Observation No. 29

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require future contractors to submit performance security with validity period that include the one-year defects liability period pursuant to Section 39 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 and GPPB Resolution No. 012-2005. The validity period shall commence from the effectivity of the contract up to one year after the completion of the project or up to the issuance of the Certificate of Acceptance.
observations.managementAction: Performance Security for the four Infra Projects of CY 2021 covers only one year from the date of Notice to Proceed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC see to it that after verifying the documents which would form part of the contract, the BAC properly affix their signature so as to ensure that no alterations will be made upon the submitted documents.
observations.managementAction: Could not be verified for CY 2021 since contractor’s documents were not included in the contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze the balance of RPT Receivable and prepare necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2021, list of taxpayers was not submitted to support the balance of RPT Receivable.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer or assigned personnel to submit to COA the list of on-going projects/programs and activities for audit purposes, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: List of all on-going projects, programs and activities were not submitted by the MLGU for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the recommendations that the: a) Accounting Office prepare the BRS of the Municipality; and b) Accounting and Treasury Offices prioritize the reconciliation of their records.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the: a) Accounting Office to maintain separate SLs for all cash accounts of the Municipality and reconcile the same accordingly with the cash control account at the end of each month; and b) Municipal Treasurer to update and balance the Cashbooks daily pursuant to Section 63 of the LTOM.

We recommended that the LCE  - 	
	direct the Accounting Office to i) comply with the provision of the MNGAS on the proper recognition of procured supplies so as not to misstate financial reports; and ii) exert efforts in the preparation and maintenance of the required SLCs;

	instruct the GSO to continue the efforts started in preparing the required SCs and regularly coordinate with the Accounting Office on the reconciliation of their records; and

	compel the constituted Inventory Committee members to conduct semestral physical count and prepare and submit RPCI thereafter to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Accounting Office to i) update the accumulated depreciation accounts and record the adjustments in the government equity account for the prior year depreciation expenses of the additional PPEs not previously recorded; and ii) prepare a Lapsing Schedule for Depreciation which serves as a guide in the computation of annual depreciation.
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the current portion of all long-term loans payable to current liabilities pursuant to the above-cited provisions.
We recommended that the LCE instruct the MHRMO and Accounting Office to submit necessary documents as enumerated under paragraph 16.5 above to the Audit Team for its review and evaluation.

We recommended that the LCE i) require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe compliance with the rendition of reports and submission of accounts within the prescribed period for the timely verification and audit of transactions; and ii) equip the Accounting Office with competent staff who can effectively assist the Municipal Accountant in the preparation of required reports.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to keep up to date the accounting records. We further recommended the evaluation of the manpower complement of the Municipality and implement remedies on the claimed situation of the Accounting Office.

We reiterated that the:
	
	LCE direct the MENRO to enjoin the nine barangays to immediately establish their respective MRFs pursuant to R.A. No. 9003 so that residual wastes will be minimized and reduce garbage disposal fee being paid by the Municipality; and

	MSWMB i) review and evaluate the implementation of the SWMP and impose prompt recording and accounting of the MRF operational costs and sales; and ii) institute other revenue-generating measures to promote and ensure the viability of its SWMP.

We further recommended that the BAC and BAC Secretariat furnish immediately the deficient documents to the Audit Team.

1.4 We recommended that Management instruct the GSO to coordinate with
the respective offices as to the conduct and reporting of the physical count
of all its inventories as at year-end and reconcile the report with the
accounting records.

2.7 We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the LPMH to properly dispose the expired and damaged
medicines;
b. Maintain Stock Cards for Drugs and Medicines and Medical
Supplies for proper monitoring of the stock levels;
c. Specify in the Bidding Documents for the Procurement of Drugs and
Medicines/Medical Supplies to deliver stocks with at least 18-months
of shelf life from the date of delivery and inspect the expiration dates
of the delivered medicines/medical supplies before acceptance to
avoid expiration;
d. Coordinate and plan the proper timing of procurement, taking into
consideration the need/demand, turnover ratio and expiration of
drugs and medicines.

3.6 We recommended that Management adheres to the guidelines set forth
in COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of physical count of PPE,
disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, and for the one-time
cleansing of PPE account balances.

4.7 We recommended that the Management cause the immediate
reconciliation of the fund transfers from NGAs and LGUs for
appropriate adjustment in the books and regularly coordinate with the
source agencies to avoid any discrepancies in the future.

5.4 We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the terms and
conditions stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement and fully utilize
any fund/s transferred from national agency within the prescribed
period to attain the desired benefits for the intended beneficiaries.

6.8 We recommended that the Management draw up journal entry vouchers
(JEV) to adjust the accounts affected and enforce timely submission of
liquidation reports to the Source Agency and strictly adhere to COA
Circular 94-013 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2020-089.

7.8 We recommended that the Management:
a. Direct the GFPS to regularly monitor the implementation of GAD
Plan and Budget in coordination with the MPDO and fully utilize the
fund to attain the desired benefits for the intended beneficiaries; and
b. Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant on the utilization of the
fund and conduct thorough check and review to ensure accurate,
consistent, and reliable GAD Accomplishment Report.

8.7 We recommended that the Management ensure the optimum utilization
of the 20% Development Fund by full implementation of programmed
development PPAs during the year and monitor the implementation
thereof to attain the desired benefits as envisioned in Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of DILG and DBM.

9.7 We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal
Development Council to identify priority projects that are investment
and capital expenditure in nature and to adhere to the guidelines set
under DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated
February 22, 2017, in the appropriation and utilization of 20%
Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

10.4 We recommended that the Management:
a. Direct the MPDO to closely coordinate with the Engineering Office
as to the status or percentage of completion of the project/s as well
as the other necessary information to be presented in the
Accomplishment Report; and
b. Conduct thorough check and review to ensure accurate, consistent,
and reliable reporting of the PPAs.

11.4 We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Treasury
Office and Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile the loan records
and for the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjustments
to ensure fair presentation of the account in the Financial Statements.

12.7 We recommended that the Management ensure compliance to
procurement-related rules and circulars as well as provisions indicated
in the contract to prevent the possible invalidity of the procurement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the Inventory Committee to:
Prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit to the Audit Team after the same has been approved by the Municipal Mayor but before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities;
Start immediately, and complete during the year, the physical count of all PPEs of the Municipality, including road networks, in accordance with the procedures and schedule in the PIP as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and other relevant issuance; and
Prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCLRN, and submit the same to the Accountant for reconciliation and to the Audit Team for verification.
We also recommended that the Property Custodian prepare and maintain Property Cards for each class of PPE, and LRNPC for all roads and its components, based on the RPCPPE and RPCLRN, respectively.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Update the PPELC for each class of PPE, and LRNLC for each local road, and reconcile these with the RPCPPE and RPCLRN, respectively; and
Start the cleansing of PPE accounts by following the procedures in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and based on the RPCPPE and RPCLRN.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Provide the Municipal Engineer with the list of projects still recorded in the books as on-going under the Construction in-Progress account; and
Verify, on the basis of the report from the Municipal Engineer, which of the identified projects comprising the P16,217,890.98 balance under the Construction in Progress accounts merit proper closure to the appropriate asset accounts and/or restatement.
We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer conduct an inspection of the projects which are still recorded as on-going under the Construction in-Progress account, to verify its existence/actual condition and prepare a report on the results of such verification to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to:
all Heads of Offices in the LGU instructing that receipt and deliveries of all procured supplies and materials should be centralized and handled only by the General Services Office for monitoring and proper recording of deliveries by the said office; and
the Inventory Committee to undertake a complete physical inventory of all supplies and materials, and submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Audit Team and the Municipal Accountant every January 31 and July 31 to ascertain the accuracy of the Inventory account balance.
We also recommended that, in subsequent procurement, the Supply Officer:
issue a pre-numbered approved Requisition and Issue Slip upon release of any kind of supplies and materials to the end-users; and
prepare the weekly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) duly supported with pre-numbered RIS issued and Distribution Lists prepared by end-user departments, and submit the same thereafter to the Municipal Accountant to reduce his accountability.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the appropriate Inventory account all supplies and materials upon purchase, except those purchased out of the petty cash fund, and record the related expense only when supported with the SSMI accompanied with pre-numbered RISs and Distribution Lists from the end-users for inventories held for distribution.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor sign a Memorandum of Agreement with any government agency when all the necessary information is completely incorporated therein, specifically the duration of the implementation of the program to set the parameters in the program implementation.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
coordinate with the Program Coordinator on the submission to the Audit Team of the needed documents to validate the implementation of the program; and
prepare and submit immediately the FUR to DOLE, copy furnished the Audit Team, upon receipt of the lacking documents.

We recommended that in future transactions, the MDRRMC, through the MDRRMO, incorporate in the annual/revise LDRRMF Investment Plan and the Annual/Supplemental Investment Plan, all programs, projects and activities to be charged to LDRRMF, including subsequent changes thereon, duly supported with proper authority through Resolutions from MDRRMC, MDC and the Sangguniang Bayan.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor authorize, and the Municipal Budget and Municipal Accountant process payments, for the utilization of the LDRRMF only for PPAs which are included in the LDRRMF Investment Plan and integrated in the AIP to ensure that the transactions are duly authorized.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Require the suppliers of the transactions in nos. 2 to 5 in Table 9, to pay the retention money or post a special bank guarantee as warranty security for the goods delivered; and
Require Warranty Security in future procurements of the LGU, before payment is made, to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer and Municipal Accountant submit the following supporting documents to validate the eligibility and entitlement of the claimants, and to support the completeness, validity and authenticity of the payments.
Pre-determined list of the frontline HRHs and HWs, including the BHWs prepared by the Local Health Board and validated by the Department of Health authorized representative;
Duly accomplished time record of recipients of COVID-19 AHDP and SRA; and
Accomplishment Report for the Job Order and BHWs personnel.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the persons concerned to refund the amount received by the JOs totaling ₱27,727.26.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer submit an explanation, justification or any form of certification from the department head concerned that the savings it generated from an item of appropriation is no longer needed because the work, activity or purpose by which it was authorized has been satisfactorily completed, discontinued or abandoned.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor approves realignment of savings of an item of appropriation to augment another item of appropriation ONLY when supported with justification or certification from the official concerned as to the source of such savings.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant obligate and utilize the COVID-19 Pandemic Fund only for its specific purpose, and charge administrative expenses to the proper appropriation of the respective offices/departments of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee follow strictly the rules and regulations set forth in the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184, pertaining to the alternative mode of procurement of Shopping to avoid audit disallowances.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to adopt the appropriate mode of procurement for POL products, and resort only to alternative methods of procurement for highly exceptional cases or if warranted by attending circumstances, and see to it the amount does not exceed the thresholds as prescribed in Annex H of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184.

a. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to complete and update entries in the General Fund cashbook and establish balances from August 2020 to present and exercise due diligence in its fiscal responsibility over the financial affairs, transactions, and operations of the Municipality.
b. It was also recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasury Office conduct regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made or errors detected in either books will be adjusted.
observations.reason: The General Fund Cashbook was turned over to the Municipal Treasurer, still for validation by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct actual count of all its inventories as at year-end and submit the RCPI to the Accounting Office. Further, instruct the GSO to submit the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued together with the distribution list and/or issuance slips to the Accounting Office as basis for drawing the Journal Entry Voucher.
We recommended that the Management conduct regular reconciliation of PPE per books and per count to present an accurate and reliable PPE accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The GSO submitted RCPPE as of December 31, 2021. However, the reliability of the report could not be ascertained due to the incomplete information presented in the Report. This audit observation is reiterated in CY 2021 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Local Sanggunian shall revisit the locally-enacted resolutions if the same are still relevant/reasonable nowadays considering the cheaper, prevailing rates offered by the mobile networks. Otherwise, the enjoined individuals should further decrease the communication allowance to a more conscionable rate. A detailed report must also be submitted to justify the excess, indicating therein the breakdown of expenses covered by the monthly communication allowance given.
In addition, communication allowance should be granted only to those officials and employees whose duties would genuinely require additional communication expenses. The principle of judicious utilization of government funds as emphasized in Section 2 of PD 1445 must be considered at all times.
observations.managementAction: A notice of disallowance was issued in CY 2021 amounting to P197,000.00. Partial settlements are made by the Municipality during the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to require contractors to install project billboards at the project sites containing complete information, and correct design and format in accordance with COA Circular 2013-004 and its Annexes;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to require attachment of photos of the installed project billboards at the project site upon payment of Mobilization Costs or First Progress billing. Information contained in the billboard should be clearly visible in the photos and that it can be determined that it is installed in the project site; and
c. Strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 for the timely verification of the PPAs’ status and validation of projects/programs/activities signboards and/or public notices.

We recommended that:
a. the LFC prepare the required monthly reports in accordance with Item Nos. 4.2 and 4.3 of the DBM-LBC 125 and post the same in at least three (3) conspicuous public places;
b. the LCE send a written notice to the offices as required in Item No. 4.4 of the same circular including the Commission on Audit when said reports have been posted; and
c. the management strictly adhere to posting and reportorial requirements of public funds to uphold transparency, responsibility and accountability.

We recommended that Management submit the required supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor immediately. Henceforth, all disbursement should be supported with complete documentation to establish the validity and regularity of transactions.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the MDRRMO, MSWDO, MMO, Municipal Accountant and other offices concerned to prepare the required summary list together with the required supporting documents and the Reports on Donations in cash and in-kind and submit the same promptly to COA;
b. Instruct the GSO-designate to coordinate with the MDRRMO, MSWDO, MMO and other offices with regard the donated items to be able to maintain an inventory list thereof; and
c. Adhere strictly to COA Circular No. 2014-002 and COA Circular No. 2020-009 on the accounting and reporting guidelines of cash and in-kind donations.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Ensure the optimum utilization of the funds loaned from DBP by full and timely implementation of the PPAs and monitor the implementation thereof in order to attain the desired benefits as envisioned in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of DILG and DBM.
b. Find possible alternative ways and exert maximum efforts for faster implementation of the said projects.
c. Impose liquidated damages and deduct the same from contract billing payments to the contractor.
We enjoin the Management to submit immediately the contracts and justify in writing the delay of its submission as well as the delay of project completion.
observations.managementAction: Some of the projects funded out from loans were already completed during the year while others are not yet completed as at year-end .

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:
 Mobilize the Inventory Committee of the MLGU to:
 Conduct complete physical count and inventory of PPE. See to it
that all columns in the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) shall contain the required
important details on PPE;
 Ensure that that Property Sticker be duly accomplished and
pasted on the prominent but secure portion of the property for
easy identification; and
 For the donated PPE to Day Care Centers and Farmers’
Association, Invoice Receipts of Property or Deed of Donation be
executed to effect the dropping of said PPE from the books of the
MLGU;
 Submit a copy of the RPCPPE to COA for verification not later
than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the
Manual on the NGAS
 Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to keep Property Card
for each PPE item and maintain ARE /PAR for every
equipment/property issued to Municipal officials and employees and
ensure that the same be renewed every after three years pursuant to
Section 492 of GAAM, Volume 1, to establish effective Management
and control over PPE;
 For discrepancy/ies that would be noted between actual physical
count of PPE vis updated GSO and Accounting PPE records,
investigate the cause and prepare corresponding report. Also, instruct
the Municipal Accountant to prepare separate subsidiary ledger for
such difference/s; and
 For the unserviceable/damaged property and equipment, the Property
and Accounting Units comply with the prescribed procedures in COA
Circular No. 89-296 on the disposal of unserviceable PPE. Use the
template – Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property (IIRUP) for items for disposal.

We have recommended that the General Services Officer update the Local
Road Inventory and Road Map and submit to the Audit Team for proper
evaluation of Road Network account.

We have recommended that the –
 Municipal Accountant reconcile the cash in bank ledger balance not
only with the bank but also the cashbook to establish correct balance of
the account;
 Municipal Treasurer foot correctly the monthly balances and see to it
the same previous month’s balance be forwarded on the ensuing
months to reflect accurate balance per cashbook; and
 Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records
at least quarterly to thresh out discrepancies and adjust their records as
necessary, to fairly present balances.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant –
 Analyze the CIP assets accounts, trace back differences and prepare
necessary adjusting entry to correct the balances of the accounts; and
 Maintain subsidiary ledger per CIP asset per project for ready
reference and to support balances in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
 Exert more effort to establish existence and determine nature of the
accounts. For receivables, measures which include sending demand
letters to debtors reminding them of their overdue obligations should be
made. Only when it is definitely determined said accounts to be
uncollectible that a request for authority to write-off the accounts
together with all the necessary documents be submitted before the
Commission;
 Analyze the accounts and subsequently adjust as necessary. Prepare
the Fund Utilizations Reports and or coordinate with the source agency
for the closing of the accounts; and
 For future transactions, maintain complete and updated subsidiary
ledgers in accordance with Section 4(e) of the NGAS to provide ready
and complete information on the accounts and to attain fair
presentation of financial statements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
 Analyze the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts,
trace back differences and the nature of the accounts; and prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to bring the correct balances of the
accounts; and
 Henceforth, in the preparation of consolidated year-end financial
statements, apply elimination of balances for intra-agency receivables
and payables and include disclosures thereof in the Notes to Financial
Statements in consonance with IPSAS 6 and IPSAS 1, for fair
presentation of receivables and payables in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the:
 Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile entries in the
Cashbook and General Ledger to bring the correct balance of Cash
Local Treasury account or undeposited collections and demand the
Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact the full amount to the AGDB;
 Municipal Treasurer properly accomplish RCDs and forward copies of
these along with validated deposit slips to the Accounting unit as their
bases in recording collections and deposits; and
 Ensure that at least on a quarterly basis, the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant reconcile their records in compliance with Item
C, Section 181 of GAAM Volume I and Section 59 of Book 2, Chapter 3
of Updated LTOM, to establish accurate balance of Cash Local
Treasury account.

We have recommended that the:
 Municipal Budget Officer properly accomplish the Obligation Requests
for all expenditures. Ensure that the Registry of Appropriations,
Allotment and Obligations be maintained to properly monitor the
budget of the agency and serve as the basis to prepare the Statement of
Appropriations, Allotment, Obligations and Balances; and
 Municipal Accountant do not attend claims without the required
certification as to the availability of appropriation including the specific
cost centers or function/program/project where expenses will be
charged.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit
to the auditor the Disbursement Vouchers and Liquidation Documents of
Cash Advances for verification, to avoid possible suspension and or
disallowance in audit.

We have recommended that the –
 Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer see to it that
Disbursement Vouchers, Payrolls, Official Receipts, and Other
Financial Reports be submitted within the prescribed reglementary
period for timely examination of accounts and communication/
correction of possible errors; and
 Municipal Accountant ensure the submission of accounts and
financial statements within the prescribed periods pursuant to Section
347 of Republic Act No. 7160, Section 70 of NGAS for LGUs, Item 4
of COA Circular No. 2010-01 and Paragraph 7 of COA Circular No.
2009-006. Monthly accounts should be submitted along with the
journals and financial statements and trial balances to facilitate
systematic audit and verification.

We have recommended that Management submit the following verifiable
supporting documents to enable proper evaluation of the COVID-19
response efforts of the Municipality:
 Acknowledgement Receipts showing the address and signature of the
beneficiary /recipient as well as the description, amount and quantity of
goods being received; and
 Photograph documentations of the COVID-19 response aid and operation
of the Municipality relative to the above expenditures, properly captioned
and dated.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant –
 Attach and submit Certified copies of Contract of Services/Job Order
contracts from the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO), along
with the statement of expected work or output; and their verified
Accomplishment Reports. For the newly hired employee, copy of
appointment, oath of office and assumption notice; and
 Henceforth, for payment of salaries and wages, evaluate and ensure that
payrolls or disbursement vouchers are supported with complete
documents before payment is effected to establish the validity of the
transactions as well as the correctness of the accounts used.

We have reiterated our recommendation that Management instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to pay disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00 through
checks direct to the payee in compliance with Sections 2.2 and 4.3.2 of COA
Circular No. 97-002.

We have recommended that the BAC Chairperson –
 Submit to the audit team the documentary evidence enumerated under
Item 14.4, to support the validity of the award of the contracts and
eventually the payments thereof; and
 Always ensure that all contracts be supported with complete
documentations required under RA No. 9184 and its IRR and other
relevant guidelines on procurement, to establish propriety of awarded
contracts.

We have recommended that the BAC Chairperson ensure the submission
of government contracts within the reglementary period sets forth in Item
3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 for a systematic and effective review
process to come up with timely and relevant audit results.

We have recommended that Management –
 Require the conduct of materials testing for infrastructure projects to
uncover any possible inferior construction materials used or
components supplied by the contractor to ensure the quality of projects;
and that the results be required before the payment of the contractor’s
accomplishments; and
 Ensure that the provision on the conduct of material testing and the
submission of the test results as a requirement of final payment be
incorporated in the Special Condition of the Contract.

We have recommended that in case payment/release of retention money be
substituted by a Surety Bond, Management see to it the same be attached
with the Specific Certification issued by the Insurance Commission in
compliance with GPPB Non-Policy Matter Opinions No. 017-2012.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination
with the MSWDO, submit the following documents to enable proper
evaluation of the AICS implementation:
a) Memorandum of Agreement covering AICS; and
b) Social Case Study Report along with the documentary requirements
for the assistance given, for the 4,839 served clients.
Henceforth, see to it that guidelines on fund transfers received from
national government agencies be strictly adhered to ensure attainment of
its objectives and to facilitate effective audit.

We have recommended that Management always see to it that Municipal
/Local School Board Budget be accordingly followed in accordance with
Section 4.1 of PD 1445, to effectively and efficiently utilize the fund.

We have recommended that Management:
 Submit the necessary issuance for the amendments made in the original
20% Development Fund Investment Plan, aside from the above-
mentioned Appropriation Ordinance;
 Require the Municipal Accountant and requesting offices to submit the
lacking documents and or duly accomplish the attached supporting
papers as discussed in the foregoing observations to establish the
propriety and validity of the transactions;
 For the ensuing years, see to that 20% DF Utilization Report be
supported with detailed schedule of charges to facilitate effective
evaluation; and

We have recommended that Management:
 Require the Municipal Accountant and requesting offices to submit the
lacking documents and or duly accomplish the attached supporting
papers as discussed in the foregoing observations to establish the
propriety and validity of the transactions; and
 In the future, see to it that charges under the LDRRMF be properly
documented to support propriety of the transaction and to facilitate
effective audit. Also, amendments in the Plan, if any, be submitted to
the audit team or attached to the DV for ready reference.

We have recommended that the Chairperson, GAD Focal Point System:
 Ensure that GAD AR for CY 2020 together with the following
accompanying reports be submitted to the assigned Audit Team
within five (5) working days from the end of the January of the
preceding year pursuant to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001
dated March 18, 2014 and Section 4.5.C.8 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No.
2016-01:
a) brief summary of the reported program or project;
b) copies of reported policy issuances; and
c) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and
recommendations.
 In the future, see to it that resolution on the amendments to the GAD
Plan and Budget, if any, be attached to the DVs as ready reference to
aid effective examination.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer always ensure
remittance of Pag-IBIG premiums and loan amortizations in accordance
with Item 4 of Part E of HMDF Circular No. 275 to enable municipal
employees to conveniently avail of the services and benefits of the
institution.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer:
 Look into the over/under remittance to determine proper treatment. If
found under deduction from employees or under provision of
government share, cause the collection from the concerned municipal
officials and employees and set up additional provision for government
share; and
 Always ensure remittance of PhilHealth premiums pursuant to
schedule provided under PhilHealth Circular No. 0001 s. 2014, to
enable municipal officials and employees to conveniently avail of the
services of the institutions.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer:
 Cause the remittance of ₱12,984.50 to GSIS to avoid possible
imposition of penalties and surcharges by GSIS; and
 Always ensure remittance of GSIS premiums and loans amortizations
pursuant to pertinent provision of Section 14 of the IRR of RA No.
8291, to enable municipal employees to conveniently avail of the
services and benefits of the institution.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace/ locate and
analyze the transactions recorded in the said accounts, and subsequently
prepare adjusting entry as necessary. Otherwise, communicate to the
different National Government Agencies or Financial Institutions
concerned for the settlement/reversion of the said accounts.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant always ensure that
General Journals (GJs) be attached with Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs)
as well as its supporting documents to facilitate effective analysis of
accounts.

We have reiterated our recommendation that Management-
 Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deduct, withhold and remit the
corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases and contracts as
mandated by laws, rules, and regulations to avoid impositions of
sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue; and
 Prevent paying/disbursing through cash or reimbursement in the
procurement of goods and services. Instruct the Municipal to pay
disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00 through checks direct to the
payee in compliance with Sections 2.2 and 4.3.2 of COA Circular No.
97-002, ad deduct creditable withholding taxes, as appropriate.

We have recommended that the –
 Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer cause the
immediate remittance to BIR the above due withholding taxes.
Possible penalty or surcharges thereto shall not be charged from LGU
fund, such shall be personal liability of the withholding agents, as set
forth in Revenue Regulations 2-98;
 Municipal Accountant record the withholdings and remittances
carefully to avoid misrepresentations in the financial statements, and
attach the journal entry voucher together with the necessary
supporting documents to verify the entries in the general journal; and
 Municipal Treasurer always deduct, withhold and subsequently remit
the corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases and contracts
as mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid impositions of
sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the (a) Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant determine the actual Welfare Goods and Medical, Dental and Laboratory supplies consumed, make the necessary adjusting entries and properly account for the unutilized welfare goods and medical supplies if any and prepare the SAR; (b) Municipal Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) (i) make sure that the PO is signed by the supplier or that the parties entered into a valid contract; and (ii) submit the required documents such as SPA, Secretary’s Certificate or Board/Partnership Resolution, as the case may be, evidencing authority of the supplier’s purported representative to act on behalf of his/her principal and ensure compliance of the same in future procurement contracts; (c) Municipal Budget Officer make sure that funds are utilized according to the purpose for which they are appropriated; and (d) BAC and concerned officials of the agency comply with the requirements under the 2016 Revised IRRs of R.A. No. 9184 and submit the lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team to prove the propriety and regularity of disbursement transactions noted.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant immediately make the corresponding adjusting entries as proposed above for the year 2021.

We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, the Municipal Budget Officer and others officials involved in the planning and budget process:

review the unutilized reprogrammed PPA to address COVID-19 concerns and determine whether the purpose is still valid; and

follow strictly the procedures laid down in the 2016 edition of the BOM and other related DBM guidelines with regards to the making of a supplemental budget through budgetary realignment.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, together with the members of the Municipal Planning Development Council:

Update the Annual Investment Plan through a revisit of the unimplemented development projects and review its viability and necessity, taking into consideration and strictly observing the guidelines set in DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020, as well as other budgeting guidelines; and

Include in the Annual Investment Plan only those projects which can be implemented during the year in order to maximize the utilization of the 20%DF.

We recommend that the Special Disbursing Officer liquidate within one month from receipt of this AOM the cash advance of P1,000,000.00, and refund the remaining amount to the Municipal Treasurer.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive requiring the concerned accountable officer to submit her Liquidation Report on the outstanding cash advance of P1,000.000.00, otherwise, impose the necessary sanction provided in the regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and other members of the Municipal Development Council:

comply with all the provisions of every DBM guidelines relative to the  utilization of grants or aid from the National Government; and

identify specific activities when preparing the Annual Investment Plan, after  considering the needs of the end-users and the constituents.

We recommend that the BAC Chairman, through its Secretariat, post in the PhilGEPS and GPPB website the Purchase Order, Contracts and other pertinent documents on the procurement activities of the Municipality of La Paz.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor secure a fast and upgraded internet connection to assure the BAC’s compliance with the on-line posting requirements in Republic Act No. 9184 and other relevant issuances.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board review its SWMP, and ensure that what has been programmed or planned is feasible to be implemented, as well as prioritize and provide adequate funding for the construction of the Sanitary Landfill to hasten the closure of the open dumpsite and to ensure the protection of public health.
observations.reason: MRF was already constructed but is not yet operational pending reissuance of permit from EMB to discharge leachate from the pit to the treatment pond.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation, as well as assist the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) in the generation and processing of sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information to complement the GAD database.  We also recommend that the GFPS create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team to assess whether the GAD programs implemented addresses the needs of the constituents.

We recommend that:

Management pay the salaries, wages, and other monetary benefits of the agency’s elective officials, regular, casual, contractual and job order personnel through Automated Teller Machine (ATM) instead of cash.


Management coordinate with nearby agencies and with the AGDB for the installation of ATMs nearby. | Require  the remaining LGU employees to enroll in the LBP payroll account system.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: 95% of the LGU employees were already enrolled in the system. However, stricter policy at LBP due to Covid-19 on allowing visitors have discouraged some LGU employees to enroll.

Request in writing the bank for a copy of documents pertaining to closed accounts and bank debit and credit memo.


Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the books based on the supporting documents secured by management.

Once these documents are available, instruct the GSO to immediately register these motor vehicles with the LTO.

Instruct the GSO to immediately renew the registration of the Multi Cab with plate number SKY 389.  Henceforth, oblige the registration of motor vehicles promptly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
 The Municipal Accountant reconcile the cash in bank ledger balance not only with the bank but also with cashbook pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011, to bring into agreement and establish correct treasury and ledger and bank balances. A copy thereof, along with the photocopy of cashbook, bank statements and journal entry voucher for the necessary adjusting entries, be submitted to COA for verification.
 The Municipal Treasurer foot correctly the monthly balances and see to it the same previous month’s balance be forwarded on the ensuing months to reflect accurate balance per cashbook; and
 The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant:
   Submit the cashbooks and the remaining bank statements in order to verify the Bank Reconciliation Statement and Cash in Bank balances of the MLGU; and
   Reconcile their records at least quarterly to thresh out discrepancies and subsequently prepare and adjust their records as necessary, to fairly present balances.
observations.managementAction: For the balance of ₱43,687,976.59 as of year-end of 2020, this has been reconciled by the Accounting Office. Cashbooks were brought to the office on May 18, 2022, however, retrieved on June 17, 2022. Only January to June 2021 photocopy of bank statement were submitted.
observations.reason: Discussed in Part II, Observation No. 3

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
 Municipal Treasurer
   Update and submit certified photocopy copy of collections cashbooks for audit; 
   Properly accomplish RCDs and forward copies of these along with validated deposit slips to the Accounting unit as their bases in recording collections and deposits; and
 Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant:
   Reconcile entries in the Cashbook and General Ledger to bring the correct balance of Cash Local Treasury account, establish undeposited collections and subsequently demand the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact the full amount to the AGDB; and
   Reconcile their records at least on a quarterly basis in compliance with Item C, Section 181 of GAAM Volume I, to establish accurate balance of Cash Local Treasury account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed that they have traced back documents and taken up adjusting entries to bring the correct balances of the accounts this 2022.

Ensure that the Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations be maintained to properly monitor the budget of the agency and serve as the basis to prepare the Statement of Appropriations, Allotment, Obligations and Balances; and
 Municipal Accountant do not process claims without the required certification as to the availability of appropriation including the specific cost centers or function/program/project where expenses will be charged.

Accounting Office and the GSO/Municipal Treasurer, as the case may be, to maintain the PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for all LGU properties. Also, observe the periodic reconciliation of the two records for proper monitoring of agency assets.
