Accounting Office to i) prepare and maintain SLs and registries for the special account pursuant to Section 161, Volume I of the LGAM; ii) ensure that disbursement of funds are in accordance with existing laws and regulations such as the R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001; and iii) require the concerned personnel to submit the documents for the financial transactions on the utilization of funds given by the PGT and transmit therewith to the Audit Team;

Local Finance Committee (LFC) to i) prepare its Report on Fund Utilization for the BGCM and reconcile it with the report prepared by the Accounting Office; and ii) fully conform with the requirements set forth in LBC No. 125; and 
BAC to i) initiate and strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 on the appropriate  manner of procuring goods needed by the LGU; and ii) faithfully adhere to GPPB Circulars and other relevant laws, rules and regulations specifically in the emergency procurement of goods.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned personnel to i) be informed of the reportorial and monitoring requirement as mentioned in COA Circular No. 2020-009, in relation to COA Circular No. 2014-002; ii) prepare and submit said required reports to the Accounting Office for recording in the books and for disclosure in the Notes to the FS, and to the Audit Team for further verification; iii) strategize the receipt and distribution of donations-in-kind identifying the personnel who will be responsible in the preparation of acknowledgement receipts and in the distribution thereof; and iv) perform inventory taking of remaining undistributed/ unconsummated donated goods for them to be accounted, valued and recorded in the registries and to be reported in the FS.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the a) BAC, BAC Secretariat and Accounting Office to i) submit the lacking documents and/or explanation for the non-submission of said lacking documents to the Audit Team to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension on the transactions; and ii) observe compliance with pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the procurement through public bidding; and b) Municipal Accountant to observe timeliness in the submission of DVs to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC and its Secretariat and the Accounting Office to comply with the provisions of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.  Moreover, we recommended that said Offices immediately submit the lacking documents and or explanations/justifications on the issues indicated in paragraphs 11.4 and 11.5.

We recommended that the LCE i) direct the BAC and its Secretariat to submit the remaining deficient documents in accordance with above-cited provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its revised IRR; and ii) instruct the Accounting Office to submit the perfected contract together with the supporting documents related to the CY 2020 LGSF-AM project.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Human Resources Management Office (MHRMO), Treasury Office and the Accounting Office to submit the required supporting documents which the Audit Team will evaluate in order to make a decision on the audit of the CNA incentive.

We recommended that the LCE a) direct the MSWDO i) to review the profile of the 241 individuals whose names appeared as beneficiaries of two financial assistance programs and assist in effecting refund should it be found out that double assistance was indeed extended to them; and ii) reconcile the number of beneficiaries appearing in the MSWDO and DSWD records; b) instruct MSWDO to coordinate with the Audit Team for the purpose of clearing out the two negative replies received from respondents who denied receiving ESP-SAP assistance; and c)  instruct the Accountant to properly classify received funds from other agencies.

We recommended that concerned Officials i) be cautious in preparing payrolls to avoid double payment of benefits; and ii) exert extra effort in locating available funds and/or sacrifice other trivial expenses to provide the authorized benefits to frontliners in the future.

We reiterated that the Municipal LSB be apprised of the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and ensure that the preparation and approval of the SEF budget shall fall within the indicative timelines set thereon for the proper implementation of PPAs. We further recommended that the Municipal LSB submit quarterly and annual utilization reports to the appropriate offices.

We recommended that the Management i) evaluate the manpower complement and warrant sufficient staffing for the Treasury Office, accordingly; ii) orient all AOs of their duties, responsibilities, and accountabilities upon issuance of memorandum orders; iii) ensure that memorandum orders to AOs include their specific duties, responsibilities, and accountabilities; and iv) strictly comply with existing regulations requiring all AOs/employees to be bonded.

We recommended that the:

Municipal Budget Officer: i) thoroughly ascertain the availability of appropriations before the approval of obligations; and ii) ensure strict monitoring of obligations by properly preparing the Registries of RAAO in accordance with the MNGAS;

BAC and its Secretariat: i) submit explanation/justification for the non-use of Standard PBD during the procurement process; and ii) submit immediately the deficient procurement documents pursuant to R.A. 9184 and its Revised IRR. 

Accounting Office: i) meticulously check the financial transactions and ensure that all required supporting documents are attached and necessary data/information are complete before payment; ii) immediately furnish the deficient supporting documents pertaining to the transactions noted above pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001; iii) make the necessary corrections/adjustments in the expense and PPE accounts affected; and iv) all other personnel concerned to require certification from the DepEd Accountant that all expenses/disbursements to be charged to the SEF are net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget for the DepEd and the Early Childhood Care and Development (ECCD) Council.

We recommended that the Accounting Office i) submit immediately the DVs with their supporting documents pertaining to the noted transactions; and ii) strictly comply with the submission of DVs and supporting documents within the reglementary period as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006.
We recommended that the LCE instruct the Accounting Office to prioritize and expedite the preparation and submission of year-end financial reports.

We reiterated the audit recommendation that the LCE instruct the GAD Focal Point to observe the timely submission of the GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001. We further recommended that the GAD Focal Point continue its endeavor in the proper monitoring and effective implementation of its GAD PPAs.
We recommended and Management agreed that all taxes withheld be fully remitted intact within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed to continuously remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed that HDMF monthly premiums deducted be fully remitted within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed to continuously remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Accounting Office to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements of the Municipality. Also, that the Accounting and Treasury Office reconcile their records at least quarterly to zero out variances on cash records. Further, we recommend that the Treasury Office consider the opening a new bank account for Time Deposit to separate the two accounts for General and Trust Fund through Sangguniang Bayan Resolution.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the: 

Accounting Office (i) immediately stop the erroneous practice of recognizing outright expense for inventory items procured by the agency and use the appropriate inventory account in recording said stocks, subject to adjustment at year-end should physical stock take showed inventories on hand; and (ii) prepare and maintain the required SLCs despite lack of personnel; and

GSO continue the efforts started in preparing the required SCs and regularly coordinate with the Accounting Office for purposes of reconciling of records; and

Local Chief Executive constitute the Inventory Committee who shall be responsible for the conduct of semestral stock take and submission of RPCI to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2020 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis for the latter in setting up the RPT Receivables for the year.

We recommended that the Accounting Office to:

Make the necessary adjusting entries for the prepayments amounting to ₱1,001,309.92, with the corresponding adjustment to Insurance Expense/ Accumulated Surplus/(Deficit) account; and

Observe the proper accounting treatment for prepayments by making adjustments at the end of accounting period to recognize the expired portion of every prepayment.

We recommended and Management agreed that the concerned Municipal officials should dutifully and promptly remit all barangay shares on RPT and CTC in the succeeding quarters within the prescribed period as provided under Section 271 (d) of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Financial Statements for the four economic enterprises of the Municipality so as to provide with the adequate information on the financial position and performance of these enterprises at any given point for decision making purposes.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:

Municipal Accountant exercise diligent monitoring on the submission of barangay financial transactions and accordingly carry out the procedures enumerated by COA Circular 2019-001 in case of non-submission of the BTs; and

Respective Barangay Treasurers observe the reglementary period on the submission of Barangay Financial Transactions and submit a duly receipted copy of the transmittal letter, together with copies of Punong Barangay Certification, to the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive justify, in writing, the hiring of private lawyer as legal consultant despite the availability of the Municipal Legal Officer and present the written acquiescence and concurrence of the OSG and COA prior to the employment of said legal consultant. Otherwise, we recommend to terminate the December 2019 contract with the private lawyer and refund any disbursement made in relation thereto to avoid receipt of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance for unauthorized transaction.

Instruct the concerned officials to ensure maintenance of documents, insertion of the required provision of the approved contract and installation of DILG Community Billboard containing all the DILG implemented projects based on guidelines set forth by the DILG Memorandum Circular 2019-71.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GAD Focal Point to observe the timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines for the local road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly adhered to, thus Management should require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the local roads and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System. observations.recommendation:
1.7 We have recommended that the:
1.7.1 General Services Officer update the Local Road Inventory and
Road Map for CY 2020, and submit it to the Audit Team for proper
evaluation of Road Network account; and
1.7.2 Municipal Accountant submit the updated subsidiary ledger/records
for the roads and its components, for examination.

2.10.1 Management see to it that a copy of the RPCPPE shall be submitted
to COA for verification not later than January 31, of each year
pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS;
2.10.2 General Services Officer maintain PPE Cards and ARE/PAR and
ensure that the ARE/PAR be renewed every after three years
pursuant to Section 492 of GAAM, Volume 1, to establish effective
management and control over PPE;
2.10.3 General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their
property records with the result of physical count to ensure that
PPE recorded in the property records really exists and that all
existing PPE are recorded in the books, in order to establish
correctness and completeness of balances of PPE accounts. Any
variance shall be dealt accordingly and subsequently adjusted as
necessary.
2.10.4 The Property and Accounting Units comply with the prescribed
procedures in COA Circular No. 89-296 on the disposal of
unserviceable PPE, if any.

3.9.1 The Municipal Accountant reconcile the cash in bank ledger balance
not only with the bank but also with cashbook pursuant to COA
Circular No. 96-011, to bring into agreement and establish correct
treasury and ledger and bank balances. A copy thereof, along with
the photocopy of cashbook, bank statements and journal entry
voucher for the necessary adjusting entries, be submitted to COA
for verification.
3.9.2 The Municipal Treasurer foot correctly the monthly balances and
see to it the same previous month’s balance be forwarded on the
ensuing months to reflect accurate balance per cashbook; and
3.9.3 The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant:

4.10 We have recommended that the:
4.10.1 Municipal Treasurer:


Update and submit certified photocopy copy of collections
cashbooks for audit;
Properly accomplish RCDs and forward copies of these along
with validated deposit slips to the Accounting unit as their
bases in recording collections and deposits; and
4.10.2 Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant:


Reconcile entries in the Cashbook and General Ledger to bring
the correct balance of Cash Local Treasury account, establish
undeposited collections and subsequently demand the
Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact the full amount to the
AGDB; and
Reconcile their records at least on a quarterly basis in
compliance with Item C, Section 181 of GAAM Volume I, to
establish accurate balance of the Cash Local Treasury
account.

5.14 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
5.14.1 Exert more effort to establish existence and determine nature of the
accounts. For receivables, measures which include sending demand
letters to debtors reminding them of their overdue obligations
should be made. Only when it is definitely determined said accounts
to be uncollectible that a request for authority to write-off the
accounts together with all the necessary documents be submitted
before the Commission;
5.14.2 Analyze the accounts and subsequently make adjustments as
necessary. Prepare the Fund Utilizations Reports and or coordinate
with the source agency for the closing of the accounts; and
5.14.3 For future transactions, maintain complete and updated subsidiary
ledgers in accordance with Section 4(e) of the NGAS to provide
ready and complete information on the accounts and to attain fair
presentation of financial statements.

6.10.1 Analyze the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds
accounts, trace back differences and the nature of the accounts;
6.10.2 Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to bring the correct balances
of the accounts; and
6.10.3 Maintain subsidiary ledger for reciprocal accounts and include
disclosure in the Notes to FS for fair presentation of Financial
Statements.

7.3.1 Municipal Budget Officer comply with the requirements of properly
accomplishing the Obligation Requests for all expenditures. Ensure
that the Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations be
maintained to properly monitor the budget of the agency and serve
as the basis to prepare the Statement of Appropriations, Allotment,
Obligations and Balances; and
7.3.2 Municipal Accountant do not process claims without the required
certification as to the availability of appropriation including the
specific cost centers or function/program/project where expenses
will be charged.

8.9.1 Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit the
aforementioned DVs and liquidation documents totaling
₱357,343.10. Henceforth, see to it that Disbursement Vouchers,
Payrolls, Official receipts and other financial reports be submitted
within the prescribed reglementary period for timely examination
of accounts and communication/ correction of possible errors; and
8.9.2 Municipal Accountant ensure the submission of accounts and
financial statements within the prescribed periods pursuant to
Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160, Section 70 of NGAS for
LGUs, Item 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-01 and Paragraph 7 of
COA Circular No. 2009-006. Monthly accounts should be
submitted along with the journals and financial statements and
trial balances to facilitate audit and verification.

9.6.1 Cause the immediate submission of JEVs and its pertinent
supporting documents to support the journal entries recorded in the
General Journal; and
9.6.2 Always ensure that the General Journals be accompanied by JEVs
to establish the validity and the correctness of accounting entries
made.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to pay disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00 through
checks direct to the payee in compliance with Sections 2.2 and 4.3.2 of
COA Circular No. 97-002.

11.5 We have recommended that Management:
11.5.1 Require the foregoing collecting officers to apply for Fidelity Bond
pursuant to Section 305(f) of RA 7160 and pertinent provisions of
Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 to protect the interest of the
Municipality; and
11.5.2 Always ensure that their Fidelity Bond shall be renewed
accordingly, pursuant to Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.

12.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant attach and submit
the following documents to support propriety of payments of salaries and
wages:
12.5.1 For permanent employees:

d) Duly approved DTRs, duly approved application for leave,
locator slip; appointment and assumption notice for the newly
hired employees;
12.5.2 For Job order employees:

e) Verified Accomplishment Reports;
f) Approved DTRs that corresponds with the claims for dates of
services rendered, and affix the signatures of personnel; and
12.5.3 Henceforth, evaluate and ensure that payment of salaries and
wages be supported with complete documents before payment is
effected to establish the validity of the transactions.

13.6 We have recommended that Management:
13.6.1 Require the conduct of materials testing for infrastructure
projects to uncover any possible inferior construction materials
used or components supplied by the contractor so as to ensure the
quality of projects; and that the results be required before the
payment of the contractor’s accomplishments; and
13.6.2 Ensure that the provision on the conduct of material testing and
the submission of the test results as a requirement of final
payment be incorporated in the Special Condition of the Contract.

14.8.1 Require the Municipal Accountant and requesting offices to
submit the proof of payment of the foregoing disbursements to
establish the propriety and validity of the transactions; and
14.8.2 Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to see that the Official Receipts
or Sales Invoices are duly accomplished, date of receipt or
payment/check indicated and duly signed the supplier before
releasing the check to the latter, to establish validity and
integrity of payments.

15.8 We have recommended that Management submit the following
verifiable supporting documents to enable proper evaluation and
appreciation of the COVID-19 response efforts of the Municipality:
 Lists of recipients, showing date of receipt, quantity and goods
received, corresponding barangay and signature, for the
distribution of relief goods worth ₱14,010,779.67;
 Lacking information per recipient of financial assistance, as
tabulated under Item 15.6.6;
 For medicines and vitamins and medical supplies, verifiable
procurement documents and distribution lists and or Report of
Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) supported with Requisition
Issue Slip (RIS), as appropriate; and for Hospital Beds, the covering
ARE; and
 Photograph documentations of the COVID-19 response aid and
operations of the Municipality relative to the above expenditures,
properly captioned and dated.

16.12 We have recommended that the BAC Chairperson:
16.12.9 Submit to the audit team the documentary evidences
enumerated under Items 16.4 and 16.5, to support the validity
of the award of the contracts and eventually the payments
thereof; and
16.12.10 Always ensure that all contracts be supported with complete
documentations required under RA No. 9184 and its IRR and
other relevant guidelines on procurement.

17.3 We have recommended that the BAC Chairperson ensure the
submission of government contracts within the reglementary period sets
forth in Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 for a systematic and
effective review process to come up with timely and relevant audit
results.

18.12 We have recommended that Management:
18.12.1 Instruct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the
MSWDO to submit MOA along with the project proposal
covering ESP-SAP and a copy of Report of Disbursements
(ROD) duly certified by the LGU accountant and approved by
the LCE, duly received by the DSWD-FO, for examination; and
18.12.2 Henceforth, all transactions involving fund transfer from
national agencies be fully documented in accordance with the
covering issuance.

19.12.1 Instruct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the
PESO Manager and Municipal Treasurer to submit MOA
covering the TUPAD program and duly accomplished list of
workers showing the amount paid; and
19.12.2 Henceforth, all transactions involving fund transfer from
national agencies be fully documented in accordance with the
covering issuance.

We have commended the Management for substantially complying with
Item 4 of the LBC 125 on the required reporting and posting on the
utilization of its BGCM Fund. Nonetheless, it was recommended that,
henceforth, instructions for such purposes be carefully observed for
better appreciation and promotion of transparency policy of the
government. The submission of distribution documents was likewise
reiterated.

21.8 We have recommended that Management submit the following
documents to enable proper examination of disbursements under SEF:
 Approved Municipal School Board Budget for CY 2020 along with
the supporting programs, projects and activities;
 Report on SEF Utilization for CY 2020 in compliance with the
provisions of Item 6.0 of DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.
1, s. 2017; and
 Deed of Donation or its equivalent, for the office equipment, to
determine recipient thereof and permit confirmation.

We have recommended that Management:
 Provide supporting schedules detailing disbursement vouchers
covered for the Utilization of 20% Development Fund, to enable
proper evaluation thereof; and
 Explain the variance noted amounting to ₱4,136,685.38.

23.9 We have recommended that Municipal Accountant and MDRRM
Officer:
23.9.1 Provide supporting schedules detailing disbursement vouchers
covered for the Utilization of 5% LDRRMF, to enable proper
evaluation thereof; and
23.9.2 Explain the variance noted amounting to ₱506,023.11.

24.6 We have recommended that Management cause the immediate
submission of SB Resolution covering the disbursement vouchers under
Special Trust Account LDRRMF for examination.

25.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant include the
required disclosures on DRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements to
come up with more informative financial statements.

26.10 We have recommended that the Chairperson, GAD Focal Point System
always see to it that ensuing GAD ARs be accompanied by hereunder
reports to enable judicious evaluation of GAD Fund utilization. And
that these shall be submitted to the Audit Team within five (5) working
days from the end of the January of the preceding year pursuant to
Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 and
Section 4.5.C.8 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as
amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01:
 brief summary of the reported program or project;
 copies of reported policy issuances; and
 actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and
recommendations.

27.6 We have recommended that Management establish and/or strengthen
their monitoring and evaluation systems to assess the effectiveness and
efficiency of their gender mainstreaming efforts, and the M&E
activities shall be included in the annual GPB.

28.22.1 Provide explanation for the over/under remittance and delays
thereof;
28.22.2 Submit justification for the interest of ₱578.73 paid for
PhilHealth July 2019 premiums; and
28.22.3 See to it that all premiums and loans amortizations be fully
remitted within the prescribed period set forth by those
institutions, to avoid possible imposition of surcharges and
penalties and inconvenience of employees in availing services
thereto.

29.10 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace/ locate and
analyze the transactions recorded in the said accounts, and
subsequently prepare adjusting entry as necessary. Otherwise,
communicate to the different National Government Agencies or
Financial Institutions concerned for the settlement/reversion of the said
accounts.

30.6.1 Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer as
withholding agents, to deduct, withhold and remit the
corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases and
services as mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid
impositions of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue;
and
30.6.2 Prevent paying/disbursing through cash or reimbursement in
the procurement of goods and services. We reiterate our
recommendation to the Management to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to pay disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00 through
checks direct to the payee in compliance with Sections 2.2 and
4.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

31.9.1 Municipal Accountant:
 Record the withholdings and remittances carefully to avoid
misrepresentations in the financial statements, and attach
the journal entry voucher together with the necessary
supporting documents to verify the entries in the general
journal; and
 Analyze the Due to BIR balances under 20% DF and RA
7171, and draw necessary adjusting entry to bring the
correct balances.
31.9.2 Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer as
withholding agents:
 Remit to BIR the withholding tax balances under SEF and
Trust Fund. Possible surcharges and or penalties should
not be charged from the coffers of MLGU La Paz, pursuant
to Section 4(11) (4E1) of Revenue Regulations 2-98; and
 We have reiterated, always deduct, withhold and remit the
corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases and
services as mandated by laws, rules and regulations to
avoid impositions of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal
Revenue.

33.3 We have recommended that Management enforce settlements of the
above suspensions and disallowances.

We reiterated the recommendations that the: a) Accounting Office prepare the BRS of the Municipality; and b) Accounting and Treasury Offices prioritize the reconciliation of their records.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the: a) Accounting Office to maintain separate SLs for all cash accounts of the Municipality and reconcile the same accordingly with the cash control account at the end of each month; and b) Municipal Treasurer to update and balance the Cashbooks daily pursuant to Section 63 of the LTOM.
Management’s comment
The LCE informed that he already called the attention of concerned offices to adhere to the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the a) Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a certified list of taxpayers indicating the amount due and collectible for the year; and b) Municipal Accountant, i) upon receipt of the list, set up immediately the RPT/SET Receivable by preparing the corresponding JEV; and ii) prepare and maintain GLs and SLs for all the accounts of the Municipality.
Management’s Comment
The Municipal Treasurer explained that list of delinquency is being prepared by her Office but was not used in the setting up of receivables because the total amount of the said list is too high.
Auditor’s Rejoinder
Section 20 of the MNGAS provides that RPT/SET shall be established based on Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card.

We recommended that the LCE  -
direct the Accounting Office to i) comply with the provision of the MNGAS on the proper recognition of procured supplies so as not to misstate financial reports; and ii) exert efforts in the preparation and maintenance of the required SLCs;
instruct the GSO to continue the efforts started in preparing the required SCs and regularly coordinate with the Accounting Office on the reconciliation of their records; and
compel the constituted Inventory Committee members to conduct semestral physical count and prepare and submit RPCI thereafter to the Audit Team.
Management’s comment
The Management informed the Audit Team that an employee was already designated for the maintenance of PCs and will be in-charged in the physical count of properties. It was further communicated that a Memorandum was issued requiring the inventory taking of assets in each department to support the annual physical count.

We recommended that the LCE require the:
Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of PPEs and come up with RPCPPE with its related reconciliation as required by Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Accounting Office and the GSO/Municipal Treasurer, as the case may be, to maintain the PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for all LGU properties. Also, observe the periodic reconciliation of the two records for proper monitoring of agency assets.
Management’s comment
The LCE informed that he already instructed the concerned Offices to make all the necessary efforts to maintain all prescribed records required by COA.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Accounting Office to i) update the accumulated depreciation accounts and record the adjustments in the government equity account for the prior year depreciation expenses of the additional PPEs not previously recorded; and ii) prepare a Lapsing Schedule for Depreciation which serves as a guide in the computation of annual depreciation.
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the current portion of all long-term loans payable to current liabilities pursuant to the above-cited provisions.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
Accounting Office to i) prepare and maintain SLs and registries for the special account pursuant to Section 161, Volume I of the LGAM; ii) ensure that disbursement of funds are in accordance with existing laws and regulations such as the R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001; and iii)  require the concerned personnel to submit the documents for the financial transactions on the utilization of funds given by the PGT and transmit therewith to the Audit Team;
Local Finance Committee (LFC) to i) prepare its Report on Fund Utilization for the BGCM and reconcile it with the report prepared by the Accounting Office; and ii) fully conform with the requirements set forth in LBC No. 125; and
BAC to i) initiate and strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 on the appropriate  manner of procuring goods needed by the LGU; and ii) faithfully adhere to GPPB Circulars and other relevant laws, rules and regulations specifically in the emergency procurement of goods.
Management’s comment
The Management averred that the BGCM Utilization Report was submitted by the LFC to all concerned agencies last November 2020. It was also claimed that the BAC and BAC Secretarial were advised to follow the GPPB Circular.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned personnel to i) be informed of the reportorial and monitoring requirement as mentioned in COA Circular No. 2020-009, in relation to COA Circular No. 2014-002; ii) prepare and submit said required reports to the Accounting Office for recording in the books and for disclosure in the Notes to the FS, and to the Audit Team for further verification; iii) strategize the receipt and distribution of donations-in-kind identifying the personnel who will be responsible in the preparation of acknowledgement receipts and in the distribution thereof; and iv) perform inventory taking of remaining undistributed/unconsummated donated goods for them to be accounted, valued and recorded in the registries and to be reported in the FS.
Management’s comment
Memorandum Order No. 2021-83 was issued to all concerned to adhere with the COA Circular No. 2020-009 regarding accepting and distributing donations.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with COA No. Circular No. 2012-02;
BAC, BAC Secretariat and Accounting Office to i) submit the lacking documents; and ii) adhere to the GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 concerning emergency procurement of goods and the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the conduct of public bidding;
MDRRMO to submit the required list of recipients with important details such as the actual quantity received, signature of the recipients, date received and the location of recipients for the COVID-19 related procurement for the proper evaluation thereof; and
Municipal Accountant to i) check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment; ii) prepare and submit the report on utilization of MDRRMF; and iii) disclose in the Notes to the FS information on the utilization of the MDRRMF.
Management’s comment
The Management agreed to implement the audit recommendations and an order to the responsible officers will be issued for them to act and comply with the said recommendations.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the a) BAC, BAC Secretariat and Accounting Office to i) submit the lacking documents and/or explanation for the non-submission of said lacking documents to the Audit Team to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension on the transactions; and ii) observe compliance with pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the procurement through public bidding; and b) Municipal Accountant to observe timeliness in the submission of DVs to the Audit Team.
Management’s comment
The BAC, BAC Secretariat and Accounting office were directed to comply and submit the lacking documents.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC and its Secretariat and the Accounting Office to comply with the provisions of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.  Moreover, we recommended that said Offices immediately submit the lacking documents and or explanations/justifications on the issues indicated in paragraphs 11.4 and 11.5.

We recommended that the LCE i) direct the BAC and its Secretariat to submit the remaining deficient documents in accordance with above-cited provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its revised IRR; and ii) instruct the Accounting Office to submit the perfected contract together with the supporting documents related to the CY 2020 LGSF-AM project.
Management’s comment
The LCE informed that he already instructed the concerned offices to forward the cited lacking documents.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Human Resources Management Office (MHRMO), Treasury Office and the Accounting Office to submit the required supporting documents which the Audit Team will evaluate in order to make a decision on the audit of the CNA incentive.
Management’s comment
The Management submitted some documents related to the grant of CNA.
Auditor’s Rejoinder
Initial evaluation of the submitted documents disclosed that the Resolution signed by both parties incorporating the guidelines/criteria for granting CNA incentive was not yet submitted.

We recommended that the LCE a) direct the MSWDO i) to review the profile of the 241 individuals whose names appeared as beneficiaries of two financial assistance programs and assist in effecting refund should it be found out that double assistance was indeed extended to them; and ii) reconcile the number of beneficiaries appearing in the MSWDO and DSWD records; b) instruct MSWDO to coordinate with the Audit Team for the purpose of clearing out the two negative replies received from respondents who denied receiving ESP-SAP assistance; and c)  instruct the Accountant to properly classify received funds from other agencies.
Management’s comment
The Memorandum Order No. 2021-90 mvj was issued to MSWDO to review the 241 individuals, reconcile number of beneficiaries with DSWD records and coordinate with Audit Team.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MHRMO and Accounting Office to submit necessary documents as enumerated under paragraph 16.5 above to the Audit Team for its review and evaluation.
Management’s comment
An order was issued to all concerned offices requiring them to submit the pertinent documents.

We recommended that concerned Officials i) be cautious in preparing payrolls to avoid double payment of benefits; and ii) exert extra effort in locating available funds and/or sacrifice other trivial expenses to provide the authorized benefits to frontliners in the future.

We reiterated that the Municipal LSB be apprised of the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and ensure that the preparation and approval of the SEF budget shall fall within the indicative timelines set thereon for the proper implementation of PPAs. We further recommended that the Municipal LSB submit quarterly and annual utilization reports to the appropriate offices.
Management’s comment
An order was issued by the LCE to all concerned to strictly follow the procedure in SEF budget preparation and disbursement.

We recommended that the Management i) evaluate the manpower complement and warrant sufficient staffing for the Treasury Office, accordingly; ii) orient all AOs of their duties, responsibilities, and accountabilities upon issuance of memorandum orders; iii) ensure that memorandum orders to AOs include their specific duties, responsibilities, and accountabilities; and iv) strictly comply with existing regulations requiring all AOs/employees to be bonded.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Budget Officer: i) thoroughly ascertain the availability of appropriations before the approval of obligations; and ii) ensure strict monitoring of obligations by properly preparing the Registries of RAAO in accordance with the MNGAS;
BAC and its Secretariat: i) submit explanation/justification for the non-use of Standard PBD during the procurement process; and ii) submit immediately the deficient procurement documents pursuant to R.A. 9184 and its Revised IRR.
Accounting Office: i) meticulously check the financial transactions and ensure that all required supporting documents are attached and necessary data/information are complete before payment; ii) immediately furnish the deficient supporting documents pertaining to the transactions noted above pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001; iii) make the necessary corrections/adjustments in the expense and PPE accounts affected; and iv) all other personnel concerned to require certification from the DepEd Accountant that all expenses/disbursements to be charged to the SEF are net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget for the DepEd and the Early Childhood Care and Development (ECCD) Council.
Management’s comment
The LCE instructed the Municipal Budget Officer to thoroughly ascertain the availability of appropriations before the approval of obligations; and ensure strict monitoring of obligations by properly preparing the RAAO in accordance with the MNGAS.

We recommended that the Accounting Office i) submit immediately the DVs with their supporting documents pertaining to the noted transactions; and ii) strictly comply with the submission of DVs and supporting documents within the reglementary period as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006.
Management’s comment
The corresponding DVs were partially submitted to the Audit Team. The remaining lacking documents will be submitted upon review transmittal documents.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Accounting Office to prioritize and expedite the preparation and submission of year-end financial reports.
Management’s comment
The LCE instructed the withholding of the salary of the Municipal Accountant until the submission of all the required reports.

We recommended that the LCE i) require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe compliance with the rendition of reports and submission of accounts within the prescribed period for the timely verification and audit of transactions; and ii) equip the Accounting Office with competent staff who can effectively assist the Municipal Accountant in the preparation of required reports.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to keep up to date the accounting records. We further recommended the evaluation of the manpower complement of the Municipality and implement remedies on the claimed situation of the Accounting Office.

We reiterated that the:
LCE direct the MENRO to enjoin the nine barangays to immediately establish their respective MRFs pursuant to R.A. No. 9003 so that residual wastes will be minimized and reduce garbage disposal fee being paid by the Municipality; and
MSWMB i) review and evaluate the implementation of the SWMP and impose prompt recording and accounting of the MRF operational costs and sales; and ii) institute other revenue-generating measures to promote and ensure the viability of its SWMP.
We further recommended that the BAC and BAC Secretariat furnish immediately the deficient documents to the Audit Team.

We reiterated the audit recommendation that the LCE instruct the GAD Focal Point to observe the timely submission of the GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001. We further recommended that the GAD Focal Point continue its endeavor in the proper monitoring and effective implementation of its GAD PPAs.
Management’s comment
An order was issued by the LCE to all concerned to strictly follow the procedure in SEF budget preparation and disbursement.
We recommended and Management agreed that all taxes withheld be fully remitted intact within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed to continuously remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed that HDMF monthly premiums deducted be fully remitted within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed to continuously remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant immediately make the corresponding adjusting entries as proposed above for the year 2021.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, together with other municipal officials, follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 regarding the “one-time cleansing of PPE Account Balances” in order that derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs will be undertaken.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record subsequent procurement of supplies and materials as an asset under the appropriate Inventory account upon purchase, and to recognize its related expenses only when supported with the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued from the end-users and Supply Officer with attached pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slips.

We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, the Municipal Budget Officer and others officials involved in the planning and budget process:
review the unutilized reprogrammed PPA to address COVID-19 concerns and determine whether the purpose is still valid; and
follow strictly the procedures laid down in the 2016 edition of the BOM and other related DBM guidelines with regards to the making of a supplemental budget through budgetary realignment.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, together with the members of the Municipal Planning Development Council:
Update the Annual Investment Plan through a revisit of the unimplemented development projects and review its viability and necessity, taking into consideration and strictly observing the guidelines set in DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020, as well as other budgeting guidelines; and
Include in the Annual Investment Plan only those projects which can be implemented during the year in order to maximize the utilization of the 20% DF.

We recommend that the Special Disbursing Officer liquidate within one month from receipt of this AOM the cash advance of P1,000,000.00, and refund the remaining amount to the Municipal Treasurer.
We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive requiring the concerned accountable officer to submit her Liquidation Report on the outstanding cash advance of P1,000.000.00, otherwise, impose the necessary sanction provided in the regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and other members of the Municipal Development Council:
comply with all the provisions of every DBM guidelines relative to the utilization of grants or aid from the National Government; and
identify specific activities when preparing the Annual Investment Plan, after considering the needs of the end-users and the constituents.

We recommend that the BAC Chairman, through its Secretariat, post in the PhilGEPS and GPPB website the Purchase Order, Contracts and other pertinent documents on the procurement activities of the Municipality of La Paz.
We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor secure a fast and upgraded internet connection to assure the BAC’s compliance with the on-line posting requirements in Republic Act No. 9184 and other relevant issuances.

1.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the
Municipal Treasurer to complete and update entries in the General
Fund cashbook and establish balances from August 2020 to present and
exercise due diligence in its fiscal responsibility over the financial affairs,
transactions, and operations of the Municipality.

1.8 It was also recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office
and the Municipal Treasury Office conduct regular reconciliation of their
balances so that the adjustments made or errors detected in either books will
be adjusted.

2.8 We recommended that the Management instruct the Inventory
Committee to conduct actual count of all its inventories as at year-end, and
submit the RCPI to the Accounting Office. Further, instruct the GSO to
submit the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued together with the
distribution list and/or issuance slips to the Accounting Office as basis for
drawing the Journal Entry Voucher.

3.7 We recommended that the Management conduct regular
reconciliation of PPE per books and per count to present an accurate and
reliable PPE accounts in the Financial Statements.

4.9 We recommended the following:

a. The Inventory Committee shall conduct a physical inventory of the
all the local road network under the LGU’s jurisdiction and
reconcile the local road inventory with the records of the
accounting office;

b. The Municipal GSO must maintain the Local Road Inventory and
Road Map and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property
Card for each road network;

c. The Municipal Engineer shall provide the local accountant and the
GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for all its local road networks with the help of the
Municipal Assessor on the assessment of road lot component; and

d. The Municipal Accountant shall ensure that all local road
networks and its component will be recorded in the book of
accounts.

5.14 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Local
Sanggunian shall revisit the locally-enacted resolutions if the same are still
relevant/reasonable nowadays considering the cheaper, prevailing rates
offered by the mobile networks. Otherwise, the enjoined individuals should
further decrease the communication allowance to a more conscionable rate.
A detailed report must also be submitted to justify the excess, indicating
therein the breakdown of expenses covered by the monthly communication
allowance given.

5.15 In addition, communication allowance should be granted only to
those officials and employees whose duties would genuinely require
additional communication expenses. The principle of judicious utilization of
government funds as emphasized in Section 2 of PD 1445 must be
considered at all times.

6.7 We recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the concerned officials to submit immediately all contracts
and documents related to COVID-19 response;

b. Instruct the Municipal Engineer with the timely preparation and
submission of POWs, and pakyaw contracts, and other supporting
documents of PPAs implemented by administration;

c. Ensure enough manpower for the BAC Secretariat so that they can
perform their duties effectively; and

d. Ensure the timely submission of copies of all government contracts
and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5)
working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-
001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and
evaluation of the same, so that the Management could be
immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

7.10 We recommended that the Management:

a. Instruct the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to require
contractors to install project billboards at the project sites
containing complete information, and correct design and format in
accordance with COA Circular 2013-004 and its Annexes;

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to require attachment of photos
of the installed project billboards at the project site upon payment
of Mobilization Costs or First Progress billing. Information
contained in the billboard should be clearly visible in the photos
and that it can be determined that it is installed in the project site;
and

c. Strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 for the
timely verification of the PPAs’ status and validation of
projects/programs/activities signboards and/or public notices.

9.4 We recommended that:

a. Require the MPDC to install Full Disclosure Bulletin Boards in at
least three (3) accessible and conspicuous places in the
Municipality;

b. Instruct the concerned department heads to update the posting of 14
reports enumerated in Section 3.2 of DILG MC No. 2019-149 as
well as COVID-19 fund utilization reports as of December 31,
2020;

c. Henceforth observe the timeliness in the posting of the required
operational reports at the designated places in order to attain
maximum transparency in LGU governance.

10.5 We recommended that:

a. the LFC prepare the required monthly reports in accordance with
Item Nos. 4.2 and 4.3 of the DBM-LBC 125 and post the same in at
least three (3) conspicuous public places;

b. the LCE send a written notice to the offices as required in Item No.
4.4 of the same circular including the Commission on Audit when
said reports have been posted; and

c. the management strictly adhere to posting and reportorial
requirements of public funds to uphold transparency,
responsibility and accountability.

11.8 We recommended that Management submit the required
supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor immediately.
Henceforth, all disbursement should be supported with complete
documentation to establish the validity and regularity of transactions.

12.8 We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the MDRRMO, MSWDO, MMO, Municipal Accountant
and other offices concerned to prepare the required summary list
together with the required supporting documents and the Reports
on Donations in cash and in-kind and submit the same promptly to
COA;

b. Instruct the GSO-designate to coordinate with the MDRRMO,
MSWDO, MMO and other offices with regard the donated items
to be able to maintain an inventory list thereof; and

c. Adhere strictly to COA Circular No. 2014-002 and COA Circular
No. 2020-009 on the accounting and reporting guidelines of cash
and in-kind donations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accounting Office and the GSO/Municipal Treasurer as the case may be to:

conduct annual physical count of PPEs and come up with RPCPPE with its related reconciliation as required by Section 124 of LGU-NGAS;

maintain the Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for all LGU properties. Also, observe the periodic reconciliation of the two records for proper monitoring of agency assets; and

exert extra effort to provide details of those unserviceable assets like motor vehicles in order to execute proper disposal thru preparation of IIRUP and avoid further deterioration of the same.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2020 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the:

Accounting Office to (i) provide adjusting entries for the misstatements noted in recording collections and remittances of local tax collections; and (ii) strictly comply with the proposed accounting entries in recording the collection of property taxes as laid down by the NGAS Manual for LGUs and COA Circular No. 2015-009; and

Treasury Office to discontinue the practice of remitting the 1% Special Education Tax to Special Education Fund the following month after collection.  Instead, deposit immediately the SEF Share to SEF books upon collection.

We recommended and Management agreed to provide adjusting entry to establish correct balances of the accounts Due to NGAs, CIP-Infra Assets and Other Payables and be apprised with the correct entries in handling financial transactions based on accounting standards, rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the concerned agency officials to allocate budget for insurance policies covering all insurable properties of the Municipality and to secure said policies from the GSIS or other reputable insurance companies so that the agency is protected and indemnified should calamities arise.

We recommended and Management agreed for the:

Local Finance Committee to identify additional sources of income and/or push for the passage of updated revenue code to boost local taxation and design a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue collection based on the current state of the Municipality’s economic enterprises; and

Municipal Treasurer to monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts from taxpayers /stall owners/concessionaires such as deployment of demand letters and tapping the assistance of the barangays for real property collections.

We recommended and Management agreed that the: 

Management (i) observe strictly the laws, rules and regulations on the use of calamity fund in order to ensure that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to its constituents; and (ii) ensure the compliance of the Municipality with respect to minimum standards on disaster preparedness including the filling out of vacant MDRRMO plantilla positions and acquisition of lacking equipage;

Local Chief Executive direct the: i) LDRRM Officer to prepare the necessary LDRRMFIP; and ii) Municipal Accountant to follow the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 as regards the reporting requirements for the DRRMF; and

Municipal Budget Officer maintain separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for the DRRMF on the following: (i) RAAO - for Quick Response Fund; (ii) RAAO-for Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses; and (iii) RAAO- for Capital Outlay.

We recommended the Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payments, and order the BAC and its secretariat, Engineering Office, Accounting Office and MDRRMO to submit the lacking documents mentioned above.

We recommended and Management agreed to: 

Require the GAD Focal Point to maximize the utilization of GAD fund and ensure that identified GAD-related programs and projects are implemented in order to deliver its relative purposes in line with R.A. No. 9710; and

Ensure that GAD-programs/activities and attributions are genuinely for GAD- related purposes anchored on the provisions of R.A. No. 9710 and PCW- DILG- DBM- NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 dated January 12, 2016.
We recommended that all taxes withheld be fully remitted intact within the prescribed period.
We recommended that the management to continuously remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
We recommended that HDMF monthly premiums deducted be fully remitted within the prescribed period.
We recommended the management to continuously remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to draw a journal entry voucher (JEV) to reclassify the completed projects still recorded in the CIP account, to its appropriate asset account, as well as reclassify the assets recorded in the Other Land Improvements account, also to its appropriate asset account.  Likewise, require the concerned officers to strictly observe the accounting and reporting requirements for the local road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.  The Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee that will handle the annual physical count of property plant and equipment as well as the preparation of the Inventory Report or the Report on the Physical Count of PPE.
observations.reason: The CIP Infrastructure Asset Account for RAY 2 Batch 2 Barangay Project was not reclassified because the Municipal Engineer’s office did not submit to Municipal Accounting Office the actual cost of material allocation per barangay which is necessary in determining the total cost of each completed barangay project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure the implementation and optimal utilization of the 20% DF to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated Feb. 22, 2017.
observations.reason: Some PPAs has yet implemented
Some of the PPAs in the LDRRMFIP has yet implemented due to COVID 19

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fast-track the implementation of the various projects supported by the fund transfers for the benefits of the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary documents on the dormant receivable accounts with evidences that the same could no longer be collected.  Likewise, management should file with COA, the request for write-off  of dormant accounts, duly supported by evidences showing efforts made to collect the accounts, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: Difficulty in gathering the supporting documents required in filing the request for write-off.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator/Municipal Engineer be required to:
Submit immediately to this Ofﬁce the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented for the year together with the monthly monitoring report on implementation of PPAs;  and

Inform the Audit Team within ten (10) calendar days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

We recommended that the LGU create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team to take charge in assessing the effectiveness and efficiency of the LGU gender mainstreaming efforts contained in the GAD Plan, pursuant to Items 3.0 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.reason: Executive Order No. 13 dated August 11, 2020

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board prioritize the construction of the MRF to hasten the closure of the open dumpsite and to ensure the protection of public health.
observations.reason: Difficulty in transporting materials due to poor access road.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that programs, projects and activities (PPAs) that are to be funded out of the 20% development plan be implemented within the year it was appropriated.  We also recommend that the Municipal Planning & Development Officer and Budget Officer be required to evaluate the PPAs included in the continuing appropriation and propose its reversion and appropriation for priority eligible projects indicated in Item 3 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction:
To implement PPAs within the time frame (as per approved plan) 

Instruct the MPDO and MBO to monitor and evaluate the utilization of the 20% Development Fund

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board review its SWMP, and ensure that what has been programmed or planned is feasible to be implemented, as well as prioritize and provide adequate funding for the construction of the Sanitary Landfill to hasten the closure of the open dumpsite and to ensure the protection of public health.
observations.reason:
Dumpsite closed.

Make a safe closure plan Site rehabilitation

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation, as well as assist the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) in the generation and processing of sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information to complement the GAD database.  We also recommend that the GFPS create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team to assess whether the GAD programs implemented addresses the needs of the constituents.
observations.reason: Survey was already conducted and is currently finalizing all its data.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Inventory Committee be required to conduct a complete physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality so that it can prepare and submit the required physical inventory reports to the Auditor and thereafter, regularly submit such reports to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.  Likewise, provide depreciation on those properties that were not depreciated.

We further recommend that the PARs of such properties be renewed periodically every three (3) years.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee was required to conduct complete physical inventory of all LGUs assets and submit to COA the RCPPE within the reglamentary period.




Require the Property Custodian to renew PARs every three years and reconcile records with Accounting Office.
observations.reason:
Conducted the physical count of PPEs for CY 2018 but no report was submitted to the audit team. 
On-going adjustments of PPE accounts




Reconciliation of records in Accounting Office and GSO is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management file the request for authority to write-off dormant/ accounts totaling P173,105.40 to the COA Audit Team pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The management will file the request for authority to write-off dormant accounts totaling P173,105.40 to the COA Audit Team pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: Difficulty in gathering the supporting documents required in filing the request for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of the taxpayers, as well as its total amount due and collectible at the beginning of every year.  Accordingly, we also recommend that the Municipal Accountant record immediately the receivables as soon as the Municipal Treasurer provide such list.  Further, we require that the Municipal Assessor submit to the Municipal Treasurer on or before the 31st day of December each year, an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties.
observations.managementAction: Requested the Municipal Assessor to submit Assessment Roll to the Municipal Treasurer

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Municipal Treasurer and Accountant strictly observe the timely submission of accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit transaction documents to COA within the prescribed period

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Inventory Committee undertake an annual inventory of all insurable property of the agency and submit the report to GSIS and furnish a copy to the COA Auditor on or before October 31 of ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: Inventory of PPEs to be made and inclusion of insurance payment to the budget
observations.reason: Inventory of insurable PPEs to be made and inclusion of insurance payment to the budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that, management, through the LDRRMO, implement immediately the projects indicated in the LDRRMFIP, as preventive measure to secure the constituents in times of emergencies caused by disasters.
observations.managementAction: Implement projects as planned

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Exhaust all possible means to find the addresses of those whose whereabouts are unknown and update immediately the COA Auditor with a written report on the actions taken to locate them.

Send at least three (3) consecutive demand letters to those Accountable Officers in item (c) once their addresses are obtained, and furnish the COA Auditor with copies duly- received by the Accountable Officers.

File the appropriate charges against those erring Accountable Officers in item (c) who failed to liquidate the cash advances despite the 3 demand letters and furnish the COA Auditor with certified copies.

Their addresses cannot be located



Not all Accountable Officers were sent copies of demand letters

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

Management pay the salaries, wages, and other monetary benefits of the agency’s elective officials, regular, casual, contractual and job order personnel through Automated Teller Machine (ATM) instead of cash.

Management coordinate with nearby agencies and with the AGDB for the installation of ATMs nearby.

The management coordinated already with Land Bank of the Phil. 



90% of the employees were already enrolled in their system. Currently, the LGU is waiting the rest of its employees’ enrollment to the system so it can fully implement the payment of salary through ATM.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Request in writing the bank for a copy of documents pertaining to closed accounts and bank debit and credit memo.

Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the books based on the supporting documents secured by management.

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the paid checks and bank debit and credit memo, and to inform management immediately in case bank debit and credit memo are not attached to the Bank Statement in order for management to request for a copy thereof.
observations.managementAction: Send a letter request to the bank inquiring the status of the dormant accounts
