observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Coordinate with concerned previous Municipal Treasurers and Municipal Accountant to locate and submit to the Municipal Auditor the Disbursement Vouchers and paid checks which were not recorded in the books to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.

We recommend that management:

Record all purchases of supplies and materials in an Inventory Account both in the Accounting Office and in the General Services Office. As such, the General Services Office is responsible in the safe keeping and issuance of these supplies and materials by requiring the end-users to fill up a Requisition and Issue Slip duly approved by the approving official, as well as the preparation and maintenance of a Supplies Ledger Card for each type of supplies.
 
Enjoin all department heads to submit to the General Services Office their usage of the various supplies and materials thru the Requisition and Issue Slip, so that the latter can prepare the required Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued monthly which will become the basis for recording the expenses in the books and direct the General Services Office to properly maintain the supplies cards/stock cards.

Require the Inventory Team to conduct Inventory Supplies and Materials semestrally and to submit the corresponding report using the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories.   Instruct the Accounting Office to regularly reconcile this Inventory Report submitted by the General Services Office as against their own record and to verify discrepancies in the process and to adjust records accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Corrective entry has been made for Supplies and Spare Parts Inventory in the previous years. Only Accountable Forms was carried in inventory.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel who will take charge in the recording, issuance and safekeeping of the supplies and materials.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the actual Itinerary of Travel of each LGU official and employee who participated in the Integrating Gender and Development in Strategic Performance Management System Seminar /Workshop held on July 17-18, 2014 at Cebu City.
observations.managementAction: Instructed all personnel concerned to submit needed documents.
observations.reason: No actual Itinerary submitted by each LGU official and employee who participated in the seminar.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledger for each special account in the General Fund and prepare the corresponding financial statements to form part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance, as required under Sections 105, 107 and 110, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.reason:
Lack of accounting personnel who will take charge in the recording of financial transactions of the Economic Enterprise.
Only subsidiary ledgers were maintained. The budget of each economic enterprise is still incorporated in the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should require the Municipal Treasurer as well as the Revenue Collection Clerk to strictly adhere the provisions of Sections 69 of PD 1445 and Section 32 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Vol. 1 on the remittance/deposit of collections and to stop the practices of not depositing collections intact daily or the next banking day.
observations.reason:
RCCs turn-over their collections to Liquidating Office daily.

Collections are deposited bank once or twice a week only.
The Mun. Treasurer having multi functions and no bank in the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive instruct the Municipal     Treasurer to furnish the Municipal   Accountant at the beginning of the   year a certified list of tax payers and the amount due and collectible   during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2019 AAR. Audit Observation No. 5

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive instruct the Municipal     Accountant to recover all documents  related to the receivables and       consider to perform required         procedures under COA Circular        2016-005 as prelude to the request   for write-off.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive: a) represent to Sangguniang Bayan and proper amendment of the Municipal Ordinance governing the operation of LWSS and consider the inclusion of penalty provision; b) instruct Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with Engineering’s Office and improve the collection strategies by strictly enforcing the provisions of the ordinance and disallowing the execution of promissory notes; c) direct the Engineering’s Office to prepare a monthly report of collectibles and submit a copy to Treasury and Accounting Office; and d) require Accounting Office to record collectibles based on the report to be submitted by the Engineering’s Office.
observations.reason: The Engineering Office prepared and submitted the monthly report of collectibles to Treasury Office. But, the Accounting office did not record receivables based on the report submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) immediately maintain proper recording and accounting of the MRF production and sales to properly monitor the income generated from the operation of the MRF; and (b) institute other revenue-generating measures to promote and ensure the viability of its Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2019 AAR. Audit Observation No. 18

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee invite qualified observers both from the private sector and the NGO to observers to the procurement process in accordance with Sec. 13.1 and 13.2 of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the Management (a) adhere to the provisions of the laws, rules and regulations for government procurement; and (b) require the Municipal Engineer, Members of the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to review, evaluate and ensure the completeness of the documents supporting the contracts entered into by the Municipality in order to prove the integrity of the bidding process conducted and also to facilitate their submission for audit purposes.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2019 AAR. Audit Observation No. 19

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) ensure the full implementation of the GAD PPAs during the year and the fund was utilized in accordance with the above guidelines to fully achieve the GAD mandate in accordance with Section 28 of Republic Act No. 10155; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant and the GAD Focal Point to submit GAD utilization report and accomplishment report within the prescribed period under COA Circular No. 2014-001.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2019 AAR. Audit Observation No. 21

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines for the local road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly adhered to, thus Management should require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the Local Road Network and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System. Likewise, enjoin the General Services Officer to maintain Local Road Network Property Card, and render a Report on Local Road Network.
observations.managementAction:
To Create new set of members of the Inventory Committee and to conduct complete physical count of the Local Road Network and to prepare and submit the report to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

To Instruct the GSO to prepare and maintain property ledger cards for Local Road Network account.
observations.reason: Failed to conduct complete physical count of the Local Road Network for CY 2019 and failed to prepare and maintain property ledger cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management file the request for authority to write-off dormant/ accounts totaling P173,105.40 to the COA Audit Team pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The management will file the request for authority to write-off dormant accounts totaling P173,105.40 to the COA Audit Team pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: Documentary requirements in Section 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 were not complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of the taxpayers, as well as its total amount due and collectible at the beginning of every year.  Accordingly, we also recommend that the Municipal Accountant record immediately the receivables as soon as the Municipal Treasurer provide such list.  Further, we require that the Municipal Assessor submit to the Municipal Treasurer on or before the 31st day of December each year, an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties.
observations.managementAction: Requested the Municipal Assessor to submit Assessment Roll to the Municipal Treasurer
observations.reason:
Could not determine the correct tax due of some real properties because the Assessment Roll submitted by the Municipal Assessors differs that with the records of the Municipal 
Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Exert extra effort to coordinate / follow-up with concerned agencies to facilitate the release of the necessary documents for those motor vehicles with lacking documentary requirements for purposes of registration thereof with the LTO under the name of the municipality.  Once these documents are available, instruct the GSO to immediately register these motor vehicles with the LTO.

Instruct the GSO to immediately renew the registration of the Multi Cab with plate number SKY 389.  Henceforth, oblige the registration of motor vehicles promptly.
observations.managementAction:
Repairs needed of the 3 dump trucks would be very costly. The units are located in the motor pool.
Also, the 3 units  multi-cab are parked at the municipal compound need some repairs.
observations.reason:
The 3 units dump trucks and 2 units multi-cab has no retrievable documents for registration because it was destroyed during the typhoon Yolanda


The 1 unit multi-cab has no registration document. It was turned-over by the donor NGO (International Media Corp).  Only donation papers were available.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the actual Itinerary of Travel of each LGU official and employee who participated in the Integrating Gender and Development in Strategic Performance Management System Seminar /Workshop held on July 17-18, 2014 at Cebu City.
observations.managementAction: Instructed all personnel concerned to submit needed documents.
observations.reason: No actual Itinerary submitted by each LGU official and employee who participated in the seminar.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements of the Municipality. Also, that the Accounting and Treasury Office reconcile their records at least quarterly to zero out variances on cash records. Further, we recommend that the Treasury Office consider the opening a new bank account for Time Deposit to separate the two accounts for General and Trust Fund through Sangguniang Bayan Resolution.

We recommended that the:
Accounting Office (i) immediately stop the erroneous practice of recognizing outright expense for inventory items procured by the agency and use the appropriate inventory account in recording said stocks, subject to adjustment at year-end should physical stock take showed inventories on hand; and (ii) prepare and maintain the required SLCs despite lack of personnel; and
GSO continue the efforts started in preparing the required SCs and regularly coordinate with the Accounting Office for purposes of reconciling of records; and
Local Chief Executive constitute the Inventory Committee who shall be responsible for the conduct of semestral stock take and submission of RPCI to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accounting Office and the GSO/Municipal Treasurer as the case may be to:
conduct annual physical count of PPEs and come up with RPCPPE with its related reconciliation as required by Section 124 of LGU-NGAS;
maintain the Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for all LGU properties. Also, observe the periodic reconciliation of the two records for proper monitoring of agency assets; and
exert extra effort to provide details of those unserviceable assets like motor vehicles in order to execute proper disposal thru preparation of IIRUP and avoid further deterioration of the same.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the:
Accounting Office to (i) provide adjusting entries for the misstatements noted in recording collections and remittances of local tax collections; and (ii) strictly comply with the proposed accounting entries in recording the collection of property taxes as laid down by the NGAS Manual for LGUs and COA Circular No. 2015-009; and
Treasury Office to discontinue the practice of remitting the 1% Special Education Tax to Special Education Fund the following month after collection.  Instead, deposit immediately the SEF Share to SEF books upon collection.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis for the latter in setting up the RPT Receivables for the year.

We recommended that the Accounting Office to:
Make the necessary adjusting entries for the prepayments amounting to ₱1,001,309.92, with the corresponding adjustment to Insurance Expense/ Accumulated Surplus/(Deficit) account; and
Observe the proper accounting treatment for prepayments by making adjustments at the end of accounting period to recognize the expired portion of every prepayment.

We recommended and Management agreed to provide adjusting entry to establish correct balances of the accounts Due to NGAs, CIP-Infra Assets and Other Payables and be apprised with the correct entries in handling financial transactions based on accounting standards, rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the concerned agency officials to allocate budget for insurance policies covering all insurable properties of the Municipality and to secure said policies from the GSIS or other reputable insurance companies so that the agency is protected and indemnified should calamities arise.

We recommended and Management agreed that the concerned Municipal officials should dutifully and promptly remit all barangay shares on RPT and CTC in the succeeding quarters within the prescribed period as provided under Section 271 (d) of the Local Government Code.

We recommended and Management agreed for the:
Local Finance Committee to identify additional sources of income and/or push for the passage of updated revenue code to boost local taxation and design a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue collection based on the current state of the Municipality’s economic enterprises; and
Municipal Treasurer to monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts from taxpayers /stall owners/concessionaires such as deployment of demand letters and tapping the assistance of the barangays for real property collections.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Financial Statements for the four economic enterprises of the Municipality so as to provide with the adequate information on the financial position and performance of these enterprises at any given point for decision making purposes.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant exercise diligent monitoring on the submission of barangay financial transactions and accordingly carry out the procedures enumerated by COA Circular 2019-001 in case of non-submission of the BTs; and
Respective Barangay Treasurers observe the reglementary period on the submission of Barangay Financial Transactions and submit a duly receipted copy of the transmittal letter, together with copies of Punong Barangay Certification, to the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive justify, in writing, the hiring of private lawyer as legal consultant despite the availability of the Municipal Legal Officer and present the written acquiescence and concurrence of the OSG and COA prior to the employment of said legal consultant. Otherwise, we recommend to terminate the December 2019 contract with the private lawyer and refund any disbursement made in relation thereto to avoid receipt of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance for unauthorized transaction.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
Management (i) observe strictly the laws, rules and regulations on the use of calamity fund in order to ensure that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to its constituents; and (ii) ensure the compliance of the Municipality with respect to minimum standards on disaster preparedness including the filling out of vacant MDRRMO plantilla positions and acquisition of lacking equipage;
Local Chief Executive direct the: i) LDRRM Officer to prepare the necessary LDRRMFIP; and ii) Municipal Accountant to follow the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 as regards the reporting requirements for the DRRMF; and
Municipal Budget Officer maintain separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for the DRRMF on the following: (i) RAAO - for Quick Response Fund; (ii) RAAO-for Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses; and (iii) RAAO- for Capital Outlay.

We recommended the Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payments, and order the BAC and its secretariat, Engineering Office, Accounting Office and MDRRMO to submit the lacking documents mentioned above.
Management commented that other documents as enumerated were already furnished previously. However, based on the records on hand by the Audit Team, no documents exist nor any receiving copy were presented to establish the auditees claim.

We recommended that the management direct the MENRO to:
enjoin the nine (9) barangays noted and to immediately establish their respective MRFs pursuant to RA No. 9003 so that residual wastes will be minimized and relatively reduce the final disposal fee of garbage being paid by the municipality; and
capacitate the households on how to segregate waste at source by conducting seminars and/or information drive for each barangay of the municipality;
We also recommended that the MSWMB headed by the Local Chief Executive as Chairman to:
review and evaluate the implementation of the SWMP and impose prompt recording and accounting of the MRF from total production and corresponding sales;
institute other revenue-generating measures to promote and ensure the viability of its Solid Waste Management Plan.
Management commented that establishment of MRF for the nine (9) barangays will be implemented immediately. Also, it was mentioned that the non-segregation of waste at source is caused by residents who do not participate in putting their waste properly on designated trash containers. The segregation practice of solid waste management is said also to be one of the major concerns of the LGU and they are creating Solid Waste Management Council in each barangay to educate household and individuals in terms of segregation of wastes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee and /or BAC Secretariat to submit the deficient documents in accordance with above-cited provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR in the procurement activities; and
Instruct the concerned officials to ensure maintenance of documents, insertion of the required provision of the approved contract and installation of DILG Community Billboard containing all the DILG implemented projects based on guidelines set forth by the DILG Memorandum Circular 2019-71.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the GAD Focal Point to maximize the utilization of GAD fund and ensure that identified GAD-related programs and projects are implemented in order to deliver its relative purposes in line with R.A. No. 9710; and
Ensure that GAD-programs/activities and attributions are genuinely for GAD- related purposes anchored on the provisions of R.A. No. 9710 and PCW- DILG- DBM- NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 dated January 12, 2016.
GAD Plan and Budget and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report were not submitted promptly to the Audit Team which is in violation of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GAD Focal Point to observe the timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the:
Authorized Agency Officer to adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 11260 with regard to deduction of loan amortization from the salary of municipal employees who availed of various GSIS loan products to avoid additional surcharges/interests on part of the employees; and
Municipal employees to immediately coordinate with the GSIS Tarlac branch and inquire about the status of loans and seek advice on how to settle their accountabilities, taking into consideration their net take home pay.
Management commented that in line with the noted observation, the employees concerned were already informed of their loan obligation to GSIS thru a letter dated May 19, 2020
We recommended that all taxes withheld be fully remitted intact within the prescribed period.
We recommended that the management to continuously remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
We recommended that HDMF monthly premiums deducted be fully remitted within the prescribed period.
We recommended the management to continuously remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to draw a journal entry voucher (JEV) to reclassify the completed projects still recorded in the CIP account, to its appropriate asset account, as well as reclassify the assets recorded in the Other Land Improvements account, also to its appropriate asset account.  Likewise, require the concerned officers to strictly observe the accounting and reporting requirements for the local road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.  The Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee that will handle the annual physical count of property plant and equipment as well as the preparation of the Inventory Report or the Report on the Physical Count of PPE.

We recommended that the LCE ensure the implementation and optimal utilization of the 20% DF to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated Feb. 22, 2017.
We recommended that Management fast-track the implementation of the various projects supported by the fund transfers for the benefits of the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary documents on the dormant receivable accounts with evidences that the same could no longer be collected.  Likewise, management should file with COA, the request for write-off  of dormant accounts, duly supported by evidences showing efforts made to collect the accounts, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator/Municipal Engineer be required to:
Submit immediately to this Ofﬁce the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented for the year together with the monthly monitoring report on implementation of PPAs;  and
Inform the Audit Team within ten (10) calendar days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

We recommended that the LGU create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team to take charge in assessing the effectiveness and efficiency of the LGU gender mainstreaming efforts contained in the GAD Plan, pursuant to Items 3.0 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board prioritize the construction of the MRF to hasten the closure of the open dumpsite and to ensure the protection of public health.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
present complete cashbooks and relevant reports and documents to the Audit Team during cash examinations
update and balance the Treasury cashbooks daily and rule and foot the same at the end of the month or whenever deemed necessary
maintain complete copies of Reports of Check Issued and other documents necessary to support the accuracy and reliability of the cashbooks’ entries and balances
be sensitive with the deadlines for submission of reports to give also ample time for the Accounting Office for preparation and submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements and
exercise due diligence in its fiscal responsibility over the financial affairs, transactions, and operations of the Municipality.
It was also recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office will coordinate with the Municipal Treasury Office by making follow ups in order to conduct regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made or errors detected either by the Treasury or Accounting Office will be adjusted and posted in their respective books.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the total amount of Php13,927,111.74 from General Fund to LDRRM Special Trust Account to ensure cash availability for intended purpose. Henceforth, full amount should be transferred for quick response, disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
See to it, as the immediate supervisor, that all collectors remit all collections to the Municipal Treasurer / Liquidating Officer in accordance with Section 29 of the NGAS, Vol. I;
Require accountable officers to record transactions in the cashbook daily to avoid any delay in the verification/audit of his/her accountability at any given time; and
Install adequate internal control that will ensure sound cash management practices and forestall violation of the provisions of the NGAS and PD 1445.

We recommended that the Management send demand letter to the concerned Barangays, through the Municipal Accountant, for the immediate submission of liquidation report/fund utilization and accomplishment report of the abovementioned funds transfers. In the case that the Barangay continuously ignore the demand, find any legal remedies and/or strategies to compel them for the immediate liquidation for the funds transferred, if necessary.
It was also recommended to verify and monitor the implementation of projects funded out of the transferred funds especially for long outstanding downloaded funds and demand return of the fund if found not utilized or used.

We recommended that the Management:
Issue a demand letter to all accountable officers who are not already connected with the LGU for the immediate liquidation/ settlement of their unliquidated cash advances and in case of non-compliance, find any legal remedies to compel the liquidation/settlement of cash advances; and
Make necessary actions for the proper reporting of dormant accounts by submitting request for write-off of dormant accounts and its underlying supporting documents to prove that the accounts are no longer collectible.

We recommended that the Management use the maximum monthly compensation of P5,000.00 per month as basis in computing the employer share as provided in RA 9679 or a maximum of P100.00 per month regardless of the amount that the employee contributes for the personal share, in conformity to the IRR of RA 9679.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Local Sanggunian shall revisit the locally-enacted resolutions if the same are still relevant/reasonable nowadays considering the cheaper, prevailing rates offered by the mobile networks. Otherwise, the enjoined individuals should decrease the communication allowance to a more conscionable rate. A detailed report must also be submitted to justify the excess, indicating therein the breakdown of expenses covered by the monthly communication allowance given. In addition, refrain the giving of communication allowance for those officials and employees not covered with an SB Resolution and/or beyond what was authorized in the said Resolution. The principle of judicious utilization of government funds as emphasized in Section 2 of PD 1445 must be considered at all times.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Treasurer (a) to follow strictly the deadlines for submission of transaction documents to give also ample time for the Accounting Office for the recording of the transactions and (b) to submit immediately the lacking vouchers to the Accounting Office.
It is also recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office will coordinate with the Municipal Treasury Office by making follow ups so that complete submission of the accounts and other financial reports of the Municipality will be made regularly, which is on every 10th day of the succeeding month.

We recommended that the Management require the Sanggunian concerned that authorization for all realignment and/or augmentation of funds shall be through a passage of an Appropriation Ordinance as prescribed under Section 336 of RA 7160. Also, instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant that no disbursements will be made from realigned and/or augmented funds unless supported with an appropriation ordinance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same, so that the Management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

We recommended that the Management:
Establish a separate appropriation in the Capital Outlay as well as Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses for the operating and capital needs of the MDRRM Office; and
Refrain from using the LDRRMF for purposes other than those authorized under Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Management ensure that procurement of goods and services should be undertaken in pursuant to Rule IV, Section 10 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in order to have a wide dissemination of bid opportunities and participation of different private contracting parties and to obtain the most advantageous price. In addition, stop the practice of splitting of contracts in order to qualify of shopping and/or small value procurement since it is a clear violation of Section 54 of the Revised IRR of the same Act.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Management:
Charge the payment for honoraria, supplies and materials, equipment and catering services in the appropriate expense account in the PS, MOOE and Capital Outlay; and
Refrain from using the appropriations for Peace and Order for purposes other than those authorized under Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and the Policies and Guidelines of DILG MC 99-65.
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to fill out the JEVs and ObRs completely and appropriately especially the responsibility center column.
Maintain sound accounting and internal control practices over specific appropriations in the General Fund.

We therefore recommended that the Management follow the limitations imposed under COA Circular No. 2012-003, RA No.10121 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1. Likewise, instruct the concerned officials to refund the excessive/unnecessary payments, otherwise, Notices of Disallowance will be issued on the same.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Property Custodian to update the AREs every three years and in case of transfer of property from one to another official or employee.
It was also recommended to file the AREs in alphabetical order for easy tracing. Further, AREs should be pre-numbered and important information should be filled up completely.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, as the Chair of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board shall provide for the schemes and additional appropriation that would improve the dump site and solid waste management operation.
It is also recommended to direct the MENRO to:
Closely monitor the proper segregation of solid waste disposal by every household and establishments collected at the Materials Recovery Facilities as temporary dumpsite, and impose sanctions/penalties for every household and establishments which violated the proper segregation of solid waste;
Monitor the constructed MRF’s in different areas and barangays within the Municipality to see if the facility is operating effectively and its design followed the guidelines set by Section 33 of RA 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management act of 2000; and
Continue to mobilize the active participation of schools, private organization and barangay officials informing, educating and monitoring households in the proper waste disposal from source.

We recommended that the Management ensure the optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund by full implementation of programmed development PPAs during the year and monitor the implementation thereof in order to attain the desired benefits as envisioned in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of DILG and DBM. Find possible alternative ways and exert maximum efforts for faster implementation of the said projects especially for those projects not yet implemented.
It was also recommended that submission of Quarterly Report on Government PPAs should contain complete information and/or prescribed form should be completely filled up for transparency.

We recommended that the Management instruct the implementing office to find alternative ways for the said projects to be operational so that targeted beneficiaries can enjoy the intended benefits may derived therefrom. For the coming projects funded by National Agencies, feasibility studies and pre-detailed engineering should be made before the implementation of the project/s to ensure its maximum utility.

We recommended that the Management:
Review and update the LDRRMF Investment Program annually to ensure the fund is optimally utilized and that PPAs programmed and implemented are responsive to the current needs of the municipality.
Include and utilize the unexpended balance in the Special Trust Fund in its LDRRM Investment Plan and transfer funds of the same amount from general fund to LDRRM Special Trust Account.
Prioritize infrastructure projects and enhance capital outlay expenditures for disaster preparedness and mitigation.
Refrain from reverting the unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund from five years ago until the total unexpended balances from CY 2016 to 2017 are fully transferred from the General Fund to the LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that the Management direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to facilitate the institution of GAD database to have an effective basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation and to undertake thorough gender analysis based on sex-disaggregated data for proper identification of existing gender issues to ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are responsive to address the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive instruct the Municipal     Engineer to gather all the necessary documents and immediately update the ownership of the procured heavy      equipment/motor vehicles protect the interest of the Municipality. Also,  we recommended that the Municipal    Accountant to prepare a lapsing      schedule which will serve as a basis in recording Depreciation Expense.   Lastly, we reiterate the audit       recommendation that the Local Chief  Executive should create an inventory team to conduct physical count and   prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and         Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to COA; and ii) require the Accounting and   Treasury Office to complete their    property ledger cards and make the   reconciliation together with the     RPCPPE.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2019 AAR. Audit Observation No. 1

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive instruct: a) Municipal     Treasurer to; i) prepare Check       Registry and Report of Checks Issued (RCI) to be submitted to Accounting  Office daily; and ii) ensure that the Disbursement Vouchers were fully fill  out before releasing the checks to   Payees; and b) Accounting Office to: i) prepare and submit to COA the Bank Reconciliation Statements of the three (3) bank accounts; ii) prepare necessary adjusting entries for the unrecorded bank reconciling items; and iii) coordinate with LBP to confirm the particulars of all the unrecorded credit and debit memos    especially the amount of ₱10,000.00 relating to payment of Loan.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the 2019 AAR. Audit Observation No. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting   Office to: a) use the “Insurance Expense” account solely for amortization of Insurance Policy availed for the Municipality’s properties; b) avoid the recording of Insurance Premium as outright expense and uniformly apply throughout the coverage period of the Insurance Policy; and c) make an adjusting entry to recognized the unexpired portion of Insurance Premium as follows:
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2019 AAR. Audit Observation No. 6

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management   allot in its annual budget adequate insurance cost of all insurable properties of the Municipality.  We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be applied with GSIS insurance fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2019 AAR. Audit Observation No. 8

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal    Mayor directs (i) the BAC to avoid, henceforth, the employment of alternative mode of procurement, if the requirements are not present or could not be satisfied with; and (ii) observe strictly the laws, rules and regulations on the procurement of goods and services. We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on cash advances and to refrain from accommodating similar transaction in the future.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2019 AAR. Audit Observation No. 10

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive: (a) ensure that the local development fund shall be used only for PPAs authorized by  DILG-DBM JC No. 2017-01 and expenditures for development projects should be aligned and connected to the implementation of development projects, programs and activities; (b)instruct the BAC follow the above provisions on RA 9184 to promote transparency in the procurement process; and (c) direct the responsible officers to submit the approved contracts and purchase orders within five days from execution thereof in consonance with COA Circular 2009-01 and immediately, the lacking documents for infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2019 AAR. Audit Observation No. 10

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRM Officer to prepare the necessary LDRRMFIP and ensure the compliance of the auditee with respect to minimum standards on disaster preparedness; b. Management observe strictly the laws, rules and regulations on the use of calamity fund in order to ensure that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to the constituents, before, during or after disaster/calamities; c. Accountant to observe proper controls in recording the purchases of relief goods and require the responsible officers to submit appropriate documents supporting the distribution of the goods; and d. Management consider filling-up the vacant MDRMM Staff/Officer plantilla positions and employ able/competent individuals who will assist the Head of the MDRRMO in carrying out disaster risk management programs and activities.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2019 AAR. Audit Observation No. 16

observations.recommendation: We recommended that (i) management consolidate the PPMP into an APP as an effective planning tool in the procurement of goods and in order to reduce the procurement costs of competitive bidding is adopted and (ii) the BAC strictly adhere to the policies and procedures set forth in RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations on adopting appropriate mode of procurement.

We recommended that Management ensure that programs, projects and activities (PPAs) that are to be funded out of the 20% development plan be implemented within the year it was appropriated.  We also recommend that the Municipal Planning & Development Officer and Budget Officer be required to evaluate the PPAs included in the continuing appropriation and propose its reversion and appropriation for priority eligible projects indicated in Item 3 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction:
To implement PPAs within the time frame (as per approved plan) 

Instruct the MPDO and MBO to monitor and evaluate the utilization of the 20% Development Fund
observations.reason: CY 2019 budget was not fully utilized because of the transition period of the new management.  Unspent balance from continuing appropriations will be evaluated and amended if needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the MDRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant utilized the LDRRMF solely and strictly, for disaster-related activities as mandated in the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board review its SWMP, and ensure that what has been programmed or planned is feasible to be implemented, as well as prioritize and provide adequate funding for the construction of the Sanitary Landfill to hasten the closure of the open dumpsite and to ensure the protection of public health.
observations.managementAction: Request Technical Assistance from DENR
observations.reason: Previously identified lots did not pass the requirements of DENR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Human Resource Officer ensure that all contracts of service of job order personnel contained complete information, as to the duties and responsibilities or the services to be performed, in order that the accomplishment reports made are justified.

We recommended that contract of lease set forth in the Market Code/Ordinance be strictly followed especially the prompt payment of monthly rentals. Otherwise, enforce the sanctions, surcharges as provided in Section 3.7 of the Ordinance.  Likewise, post a notice of vacancy in the market premises and in conspicuous places so that interested applicants can apply.

We recommend that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation, as well as assist the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) in the generation and processing of sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information to complement the GAD database.  We also recommend that the GFPS create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team to assess whether the GAD programs implemented addresses the needs of the constituents.

We recommend that the GAD Focal Point System submit to DILG whenever there are revision(s) or amendment(s) of the originally-approved GAD Plan and Budget in order that such revisions or amendments duly addresses GAD concerns.
observations.managementAction: Amended GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2018 was submitted last June 7, 2019

observations.recommendation:
9) We recommend that the Inventory Committee be required to conduct a complete physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality so that it can prepare and submit the required physical inventory reports to the Auditor and thereafter, regularly submit such reports to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.  Likewise, provide depreciation on those properties that were not depreciated.

We further recommend that the PARs of such properties be renewed periodically every three (3) years.

Reconciliation of records in Accounting Office and GSO is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Municipal Treasurer and Accountant strictly observe the timely submission of accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit transaction documents to COA within the prescribed period

observations.recommendation: We recommend that, management, through the LDRRMO, implement immediately the projects indicated in the LDRRMFIP, as preventive measure to secure the constituents in times of emergencies caused by disasters.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the Municipal Engineer and the BAC to submit immediately to the Office of   the Municipal Accountant all documents pertaining to goods or services received on account by the municipality.

Require the Municipal Accountant to record in the books the cost of the construction materials received on account.
observations.reason: The said financial transactions were not recorded since there was no valid claims or no documents were submitted to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Coordinate with concerned previous Municipal Treasurers and Municipal Accountant to locate and submit to the Municipal Auditor the Disbursement Vouchers and paid checks which were not recorded in the books to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.

We recommend that management:

Record all purchases of supplies and materials in an Inventory Account both in the Accounting Office and in the General Services Office. As such, the General Services Office is responsible in the safe keeping and issuance of these supplies and materials by requiring the end-users to fill up a Requisition and Issue Slip duly approved by the approving official, as well as the preparation and maintenance of a Supplies Ledger Card for each type of supplies.
 
Enjoin all department heads to submit to the General Services Office their usage of the various supplies and materials thru the Requisition and Issue Slip, so that the latter can prepare the required Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued monthly which will become the basis for recording the expenses in the books and direct the General Services Office to properly maintain the supplies cards/stock cards.

Require the Inventory Team to conduct Inventory Supplies and Materials semestrally and to submit the corresponding report using the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories.   Instruct the Accounting Office to regularly reconcile this Inventory Report submitted by the General Services Office as against their own record and to verify discrepancies in the process and to adjust records accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Corrective entry has been made for Supplies and Spare Parts Inventory in the previous years. Only Accountable Forms was carried in inventory.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel who will take charge in the recording, issuance and safekeeping of the supplies and materials.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledger for each special account in the General Fund and prepare the corresponding financial statements to form part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance, as required under Sections 105, 107 and 110, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.reason: Lack of accounting personnel who will take charge in the recording of financial transactions of the Economic Enterprise.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should require the Municipal Treasurer as well as the Revenue Collection Clerk to strictly adhere the provisions of Sections 69 of PD 1445 and Section 32 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Vol. 1 on the remittance/deposit of collections and to stop the practices of not depositing collections intact daily or the next banking day.
observations.reason: Impractical to deposit collections daily considering the distance from LGU to AGDB.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that the management should create an inventory committee to conduct a complete physical inventory taking and of property to establish the existence and actual condition of the assets of the agency. Also, the Municipal Accountant should maintain PPELC to be reconciled with the balances of PPE per General Ledger and Property Cards to be maintained by the Property Custodian.; and reports /records signified that PPE inventory and reconciliation of property records were not done. Status of those unidentified amounting to ₱17.179M in CY 2016 could no longer be ascertained since year-end RPCPPE was not submitted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant (MA) said that reconciliation of PPELC, PPE per ledger and property card is on a process. The MA said she will establish data for the school building. And set a meeting with DepEd officers thereof was submitted/informed. The Municipal Treasurer / Municipal Accountant will submit the Inventory Listing and PPE Ledger Cards.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 1.
For CY 2018:
a) no RPCPPE as of December 31, 2018 was submitted.
b) Non-maintenance of PPELC and property cards
c) Non-maintenance of updated ARE/PAR
Non-submission and non-maintenance of the above reports /records signified that PPE inventory and reconciliation of property records were not done.
Status of those unidentified amounting to ₱17.179M in CY 2016 could no longer be ascertained since year-end RPCPPE was not submitted.
The School Buildings account should also be transferred to the DepED as the same is under their full control and the beneficial ownership resides in them. Management replied that they shall establish data for the school building. The Deed of Absolute Sale was submitted on April 30, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to regularly update his cashbooks and deposit his collections and henceforth abide by the rules and regulations on deposit of collections pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS, Section 111 of GAAM Vol. 1 and DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 9.
Total undeposited collections as of year-end totaled P100,401.61

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management justify the noted lapses and henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 27.
No report was submitted in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer submit duly accomplished supporting documents as required by the afore-cited regulations. Henceforth, transactions should be properly supported with complete documentation in order to establish the validity of claims. Processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented should be discontinued.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 14.
Based on CY 2017 AAR, for the CY 2017 transactions, some of the required supporting documents were already submitted. However, various transactions not properly documented in CY 2018 totaled P6,685,732.56 involving 120 DVs, showing an increase of P1,454,253.56 or 27.80%.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that copy of the contract documents and Purchase Orders shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days from issuance thereof in the standard form as prescribed by GPPB and as required under COA Circular 2009-001 for auditorial and technical review. Also, purchases shall be accompanied with complete documentary requirements.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 26.
In CY 2018, some contracts were submitted only on March 22, 2019, incurring a delay of 102 to 282 days. For the POs, these were not submitted to COA; POs were only made known to the Audit Team upon post-audit. Note that in CY 2018, DVs were submitted for audit 57 to 397 days beyond reglamentary period.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare transmittal of submitted vouchers to the Municipal Accountant, who after recording in the Books of Accounts, will then submit the same to the Auditor for the conduct of audit so as to facilitate timely communication of noted deficiencies. This way, management could easily pinpoint the persons responsible and determine the causes of delay of submission of accounts and reports. FS/TBs and DVs/payrolls/ORs shall be submitted within prescribed periods.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 7.
Significant delays in the submission of CY 2018 were noted: FS/TB – 22 to 359 days; DVs – 57 to 397 days; ORs – 35 to 391 days. DV/Accounts – started submitted on Dec. 17, 2018. FS – started submitted on Feb 7, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that progress payments should be subjected to the ten percent (10%) retention or substituted with other allowable form of guarantees to protect the interest of the municipality in case of defects in implemented projects or from third party liabilities.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 11.
For CY 2018, no 10% Retention moneys were deducted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management submit monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF sourced from GAA and Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations of the municipality to NDRRMC through the OCD on or before the 5th day following the end of each month and furnish copy to the audit team for audit/evaluation.
observations.reason: For CY 2018, the Municipality received P50,000.00 cash donations from Landbank. However, the copy of the required Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations submitted to OCD, if any, has not been furnished to COA as required under Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-002.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Mayor ensure that CDUR are attached to the DV before the utilization of cash donation for DRRM for proper monitoring and control. Also, the Accounting Unit shall prepare and maintain separate subsidiary ledger (SL) for “Trust Liabilities- DRRMF” for cash donations received amounting to ₱100,000.00 and above per donor per purpose while those below ₱100,000.00 be grouped and posted in one SL Labeled as “Various Donors” per purpose.
observations.reason: Based on post-audit, the disbursement of P50,000.00 under DV# LPZ2018-09-4733 (per accomplishment report) was not supported with CDUR.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management justify the noted findings. Henceforth, ensure the existence of an appropriation in accordance with Section 7 of R.A. No. 7160 and the afore-cited items were made or contracts are entered into by the municipality. Fund availability is, as it has always been, an indispensable prerequisite to the execution of any government contract involving the expenditure of public funds by all government agencies at all levels.
observations.reason: For CY 2018, the following items were noted to have exceeded their appropriation: Skills -13,050.00; Benchmarking of Organized Municipal Emergency Response (52,000.00 less 65,050.00); IEC/Orientation/Conduct Community Awareness Activities, Posting of Disaster Billboards and signages/ flyers (55,000.00 less 85,671.70); Stockpiling of relief goods, Rice, Medicines and Gasoline (770,043.25 less 850,043.25); Preparation of Evacuation Centers and Repair/Improvement of water and sanitation facilities (Capital Outlay) (265,000.00 less 280,100.00); Rehabilitation/Repair of damaged infrastructures (120,000.00 less 195,000.00); Immediate assistance to the victims of calamities/ man-made calamities (155,922.97 less 311,746.50); Total Clean-up Drive (255,700.00 less 295,000.00). CY 2017, excess was P586,540.00; thus decrease by P177,594.77.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management, in cases of lump sum appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB appropriation ordinance/resolution be secured by the Municipal Mayor to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government in accordance to Section 22 (c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the afore-cited jurisprudence. To eliminate the need for a separate authorization and in order to expedite the implementation of future development projects, we strongly recommend that the PPAs to be included in the Annual Investment Plan be specifically identified and be fully described in detail in the AIP.
observations.managementAction: SB Resolution authorizing the LCE to enter in those contracts was submitted on April 30, 2019; however, the resolution contains listing of projects only; the cost per project not indicated.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 30.
SB Resolution authorizing the LCE to enter those contracts was submitted on April 30, 2019 but the resolution contains listing of projects only; the cost per project not indicated as enunciated under the cited Supreme Court Decision.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that the management: • Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases and contracts as mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid impositions of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue. • Minimize payments through cash or reimbursement in the procurement of goods and services.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 41.
Amount of taxes that should be deducted and withheld on various purchases in CY 2018 amounted to P291,103.49. The Management commented that the LGU General Fund Account uses eFPS mode of remitting taxes and will submit the summary of remitted taxes in 2018 to COA; while in Annex Q2, it is mostly reimbursement that is why it is difficult if not impossible for the accounting office to withhold taxes from those transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a) the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (i) create an Inventory Committee; (ii) require each department head to fast track the inventory taking of all properties under their office and submit the inventory report to the committee for validation and verification of the correctness and existence of the property and; (iii) in the event that an inventory committee is created, require it to (1) check the condition of the property, and any damaged, obsolete and unserviceable property shall be reported; (2) prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and; (3) immediately perform the required disposal of unserviceable property b) the GSO/Municipal Treasurer (i) maintain Property Cards per class and category of property, plant and equipment indicating acquisition date, the brief descriptions; (ii) revisit  previous years’ records and reconcile with the information at the accounting office, and (iii) update the property issue slip/PAR to fix the custodianship of the property; and c) The Municipal Accountant to drop the cost of unserviceable and obsolete properties items from the books following the prescribed process.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2018 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor a.) instruct the Municipal Planning Development Officer, Budget Officer and the Accountant to adhere strictly with the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% economic development fund prescribed under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the DILG and DBM; and b.) utilize the fund to finance priority development projects and programs, as embodied in the duly approved Local Development plan.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2018 AAR Observation No. 9

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management allocate the mandatory minimum appropriation or funding for LCPC and instruct the council members to (i) ensure the proper implementation of the formulated PPAs in accordance with the above law and regulations; and (ii) consider outsourcing or partnership with private organizations and entities that provide sponsorship or financial assistance for the implementation of LCPC PPAs.
observations.reason: The management failed to appropriate funds equivalent to one percent (1%).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management consider the negotiation with government bank to enroll its employees in the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system in order to lessen the risks of loss, strengthen controls in handling cash for payroll, and improve efficiency in treasury operations.

We recommended that the LCE prioritize the hiring of mandatory positions to enhance its services in attaining full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the benefit of the Municipality and its constituents. Further, personnel movement has to be considered in order to acknowledge the exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby allowing the entry of new applicants for lower level positions.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2018 AAR Observation No. 10.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (i) immediately facilitate the transfer of ownership of these motor vehicles in favor of the Municipality in order to obtain the required government plates (red plates) and; (ii) initiate the application of renewals for registration in the LTO for the seven motor vehicles.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2018 AAR Observation No. 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management file the request for authority to write-off dormant/ accounts totaling P173,105.40 to the COA Audit Team pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.

Accordingly, we also recommend that the Municipal Accountant record immediately the receivables as soon as the Municipal Treasurer provide such list.  Further, we require that the Municipal Assessor submit to the Municipal Treasurer on or before the 31st day of December each year, an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties.

Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Inventory Committee undertake an annual inventory of all insurable property of the agency and submit the report to GSIS and furnish a copy to the COA Auditor on or before October 31 of ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the Municipal Engineer and the BAC to submit immediately to the Office of   the Municipal Accountant all documents pertaining to goods or services received on account by the municipality.


Require the Municipal Accountant to record in the books the cost of the construction materials received on account.
observations.reason: The said financial transactions were not recorded since there was no valid claims or no documents were submitted to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Exert extra effort to coordinate / follow-up with concerned agencies to facilitate the release of the necessary documents for those motor vehicles with lacking documentary requirements for purposes of registration thereof with the LTO under the name of the municipality.  Once these documents are available, instruct the GSO to immediately register these motor vehicles with the LTO.

Instruct the GSO to immediately renew the registration of the Multi Cab with plate number SKY 389.  Henceforth, oblige the registration of motor vehicles promptly.

Repairs needed of the 3 dump trucks would be very costly. The units are located in the motor pool.
Also, the 3 units  multi-cab are parked at the municipal compound need some repairs.
