The 3 units dump trucks and 2 units multi-cab has no retrievable documents for registration because it was destroyed during the typhoon Yolanda

The 1 unit multi-cab has no registration document. It was turned-over by the donor NGO (International Media Corp).  Only donation papers were available.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should require the Municipal Treasurer as well as the Revenue Collection Clerk to strictly adhere the provisions of Sections 69 of PD 1445 and Section 32 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Vol. 1 on the remittance/deposit of collections and to stop the practices of not depositing collections intact daily or the next banking day.
observations.reason: Impractical to deposit collections daily considering the distance from LGU to AGDB.

observations.recommendation: We are reiterating our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant and other Head of Offices concerned to submit the accounts and other financial reports of the Municipality regularly, on every 10th day of the succeeding month.
observations.managementAction: A directive has already been given to the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports together with all the supporting documents within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: There is still delay in the submission of Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents and the Financial Reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the Chair of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board shall provide for the schemes that would improve the dump site operation and prevent unwanted individuals to dump their garbage in the final disposal site. Further, segregation of wastes at source must be strongly implemented and the collection thereof must be properly handled in consonance with Article 2 and 3 of RA 9003. The board is also encouraged to convert its open dump site to a sanitary landfill as its final disposal of wastes and must include the required individuals to be members of the board.
observations.managementAction:
Information and Education Campaign (IEC) are already conducted in five barangays but attendance was really poor and resistance was notable. But another round of IEC is planned.
Funds from the 20% development fund is allocated to construct a concrete wall and steel gate. Two individuals were assigned in the dumpsite to segregate dumped garbage and put it in a sack for temporary containment. Street sweepers were likewise recruited to help collect household waste as well as scattered wastes and have them segregated before being hauled by the garbage truck for disposal.
observations.reason: Proper solid waste management remains a significant problem.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to provide the necessary appropriation representing one percent (1%) of the IRA for programs, projects and activities for the protection of children and to submit the required documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer in like manner assured to have the prepared FY 2019 Annual Budget appropriated with 1% LCPC portion in the pie, representing the FY 2019 Annual Budget of the Municipality of La Paz.
observations.reason: No appropriation for LCPC in the CY 2018 budget.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
1) To strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 10121 in planning and implementation of the projects, programs and activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office; and
2) To refrain from using the LDRRMF for purposes other than those authorized under Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Applicable policies, rules and regulations will be adhered to and observed.
observations.reason: Nonpriority expenditures charged against the LDRRMF has greatly increased. Administrative and Operating Expenses are charged against the Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Supply Officer to submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of government motor transportation for CY 2017 and hereafter, to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed for the period.
observations.managementAction: A directive has already been given to the Supply Officer to submit the necessary monthly reports.
observations.reason: Reports of Official Travels and the Reports of Fuel Consumption are not submitted monthly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Local Sanggunian shall revisit the locally-enacted resolutions if the same are still relevant/reasonable nowadays considering the cheaper, prevailing rates offered by the mobile networks. Otherwise, the enjoined individuals should decrease the communication allowance to a more conscionable rate. A detailed report must also be submitted to justify the excess, indicating therein the breakdown of expenses covered by the monthly communication allowance given. The principle of judicious utilization of government funds as emphasized in Section 2 of PD 1445 must be considered at all times.
observations.managementAction: We will conduct a thorough review on this matter and further consultations with concerned individuals whether or not, there is sufficient basis to make adjustments thereof.
observations.reason: No detailed report was submitted to justify the excess, and indicating therein the breakdown of expenses covered by the monthly communication allowance given precluding this office from evaluating the prudence of the utilization of government funds.

observations.recommendation:
1.15 We have recommended that:
1.15.1 Management –
1.15.1.1 create or intensify the Inventory Committee that shall
perform the conduct of complete annual physical count of
PPE in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on the
NGAS for LGUs, Vol. 1. A representative of the Auditor be
invited to witness the inventory taking for this purpose;
1.15.1.2 see to it that the Inventory Committee shall prepare the
Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit the same to the General
Services Officer for their perusal and reconciliation with
the other PPE records;
1.15.1.3 ensure compliance with the prescribe procedures in COA
Circular No. 89-296 on the disposal of unserviceable PPE, if
any.
1.15.2 The General Services Officer –
1.15.2.1 submit the RPCPPE to COA for audit purposes;
1.15.2.2 maintain property cards for PPE and ARE/PAR. The latter
shall be renewed every after three years pursuant to
Section 492 of GAAM, Volume 1. And submit a copy of the
property cards and PAR to COA for audit purposes;
1.15.3 The Municipal Accountant –
1.15.3.1 maintain complete PPELC for each category of PPE
pursuant to Section 114 on the NGAS Manual for LGUs;
and submit a copy of the said PPELC to COA for audit
purposes;
1.15.3.2 ensure the submission to COA the RPCPPE and other PPE
records;
1.15.4 The General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant reconcile
their property records after the conduct of physical count to
establish correctness of balances of PPE and thresh out differences,
if any. Such discrepancies shall be dealt accordingly.

2.11 We have recommended that the:
2.11.1 Municipal Engineer and the Municipal General Services Officer
conduct a yearly physical count of local road network projects
and submit to this office the necessary reports and accordingly
update the local road map of the LGU;
2.11.2 Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal General Services Officer the description and cost
segregation of road components of various road infrastructure
projects;
2.11.3 Municipal General Services Officer maintain a Local Road
Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components;
2.11.4 Municipal Accountant keep and maintain subsidiary ledger for
roads and its components and reconcile the same with the result of
the physical inventory taking at the end of the year; and make
disclosure about road networks on the Notes to the Financial
Statements.

3.8 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant -
3.8.1 analyze the CIP asset accounts, and trace back differences;
3.8.2 prepare necessary adjusting entry to correct the balances of the
accounts; and
3.8.3 maintain subsidiary ledger per CIP asset per project for easy
reference and fair presentation of the financial statements.

4.9 We have recommended that:
4.9.1 Management –
4.9.1.1 instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to
ascertain and collect the accounts Due from NGAs and Due
from GOCCs. Measures include confirmation with DBM
and or GSIS on the existence / veracity of the said
₱128,374.52 December 2001 IRA which was not received
and ₱505,486.76 GSIS premiums deducted from IRA. Only
when it is definitely determined said accounts to be
uncollectible that a request for authority to write-off the
accounts together with all the necessary documents be
submitted before the Commission.
4.9.1.2 submit the indispensable documents required under COA
Circular No. 2016-005 to fully support their pending
request for authority to write-off already filed before the
Commission, as enumerated under Item 4.8 (letter a to f)
herein;
4.9.1.3 In the future, for transactions involving receivables, the
Municipal Accountant shall maintain subsidiary ledgers in
accordance with Section 4(e) of the NGAS to provide
complete information on the accounts and to attain fair
presentation of financial statements.

5.10 We have recommended that:
5.10.1 The Municipal Accountant –
5.10.1.1 analyze the balance of RPT Receivable and prepare
necessary adjustments;
5.10.1.2 set-up RPT Receivable at the beginning of the year
pursuant to Section 20 of NGAS; and submit to COA the
source document in setting up the receivable for audit
purposes; and
5.10.2 The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer coordinate
and reconcile their records with the Municipal Assessor to establish
correctness of collectible RPT and determine any changes/ updates
in the real properties.

6.7 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
6.7.1 analyze the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts,
trace back differences and the nature of the accounts.
6.7.2 prepare the necessary adjusting entries to bring the correct balances of the
accounts.
6.7.3 maintain subsidiary ledger as necessary and include disclosure in the
Notes to FS for fair presentation of Financial Statements.

7.10 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer -
7.10.1 submit to COA the eight (8) DVs and payrolls amounting to
₱1,451,127.77 for audit purposes;
7.10.2 Henceforth, ensure the submission of accounts and financial
statements within the prescribed periods pursuant to Section 347 of
Republic Act No. 7160, Section 70 of NGAS for LGUs, Item 4 of
COA Circular No. 2010-01 and Paragraph 7 of COA Circular No.
2009-006.

8.2 We have recommended that:
8.2.1 Management effect the transfer of the amount ₱26,412,118.00 and
₱801,517.00 from DRRM Special Trust Fund LBP Account 1032-
1156-98 and General Fund Proper LBP Account 1032-1077-09,
respectively, to RA 7171 Fund Account 1032-1156-80 to facilitate
effective monitoring of utilization of Tobacco Excise Tax receipts.
8.2.2 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant be guided accordingly by
prescribed accounting and auditing standards in order to present
fairly the financial statements.

9.10 We have reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer
deposit his collections intact and regularly update his cashbook pursuant
to Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS, Section 111 of GAAM Vol. 1 and
DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81.

10.9 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
10.9.1 coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning
and Development Coordinator (MPDC) for the provision of the
lacking documentation cited under Item 10.7 (letter a to i) herein
and submit to COA for audit purposes;
10.9.2 Henceforth, see to it that all disbursement vouchers be properly
accompanied by the required documents and that such were duly
accomplished in accordance with Item 4.6 of PD No. 1445, to
establish the validity of the transactions. Processing and payment of
claims that are not sufficiently documented be discontinued.

11.6 We have recommended that Management submit justification for the
non-deduction of the 10% Retention Money. Henceforth, ensure that
the 10% Retention Money be deducted from the gross billing/s of the
contractor/s, as provided for under RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR,
to protect the interest of the Municipality.

12.3 We have recommended that Management submit justification on the
observations noted:
12.3.1 why the materials needed for the project appeared delivered
only on September 28, 2018, or 14 days after the labor was
started; and
12.3.2 why the project was already reported on September 24, 2018,
or 4 days before the materials were delivered.

13.4 We have recommended that Management, henceforth, see to it no cash
advance be granted on account of infrastructure or other undertakings
on a project basis pursuant Section 174(g) of GAAM, Volume 1.

14.16 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
14.16.1 provide and submit the lacking information and duly
accomplished documents as discussed in Item 14.9 to Item
14.14 herein;
14.16.2 Henceforth, prepare and attach checklist of documentary
requirements for every transaction/DV and ensure that all
required documents were submitted, accomplished and
properly reviewed. Attending payment of claims that are not
sufficiently documented be discontinued.

15.6 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant attach and
submit:
15.6.1 Sanggunian Bayan Resolution authorizing the LCE to enter
into such Contract of Service/Job Orders;
15.6.2 certified copies of Contract of Job Order contracts from the
Human Resource Management Officer(HRMO), along with
the statement of expected work or output;
15.6.3 verified Accomplishment Reports and approved DTRs;
15.6.4 clarification on the Obligation Requests attached to the daily
wage payroll indicating MTO as the sole responsibility center;
15.6.5 Henceforth, evaluate and ensure that disbursement vouchers
are supported with complete documents before payment is
effected to establish the validity of the transactions as well as
the correctness of the accounts used.

16.6 We have reiterated our recommendation that Management instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to pay disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00
through checks directly to the payee in compliance with Sections 2.2
and 4.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

17.8 We have reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer
shall stamp “PAID” on the disbursement vouchers and all their
supporting documents after payment and indicate the number of the
check issued and the date of actual payment to avert any attempt to use
the same supporting documents in payment of future claims to
safeguard government resources against loss or wastage.

18.10 We have recommended that Management:
18.10.1 require the Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman to
submit to COA the lacking documents enumerated under Item
18.5 (Table 3) and Item 18.6 (Table 4) for audit purposes.
18.10.2 Henceforth, see to it that all and contracts are duly supported
pursuant to Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and RA No. 9184 to
establish legality of contracts and payments thereof.

19.2 We have recommended that Management require the BAC Chairman
to submit the lacking documentary evidences listed below required
under RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-01 to support the
award of the contract and payments thereof.
a) Invitation to Apply for Eligibility to Bid
b) Letter of Intent
c) Agency Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) in standard
from
d) Copy of Complete set of approved plans/drawings and technical
specifications
e) Copy of the approved PERT/CPM Network Diagram and detailed
computations of contract time
f) Minutes of Pre-procurement conference (ABC above P5 Million)
g) Copy of Advertisement of Invitation to Bid –
 Printout of advertisement posted in PhilGEPS
 Certification from the Head of BAC Secretariat on the posting
and advertisement at conspicuous places
 Printout copies of advertisement posted in agency website, if
any
h) Minutes of Pre-Bid Conference, (ABC P1Million and above)
i) Bidders Proposals -
 Technical components ( legal documents, eligibility
requirements and project requirements)
 Financial documents (duly signed financial bid form, bid prices
in the bill of quantities, detailed estimates, cash flow by quarter
and payment, work schedules, equipment utilization schedules,
manpower utilization schedules)
j) Checklist on Technical Documents
k) Checklist on Financial Documents
l) Abstract of Bids as Read
m) Minutes of Bid opening
n) Bid Evaluation Report
o) Abstract of Bids as Calculated, supported with arithmetical
computation
p) Post-qualification Evaluation Report
q) Notice of Post-qualification
r) BAC Resolution declaring winning bidder
s) BAC Resolution recommending approval and approval of the
Head of the Procuring Entity of the Resolution of the BAC
recommending award of the contract
t) Notice of Award
u) Performance Security
v) Contract Agreement
w) Notice to Proceed
x) Evidence of Invitation of three observers in all stages of the
Procurement process pursuant to Section 13.2 of the Revised IRR
of RA No. 9184
y) Printout copy of posting of Notice of Award (NOA), Notice to
Proceed (NTP) and Contract of Award in the PhilGEPS

20.8. We have recommended that the BAC Chairman see to it the 5th Edition
or Latest Philippine Bidding Documents be adopted in all the
procurement process of the Municipality pursuant to Section 17.1 of the
2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

21.7. We recommended that the BAC Chairman ensure that the form of
Contract Agreement prescribed in the 5th Edition PBDs is adopted and
all integral parts stated therein form part and parcel of the contract.

22.4 We have recommended that Management:
22.4.1 require the Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman to submit
to COA the lacking documents enumerated under Item 22.1.1
and Item 22.1.2 for audit purposes.
22.4.2 Henceforth, see to it that all contracts are duly supported
pursuant to Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and RA No. 9184 to establish
legality of contracts and payments thereof.

23.5 We have recommended that the BAC Chairman see to it that the
prescribed 5th Edition or the latest Philippine Bidding Documents be
adopted and prepared accordingly in the procurement of goods and
infrastructure projects of the Municipality pursuant to Section 17.1 of
the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

24.2 We have recommended that the BAC Chairman ensure that the proper
Form of Contract Agreement prescribed in the 5th Edition PBDs is
adopted in every procurement of the Municipality and that all integral
parts stipulated therein form part and parcel of the contract.

25.7 We have recommended that Management:
25.7.1 submit their APP for CY 2018 to COA for audit purposes, if
there is any;
25.7.2 Henceforth, prepare Annual Procurement Plan which serves as
a guide on their procurement activities pursuant to Section 7.2
of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Instruct every office to
prepare their PPMP for consolidation to APP.

26.6 We have reiterated our recommendation that Management submit
perfected copy of the government contracts and Purchase Orders to
COA within five (5) working days from perfection thereof pursuant to
COA Circular 2009-001 to enable timely auditorial and technical
review.

27.10 We have reiterated our recommendation that Management instruct the
Municipal Engineer or assigned personnel to submit to COA the list of
on-going projects/programs and activities for audit purposes, in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004

28.5 We have recommended the Management to::

28.5.1 submit clarification for the non-implementation of the work
item Billboard for the project Concreting of Ganao to Sidongan
Road and Construction of Materials Recovery Facility;
28.5.2 instruct the Municipal Engineer and or the Municipal
Planning Development Coordinator to include work item for
Billboard/Signboard in the preparation of ABC/POW
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004 and ensure that such
be implemented to promote government transparency.

29.5 We have recommended that Management henceforth, in case of project
not included in the 20% Approved Investment Plan, ensure that it is
supported with covering realignment/supplemental appropriations
ordinance before the project prosecution and payment thereof.

30.8 We have recommended that:
30.8.1 The Municipal Accountant submit to COA the SB
Resolution/Ordinance on the list of projects to be implemented
authorizing the Municipal Mayor to enter into those contracts
presented in Table 6 pursuant to Section 22 (c) of RA No. 7160;
also, the realignment and/or supplemental budget to augment
the additional costs of the projects, if there is any, for audit
purposes.
30.8.2 In the future, Management instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer to provide the specific details of lump sum
appropriation in the 20% DF Annual Investment Plan to
expedite implementation of development projects.

31.14 We have recommended that Management:
31.14.1 submit explanation why the aforementioned PPAs embodied in
the CY 2018 AIP were not implemented;
31.14.2 utilize the appropriation for the intended purpose however, it
after due evaluation whether these are still priority concerns
taking into consideration current developments, accordingly
realign said funds of PPAs that have become irrelevant or not
responsive to current priority undertakings;
31.14.3 transfer the remaining 20% for CY 2018 in the amount of
₱1,022,980.20 to 20% DF account. Henceforth, ensure to
transfer the total 20% of Annual IRA to the 20% DF account
to fully finance development projects.
31.14.4 re-appropriate savings from completed projects to augment or
finance current priority development projects;

32.6 We have recommended that Management:
32.6.1 In the future, ensure that charges from unspent balance of
LDRRMF be supported with revised LDDRMFIP approved by
the Sangguniang Bayan and LCE in compliance to Item 6.5 of
the above stated JMC; and
32.6.2 Ensure that the unspent balances of LDRRMF shall be used
solely for supporting DRRM activities.

33.8 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant-
33.8.1 provide and submit the following lacking information and duly
accomplished documents:
a) revised Annual Investment Plan (AIP);
b) fuel/gas slips and Driver’s Trip Tickets;
c) BAC resolution on the use of alternative mode of
procurement;
d) approval by the HOPE of the BAC Resolution
recommending the award of the contract;
e) Abstract of submitted Price Quotation;
f) Duly accomplished Obligation Request (OBr number,
office and address of the payee, PPA, and account code);
g) Duly accomplished Purchase Request (PR number,
signature of Municipal Treasurer); and
h) Duly accomplished Purchase Order (PO number, mode of
procurement, place and date of delivery, as well as the
delivery and payment term).

33.8.2 Henceforth, see to it that all disbursement vouchers be
properly accompanied by the required documents and that
such were duly accomplished to establish the validity of the
transactions. Processing and payment of claims that are not
sufficiently documented be discontinued.

34.5 We have recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal
Person to ensure utilization the GAD funds within the approved GAD
Plan and Budget to fully attain the intended purposes.

35.5 We have recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal
Person to identify the GAD PPAs through the use of HGDG which
would be the basis in the budget determination depending on its gender
responsiveness or attribute to GAD interventions.

36.4 We have recommended that the GAD Focal Person accompany GAD
ARs with the required reports/documents and furnish the same to COA
to facilitate audit of GAD Fund utilization.

37.5 We have recommended that Management establish and/or strengthen
their Monitoring and Evaluation system to assess the effectiveness and
efficiency of their gender mainstreaming efforts, and that the M&E
activities be included in the annual GPB.

38.8 We have recommended that Management:
38.8.1 provide the lacking documents cited in Item 38.6 (letter a-d) for
audit purposes;
38.8.2 Henceforth, abide the laws, rules and regulations on the
utilization of SEF. Ensure that every transaction for
disbursement be properly documented before payments thereof.
Attending payments without complete supporting papers
should be discontinued.

39.5 We have recommended that Management submit justification why
alternative method of procurement was resorted to instead of public
bidding as required under Section 10 of RA No. 9184. Henceforth,
ensure to comply with the laws, rules and regulations on procurement.

40.4 We have recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant take
extra care in recording transactions and be guided accordingly by
prescribed accounting rules and regulations to present fairly the
financial statements.

41.7 We have recommended that Management:
41.7.1 Submit written explanation on the non-deduction and non-
withholding of the taxes pursuant to pertinent provisions of
Letters A and B of Item II of Revenue Memorandum Order
No. 23-2014; and or require the payee to submit proof that he
has paid the corresponding withholding taxes, as the case may be;
41.7.2 Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant, that in the
future, deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding
VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases of goods and services as
mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid impositions
of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue.

42.2 We have recommended that Management enforce settlements of the
above suspensions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer to gather all the necessary documents and immediately update the ownership of the procured heavy equipment/motor vehicles protect the interest of the Municipality. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing schedule which will serve as a basis in recording Depreciation Expense. Lastly, we reiterate the audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive should create an inventory team to conduct physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to COA; and ii) require the Accounting and Treasury Office to complete their property ledger cards and make the reconciliation together with the RPCPPE.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of tax payers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct: a) Municipal Treasurer to; i) prepare Check Registry and Report of Checks Issued (RCI) to be submitted to Accounting Office daily; and ii) ensure that the Disbursement Vouchers were fully fill out before releasing the checks to Payees; and b) Accounting Office to: i) prepare and submit to COA the Bank Reconciliation Statements of the three (3) bank accounts; ii) prepare necessary adjusting entries for the unrecorded bank reconciling items; and iii) coordinate with LBP to confirm the particulars of all the unrecorded credit and debit memos especially the amount of ₱10,000.00 relating to payment of Loan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to recover all documents related to the receivables and consider to perform required procedures under COA Circular 2016-005 as prelude to the request for write-off.

We recommended that the Accounting Office to:
use the “Insurance Expense” account solely for amortization of Insurance Policy availed for the Municipality’s properties;
avoid the recording of Insurance Premium as outright expense and uniformly apply throughout the coverage period of the Insurance Policy; and
make an adjusting entry to recognized the unexpired portion of Insurance Premium as follows:
Proposed Adjusting Entry:

We recommended that the Management allot in its annual budget adequate insurance cost of all insurable properties of the Municipality. We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be applied with GSIS insurance fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs (i) the BAC to avoid, henceforth, the employment of alternative mode of procurement, if the requirements are not present or could not be satisfied with; and (ii) observe strictly the laws, rules and regulations on the procurement of goods and services. We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on cash advances and to refrain from accommodating similar transaction in the future.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) represent to Sangguniang Bayan and proper amendment of the Municipal Ordinance governing the operation of LWSS and consider the inclusion of penalty provision; b) instruct Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with Engineering’s Office and improve the collection strategies by strictly enforcing the provisions of the ordinance and disallowing the execution of promissory notes; c) direct the Engineering’s Office to prepare a monthly report of collectibles and submit a copy to Treasury and Accounting Office; and d) require Accounting Office to record collectibles based on the report to be submitted by the Engineering’s Office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) ensure that the local development fund shall be used only for PPAs authorized by  DILG-DBM JC No. 2017-01 and expenditures for development projects should be aligned and connected to the implementation of development projects, programs and activities; (b)instruct the BAC follow the above provisions on RA 9184 to promote transparency in the procurement process; and (c) direct the responsible officers to submit the approved contracts and purchase orders within five days from execution thereof in consonance with COA Circular 2009-01 and immediately, the lacking documents for infrastructure projects.

We recommended that management (a) immediately maintain proper recording and accounting of the MRF production and sales to properly monitor the income generated from the operation of the MRF; and (b) institute other revenue-generating measures to promote and ensure the viability of its Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee invite qualified observers both from the private sector and the NGO to observers to the procurement process in accordance with Sec. 13.1 and 13.2 of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that (i) management consolidate the PPMP into an APP as an effective planning tool in the procurement of goods and in order to reduce the procurement costs of competitive bidding is adopted and (ii) the BAC strictly adhere to the policies and procedures set forth in RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations on adopting appropriate mode of procurement.

We recommended that the Management (a) adhere to the provisions of the laws, rules and regulations for government procurement; and (b) require the Municipal Engineer, Members of the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to review, evaluate and ensure the completeness of the documents supporting the contracts entered into by the Municipality in order to prove the integrity of the bidding process conducted and also to facilitate their submission for audit purposes.

We recommended that Management (a) ensure the full implementation of the GAD PPAs during the year and the fund was utilized in accordance with the above guidelines to fully achieve the GAD mandate in accordance with Section 28 of Republic Act No. 10155; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant and the GAD Focal Point to submit GAD utilization report and accomplishment report within the prescribed period under COA Circular No. 2014-001.
We recommended that all taxes withheld be fully remitted intact within the prescribed period.
We recommended that the management to continuously remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
We recommended that HDMF monthly premiums deducted be fully remitted within the prescribed period.
We recommended the management to continuously remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines for the local road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly adhered to, thus Management should require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the local roads and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System. Likewise, enjoin the General Services Officer to maintain Local Road Network Property Card, and render a Report on Local Road Network.

We recommended that Management ensure that programs, projects and activities (PPAs) that are to be funded out of the 20% development plan be implemented within the year it was appropriated.  We also recommend that the Municipal Planning & Development Officer and Budget Officer be required to evaluate the PPAs included in the continuing appropriation and propose its reversion and appropriation for priority eligible projects indicated in Item 3 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the MDRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant utilized the LDRRMF solely and strictly, for disaster-related activities as mandated in the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board review its SWMP, and ensure that what has been programmed or planned is feasible to be implemented, as well as prioritize and provide adequate funding for the construction of the Sanitary Landfill to hasten the closure of the open dumpsite and to ensure the protection of public health.

We recommended that the Human Resource Officer ensure that all contracts of service of job order personnel contained complete information, as to the duties and responsibilities or the services to be performed, in order that the accomplishment reports made are justified.

We recommended that contract of lease set forth in the Market Code/Ordinance be strictly followed especially the prompt payment of monthly rentals. Otherwise, enforce the sanctions, surcharges as provided in Section 3.7 of the Ordinance.  Likewise, post a notice of vacancy in the market premises and in conspicuous places so that interested applicants can apply.

We recommend that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation, as well as assist the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) in the generation and processing of sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information to complement the GAD database.  We also recommend that the GFPS create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team to assess whether the GAD programs implemented addresses the needs of the constituents.

We recommend that the GAD Focal Point System submit to DILG whenever there are revision(s) or amendment(s) of the originally-approved GAD Plan and Budget in order that such revisions or amendments duly addresses GAD concerns.

We recommended that Management exert extra effort by making follow-ups and continue sending demand letters. Further, find any legal remedies and/or strategies to compel them for the immediate liquidation for the funds transferred, if necessary.

It was also recommended that Management make necessary actions for the proper reporting of dormant accounts by submitting request for write-off of dormant accounts and its underlying supporting documents to prove that the accounts are no longer collectible.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, and identify the cause/s of the discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and make necessary adjustments. Also, exert extra effort in determining the details of previous balance under Due to Other Funds account.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to remit all the GSIS and PAG-IBIG contributions and loan amortizations intact and within the prescribed period so not to incur penalties and surcharges and to preclude delay in the processing of members’/employees’ claims and prevent possible forfeiture of the benefits due them.

It is also recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office exert extra effort in constructing subsidiary ledger for the Due to GSIS and Due to Pag-ibig accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer (a) to be sensitive with the deadlines for submission of transaction documents to give also ample time for the Accounting Office for the recording of the transactions and (b) to submit immediately the lacking vouchers to the Accounting Office.

It is also recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office will coordinate with the Municipal Treasury Office by making follow ups so that complete submission of the accounts and other financial reports of the Municipality will be made regularly, which is on every 10th day of the succeeding month.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary correcting entries regarding the deficiencies noted, if there is any, and to affix her signature in every journal entry to attest the correctness thereof.

It is also recommended that purchases of goods and supplies in bulk should be recorded to its proper inventory account upon purchase and eventually recorded to its proper expense account upon consummation or distribution.

Further, accountable officer is also required to prepare a Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (MRSMI) and submit the said report, together with the copy of distribution list properly received by the beneficiaries, to the Accounting Office for recording purposes in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management:
Establish a separate appropriation in the Capital Outlay as well as Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses for the operating and capital needs of the MDRRM Office;
Charge operational expenses of La Paz Hospital against the collection of its operation.
Send the LDRRM Officer to related trainings to acquire more knowledge in planning, utilization and implementation of LDRRMF; and
Refrain from using the LDRRMF for purposes other than those authorized under Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
We recommended that Management submit immediately the lacking supporting documents and strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-001 pertaining to fund releases to NGO/PO requirements.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
Charge the payment for security services to the LGU, supplies and materials, equipment and catering services in the appropriate expense account in the MOOE and Capital Outlay; and
Refrain from using the appropriations for Peace and Order for purposes other than those authorized under Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and the Policies and Guidelines of DILG MC 99-65.

We recommended that Management direct those involved in planning and implementation to monitor closely and hasten the implementation of the projects and optimize the utilization of 20 percent Development Fund through a) proper planning of project including timing of execution; b) coordinated action from LGU officials for better result; and c)strict monitoring in project implementation to ensure accomplishment of the project by setting time frames and target dates so that the constituents could avail of the benefits of the programs and improve the living and economic condition of the people of the municipality.

It is also recommended to furnish the Office of the Auditor of certified copies of the suspension orders for its immediate determination if the grounds for suspension is reasonable or not.

We recommended that Management instruct the implementing office to fast track the implementation of the water system and FMR projects so that beneficiaries can enjoy the safe and potable water as well as the intended benefits of the FMR.

We recommended that Management instruct the implementing office to fast track the implementation of the projects so that targeted beneficiaries can enjoy the intended benefits may derived therefrom.
It was also recommended to impose and compute corresponding liquidated damages to the supplier for the delay of the delivery of the said equipment and its accessories.

We recommended that Management instruct the concerned offices to optimize the utilization of all downloaded funds from National Agencies by finding alternative ways to fast track the implementation of the program for the benefits of the intended beneficiaries and to prevent the return of the downloaded funds due to non/partial implementation.

We recommended that Management instruct the concerned personnel to include the unexpended balance of Php16,138,516.09 in its LDRRM Investment Plan and transfer funds of the same amount from general fund to LDRRM Special Trust Account. Further, utilization thereof should be included in the Monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF.

It is also recommended that Management maximize the utilization of LDRRMF for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation pursuant to DILG DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the Chair of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board shall provide for the schemes that would improve the dump site operation.

We further recommended that the management direct the MENRO to:
Request for additional appropriation intended solely for the improvement of solid waste management;
Provide containers/receptacles which will be put in the public places and these containers must be properly marked or identified for on-site collections such as compostable, non-recyclable, recyclable, special waste and/or any other classifications as may be determined by the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board;
Closely monitor the proper segregation of solid waste disposal by every household and establishments collected at the Materials Recovery Facilities as temporary dumpsite, and impose sanctions/penalties for every household and establishments which violated the proper segregation of solid waste; and
Monitor the constructed MRF’s in different areas and barangays within the Municipality to see if the facility is operating effectively and its design followed the guidelines set by Section 33 of RA 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management act of 2000.

It is also recommended to mobilize the active participation of schools, private organization and barangay officials informing, educating and monitoring households in the proper waste disposal from source.

We have recommended that in order to establish accurately and reliably the balances of local road networks and the related existence and condition thereof, management should undertake the following: • The Municipal Engineer and the Property Custodian conduct a yearly physical count of local road network projects and consequently update the local road map of the LGU • The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian the description and cost segregation of road components of various road infrastructure projects; • The Municipal Accountant keep and maintain subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical inventory taking at the end of each year; and make disclosure about road networks on the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer (ME) and property custodian conducted annual physical count of Local Road Network as submitted and consequently on April 16, 2019. The ME would provide description and cost of the road components and to the MA. The MA said she will maintain and keep subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and submit the same to COA.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 2.
The Inventory of Road Network as of March 2019 was submitted and consequently on April 16, 2019. No Road Inventory of Road Network submitted did not contain the indispensable columns for Components, Date Constructed and Cost. It is implied that no reconciliation of records was done/ not feasible. No Subsidiary Ledger on Road Network was presented for audit.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that the management: • Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to establish existence and determine nature of the accounts. Measures which include sending demand letters to debtors reminding them of their overdue obligations should be made; • Accounting unit should maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The MA said that they will exert more effort to establish existence and determine nature of the accounts. The MA has submitted request letter for authority to write-off the dormant accounts; however, such was not properly documented, to include proof of exhaustion of all possible remedies by the Management to collect the receivables and demand liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 4.
The MA submitted request letter of write-off and all available data. The request was not supported by the full set of required documents under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that the management religiously observe the rules and regulations on cash advances. Disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00 shall be made through checks and paid directly to the payee in compliance with Sections 2.2 and 4.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 16.
In CY 2017, cash payments totaled P13,708,277.98 involving 136 DVs; in CY 2018, P5,670,009.84 involving 88 DVs. Thus, cash payments exceeding P15k were lessened in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that all Memoranda of Agreement entered into by the municipality or Notice of Authority to Debit Account be submitted to the audit team to verify if the specific purpose to which the fund was established was met.
observations.managementAction: The agency submitted required NADAI and MOA. Submitted MOA on April 25, 2019: Required MOA per 2017 AAR: • PFC Pasalubong Center P3M - submitted • Benben Salintubig, P3M – not yet submitted • Supplemental Feeding - submitted
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 7.
For CY 2017: PFC Pasalubong Center P3M - submitted; Benben Salintubig, P3M – not yet submitted; Supplemental Feeding - submitted.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that management submit the Approved DTR, Letter of authorization, and other documents that supports the payment of salaries and wages of regular employees in compliance with PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012 – 001. • We have reiterated our recommendation that management submit immediately the DTR and Accomplishment Reports and other supporting documents for audit. Henceforth, management should see to it that provisions of the cited regulations be strictly followed/complied with. Likewise, refrain from processing claims without complete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The agency will print the hard copy of the DTR and submit the same to COA. For CY 2017, submitted the Job Order Contracts on April 30, 2019. The expected output was however generic. The Municipal Accountant said that for the approved DTR’s, the LGU is using Biometric and the HRMO is using the system generated report as reference in computing the Job Orders deductions due to their late and absences as they are gearing towards paperless transactions and saving bond papers, nonetheless, as required, the LGU will submit the printout of the DTRs and other pertinent documents. Also, they submitted to this Office the SB Resolution authorizing the mayor to hire contractual and Job Order on May 16, 2019; and Job Order Contracts on April 30, 2019.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 15.
For CY 2017, submitted the Job Order Contracts on April 30, 2019 but the duties and responsibilities not specific. The required Accomplishment Reports not submitted. For CY 2018, payments totaled P4,994,828.48 to 79 Job Orders. Submitted Job Order Contracts on April 30, 2019 but duties and responsibilities generic. Other required documents not yet submitted. Discussed in Part II, Observation No. 15.
The SB Resolution No. 29 s. of 2018 submitted was enacted on December 3, 2018; thus, presumed pertains to CY 2019, not for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer shall stamp “PAID” the disbursement vouchers and all their supporting documents after payment and indicate the number of the check issued and the date of actual payment to avert any attempt to use the same supporting documents in payment of future claims, thereby safeguarding government resources against loss or wastage.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to comply with the recommendation moving forward in CY 2019.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 17.
Stamped “Paid” only for Jan to June. Payments of infra, DVs under Trust Fund and SEF not stamped “PAID”.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to locate and submit the Disbursement Vouchers of the above transactions, as well as the supporting documents attached herewith in compliance with Section 4 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The MA has submitted the DVs.
observations.reason: Per 2017 AAR, the DVs were already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management prepare APP which shall serve as a guide in the procurement activities. Every office shall prepare their PPMP for consolidation to APP. The BAC should observe the date of implementation of the items listed in the APP and see to it that the appropriate mode of procurement shall be applied.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer will submit prepared APP to the Auditor.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 25.
The APP for CY 2018 was submitted only on May 24, 2019. Still subject for review.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management ensure that the municipality shall submit the necessary written report regarding the purchased motor vehicle to DILG as required by the DILG MC No. 2014-155. Also, advise the municipal accountant to submit the Deed of Absolute Sale for verification. We further recommend that the Municipality comply with the requirements of the DBM on the purchase of second hand vehicles, submission of Purchase Orders/Contracts as required under COA Circular 2009-001 and complete documentation of claims as provided by Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Management will justify the purchase of 2nd hand motor vehicle. The Deed of Absolute Sale was submitted on April 30, 2019. The Management submitted BAC Resolution recommending the use of alternative mode of procurement for this transaction. Also, the PO is attached in the DV upon submission of the DVs for audit.
observations.reason: To date, copy of duly received “written report on the purchased motor vehicle” has not been submitted/furnished to COA for audit purposes. The Deed of Absolute Sale was submitted on April 30, 2019. The prescribed submission of PO is 5 days from perfection thereof. Non-submission of POs is reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 26.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to prioritize Public Bidding as the main method of Procurement of the Agency, in compliance to the provisions of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC prioritized Public bidding as the main method of Procurement of the Agency.
observations.reason: Conducted public bidding in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Accountant immediately submit all the necessary documents to support the payment of development projects. Henceforth, strictly adhere with the policies and procedures on the documentary requirements for all disbursements/purchases before payments are made.
observations.reason:

Required documents for CY 2017 were not submitted. In CY 2018, several of the required documents were submitted last May 23, 2018; others were found questionable and others still subject for review.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the following: • For the Municipal Accountant to submit the required SEF Budget as approved by the majority of Local School Boards for proper evaluation • For the Budget Officer to inform the Local School Boards annually regarding their responsibility on the preparation of SEF Budget in accordance with Item 5.0 of the DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board prepared the SEF Budget. The Local School Board prepared the Annual LSB Plan and submitted the same to the Municipal Budget Officer for the Preparation of SEF Budget.
observations.reason: For the CY 2018, it was submitted on May 16, 2019. The report for CY 2017 was not submitted to COA for audit purposes earlier; CY2018 LSB Budget for CY 2018 was submitted on May 16, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the following: • prepare and submit the Report on SEF Utilization in compliance with the provisions of Item 6.0 of DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and • Ensure the full implementation of the activities in the SEF budget to maximize the benefit to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Prepared and submitted the Report on SEF Utilization for CY 2018.
observations.reason: The Report on SEF Utilization for CY 2018 was submitted for audit on May 16, 2019. For CY 2018, total budget amounted to P470,000.00; per utilization report, P200,000.00 only were expended. Cash in bank balance as of year-end amounted to P88,859.87

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Accountant immediately submit all the necessary documents to support the above stated payments. observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management form a Solid Waste Management Board that will prepare Solid Waste Management Plan to fully support the objectives of the State in compliance with Republic Act 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Management formed the Solid Waste Management Board and submitted Executive Order No. 02 series of 2017 creating the Environmental Solid Waste Management Board for the Municipality of La Paz, effective January 25, 2017; and Executive Order No. 21 series of 2018, Reorganizing the Environmental Solid Waste Management Board, effective August 1, 2018.
observations.reason: Management submitted the executive order to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management establish their Local Council for the Protection of Children who shall prepare plans and ensure implementation of these in compliance with the provisions of Section 15 of RA 9344 and DILG MC 2012-120.

observations.reason: Management submitted the CY 2018 Accomplishment Report on LCPC on March 15, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and the related subsequent provisions on LDRRMF Utilization. Personnel services, maintenance and other operating expenditures, and capital outlay of the LDRRMO as well as the regular MOOE and capital outlay of the municipality shall be sourced from General Fund. The LDRRMF shall be strictly utilized for the implementation of DRRM programs.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to comply with the recommendations moving forward.
observations.reason: So far charges to LDRRMF were calamity related. Ensured attachment of duly signed RIS and listing of recipients. Listing and pictures were attached to the DVs and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the following: • That the Municipal Accountant take up under the appropriate expense account the inventory items upon distribution to end-users/beneficiaries and not at the end of the year; • See to it that these are properly supported with RIS and listings of recipients/beneficiaries which are duly signed acknowledging receipt of the items and quantities actually received submit the same to the Auditor • That the Municipal Accountant submit the RIS and the list of recipients/beneficiaries (with their address and other necessary personal information for audit purposes) duly date signed/acknowledged, showing the items and quantity of items distributed.
observations.reason: Ensured attachment of duly signed RIS and listing of recipients. Listing and pictures were attached to the DVs and submitted. However, the RIS and list of recipients for the goods distributed in CY 2017 were not yet submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management comply with the regulations on the transfer of unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to Trust Fund. We recommend that the Sanggunian Bayan Members pass a board resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate trust fund bank account for LDRRMF. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant maintain a special account and complete set of subsidiaries for the said funds.
observations.managementAction: The Management opened a Trust Fund Account in which Special Account for DRRM was opened in July 2018. The CY 2017 unexpended balance of P944,845.65 was transferred in September 2018.
observations.reason: The Trust Fund Account was opened and the unexpended balance was transferred in September 2018.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that an Investment Plan for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund be prepared and utilized with the prescribed format presented in Annexes of COA Circular 2012-002. Also, compliance to the guidelines on the utilization of the LDRRMF as prescribed under Republic Act 10121 should be strictly adhered to.
observations.managementAction: The Management prepared its LDRRMFIP with prescribed format under Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: Management prepared the LDRRMFIP with the prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose all the necessary information pertaining to LDRRMF. Henceforth, strictly comply with the disclosure requirements on LDRRMF as provided by COA Circular No. 2014-002.
observations.managementAction: The MA disclosed all necessary information pertaining to LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Implemented as per Management action.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the following: • That the LCE instruct the in-charge or the Planning and Development Office to amend or update the Annual Investment Plan in case of realignment of appropriations through SB Resolutions approved by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan; • Also, the budget officer should ascertain that a specific project is included in the AIP before signing the Obligation Request Form for disbursement of funds.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 29.
For CY 2018, several projects not in the AIP were charged from 20% DF. The management submitted SB Resolution No. 04-2008 for the realignment only on April 30, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that utilization of the 20% Development Fund should be limited to programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure that yields greater benefit to the people in accordance with DBM DILG Memorandum Circular 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: The management limited the 20% DF PPAs that will contribute to the attainment of socio economic development and environmental outcome.
observations.reason: Management limited the 20% DF PPAs that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental outcome.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant record these projects to their proper account. Also, subsidiary ledgers should be maintained in order to easily account these assets in case of physical inventory. We also proposed journal entry.
observations.managementAction: No further action noted other than clarification by Municipal Accountant during exit conference.
observations.reason: No reclassification entry was noted in CY 2018. But it was clarified by the Municipal Accountant that they justified the entry made during their exit conference.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that the GAD Focal Point prepare the annual and performance-based LGU GPB in response to the gender issues and or concerns of the locality and in the context of the mandate; and consolidate the same following the form and procedures prescribed in the Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC).
observations.managementAction: The Municipality’s GAD Focal point prepared the Annual and performance based LGU GPB.
observations.reason: The Municipality submitted on May 16, 2019 a Certification issued on March 6, 2018 from DILG Provincial Office that the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) for CY 2018 of the Municipality of La Paz has been reviewed and found fully compliant with JMC 2013-01 as amended by JMC 2016-01.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipality establish a GAD database to serve as basis for planning, programming and formulation of gender related issues or concerns of the locality before the GAD Plan is finalized and submitted for review to the Municipal Planning and Development Office.
observations.managementAction: The Management established a GAD Database. Updated April 2018 was submitted on May 16, 2019.
observations.reason: GAD Database was established and submitted on May 16, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation to management: • that the Municipal Engineer verify the reported accomplishments before certifying the Statement of Work Accomplished submitted by the contractor. He shall measure the value of the works actually in place in accordance with the contract; and • Municipal Officials should oblige the contractors to implement the projects otherwise, the payments will become the personal liability of the municipal officials who certified the correctness and validity of the claims.
observations.managementAction: Payments of some Infrastructure projects were not sufficiently documented to include SWA and Statement of Work Worked Accomplished and Time Elapsed thus, raising doubt whether such required verification/validation was done by the ME before attending payments of the projects.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 10.
For CY 2017, not implemented: Paid in Sept 16, 2016: Improvement /Rehab of Poblacion La Paz- Poblacion Road – paid was 54% (1,800,000.00); based on SWA, 38.50% • Improvement / Rehab of Buli-Malabbaga Road – paid was 99% (1,993,281.30); per SWA 83.5%.

observations.recommendation: No specific recommendation other than noting that inclusion of PCCP in the program of work would yield longer benefits.
observations.reason: For CY 2018, the Concreting of San Gregorio Barangay Road was executed (details referenced).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Head of LDRRMO (i) to monitor monthly utilization of LDRRMF and corresponding Accomplishment Report and (ii) to observe strictly the laws rules and regulations on the use of calamity fund to ensure that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to the constituents in times of calamities; (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare, certify and submit monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF in prescribed format under Annex E of COA Circular No. 2014-002.
observations.reason: The management was compliant with the rules & regulations on the use of calamity fund but failed to prepare the required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (i) require the LSB to follow and perform the activities and preparation and approval of the SEF Budget with indicative timelines as prescribed by DepEd- DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 or the Revised Guidelines on the Use of Special Education Fund (SEF); and (ii) require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report of SEF Utilization quarterly and annually using the prescribed from for submission to appropriate offices. (iii) require the Local School Board coordinate with the Property Offices of both the LGU and the DepEd in order to strengthen the controls on property accountability to accurately monitor the custodianship of government property.

While we took cognizance of the relentless efforts of the LGU to support gender equality and women empowerment, we recommended that the Executive Committee and the Technical Working Group (i) Endeavor to capaciate themselves through attendance to trainings under GFPS for more responsive planning; and (ii) Update the GAD Code, if necessary, in coordination with the Sanggunian and disseminate the same in accordance with the guidelines provided under PCW Memorandum Circular No. 2009-1 dated September 16, 2009.
observations.reason: The management was complaint with the minimum standards but still failed to submit reports on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Office of the Accountant to submit the required financial records, reports and supporting documents within the prescribed period.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (a) coordinate the efforts of the public and private sectors and implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; (b)  work on the promulgation and implementation of “No Littering” Ordinance; (c) maximize the operation of the MRF and implement the Municipality’s plan of producing organic fertilizer and CHB Pavers from biodegradable wastes and adopt specific revenue-generating measures to promote and ensure the viability of its Solid Waste Management Plan; and (d) encourage the full participation of the barangays in the implementation of the R.A. No. 9003.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2018 AAR Observation No. 11.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Engineer strictly comply with the provision cited under DPWH Department Order No. 197 series of 2016 in the preparation of ABC for infrastructure projects.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR in the procurement activities and submit immediately the above required documents.

We recommend that the Inventory Committee be required to conduct a complete physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality so that it can prepare and submit the required physical inventory reports to the Auditor and thereafter, regularly submit such reports to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.  Likewise, provide depreciation on those properties that were not depreciated.

We further recommend that the PARs of such properties be renewed periodically every three (3) years.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee was required to conduct complete physical inventory of all LGUs assets and submit to COA the RCPPE within the reglamentary period.
observations.reason: Conducted the physical count of PPEs for CY 2018 but no report was submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant obtain the records from the Municipal Assessor, and immediately thereafter, record the unrecorded real properties by using its appraised value, if the acquisition costs are not known nor available.  We also recommend that the Municipal Assessor be directed to prioritize the titling of those untitled lands in order to establish absolute ownership thereof, and for the Municipal Budget Officer to consider the funding requirement for the titling of lands in the budget preparation for the ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: Entries have been made per JEV No. 18-12-2743 and JEV No. 18-12-2744. The LGU allocated funds for the titling cost and other related expenses in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) be required to strictly observe the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as provided for in DILG-DBM JMC no. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.  Likewise, maximize the utilization of appropriated fund in implementing the development projects indicated in the Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: MPDO was instructed to follow the guidelines pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the SEF Budget be prepared by the District Supervisor and approved by the Local School Board (LSB), and duly supported with programs, projects, and activities that are aligned with Section 100(b) of RA 7160, and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017.  Limit the use of the SEF for purposes for which it was intended.
observations.managementAction: The SEF Budget for CY 2018 was prepared as recommended

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit at the beginning of the year a list of on-going and to be implemented projects/programs/activities (PPAs) for the current year, and inform COA within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices are already posted as required in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The office of the Municipal Engineer and the BAC regularly submit the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the concerned Disbursing Officer-designate to immediately and completely produce the missing accountable forms in her custody as then Revenue Collection Officer and remit to the Municipal Treasurer all remaining collections that may have been collected out of the issuance of the missing accountable forms to avoid sanctions under existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommend that management:

Record all purchases of supplies and materials in an Inventory Account both in the Accounting Office and in the General Services Office. As such, the General Services Office is responsible in the safe keeping and issuance of these supplies and materials by requiring the end-users to fill up a Requisition and Issue Slip duly approved by the approving official, as well as the preparation and maintenance of a Supplies Ledger Card for each type of supplies.
 
Enjoin all department heads to submit to the General Services Office their usage of the various supplies and materials thru the Requisition and Issue Slip, so that the latter can prepare the required Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued monthly which will become the basis for recording the expenses in the books and direct the General Services Office to properly maintain the supplies cards/stock cards.

Require the Inventory Team to conduct Inventory Supplies and Materials semestrally and to submit the corresponding report using the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories.   Instruct the Accounting Office to regularly reconcile this Inventory Report submitted by the General Services Office as against their own record and to verify discrepancies in the process and to adjust records accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Corrective entry has been made for Supplies and Spare Parts Inventory in the previous years. Only Accountable Forms was carried in inventory.

Not Implemented




Lack of personnel who will take charge in the recording, issuance and safekeeping of the supplies and materials.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections regularly and to deposit the balance of ₱147,682.34 as of December 31, 2015and henceforth abide by the rules and regulations on deposit of collections pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS, Section 111 of GAAM Vol. 1 and DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81.

We reiterated our recommendation that:

-Copy of the contract documents shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days from issuance thereof and it shall be in the standard form as prescribed by GPPB and as required under COA Circular 2009-001 for auditorial and technical review; and

-BAC should submit written justification/s for non-adherence to provisions of R.A. No. 9184.

We have recommended that management, especially the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), explain the reasons for not abiding with the rules on procurement. Purchases beyond the threshold on shopping and small value items should be done through competitive bidding as mandated by RA 9184. In all instances, ensure that the most advantageous price for the Government of the Philippines is obtained.

We have recommended that progress payments should be subjected to the ten percent (10%) retention or substituted with other allowable form of guarantees to protect the interest of the municipality in case of defects in implemented projects or from third party liabilities.

We have reiterated our recommendation to management:

-that the Municipal Engineer verify the reported accomplishments before certifying the Statement of Work Accomplished submitted by the contractor. He shall measure the value of the works actually in place in accordance with the contract; and 

-Municipal Officials should oblige the contractors to implement the projects otherwise, the payments will become the personal liability of the municipal officials who certified the correctness and validity of the claims.

We have recommended the management to included PCCP in the program of works if additional funds could have been obtained, also implement approved projects in accordance with the objectives of the fund stated in RA 7171.

We have reiterated our recommendation that an Investment Plan for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund be prepared and utilized with the prescribed format presented in Annexes of COA Circular 2012-002.  Also, compliance to the guidelines on the utilization of the LDRRMF as prescribed under Republic Act 10121 should be strictly adhered to.
observations.managementAction: Observations were still noted during the year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management follow the programmed timelines for period of implementation in the AIP to ensure the full implementation of the development projects.  Project implementation schedule shall be made in order to effectively implement and monitor all the projects of the municipality.

observations.managementAction: Projects not capital in nature were still noted.

Likewise, the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contract within five (5) days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents and such other documents peculiar to the contract which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
