observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipality should implement the provisions of COA Circular 2007-001 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of fund transfers.

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should review all outstanding accounts and record adjustments for fund transfers that actually partake of the nature of donations. 

Further, the Municipality should institute action against NGO/PO’s and their officers/organizers that failed to implement the programs and projects funded by the Municipal government pursuant to the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement covering these transactions.
observations.managementAction: Meeting with NGO/PO's personnel were already done to liquidate previous fund transfers.
observations.reason: Still failed to effect prompt liquidations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Management:

a) 	Set a limit to the mobile communication allowance granted to the employees and provide justification/supporting documents on the reasonable basis/limit to the allowable amounts indicated in the Memorandum Order such as the result of study of the frequency of official calls, the name/office of the persons being contacted by LGU official and employees on official business in order to determine the average communication allowance needed for each individual. 

b) 	Require the Accounting Office as an internal control unit to attach complete supporting documents for the claims (i.e. justification/supporting notes, reasonable basis/limit, certification from the officials & employees that the communication allowance was used for official business only, and other documents in needed in support of the claims) and proper classification of the account used in the charging of expenses.

c) 	Show the breakdown/detail of the amount stated in the Resolution No. 167 s. of 2015.
observations.managementAction: We already set a limit to all department heads, assistant department heads and all officers/staff duly authorized to receive communication allowances through SB Resolution No. 113 Series of 2012 which was adopted unanimously on 19th day of December 2012. To those other officers/staff granted with communication allowance through issuance of Memorandum Order, we already required them to submit justification letters for further evaluation and as attachment to the claim.
observations.reason: Still no review regarding their Resolutions and Memorandum Order pertaining to the communication allowance.

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We have reiterated our recommendation that the management should create an inventory committee to conduct a complete physical inventory taking of property to establish the existence and actual condition of the assets of the agency.  Also, the Municipal Accountant should maintain PPELC to be reconciled with the balances of PPE per General Ledger and Property Cards to be maintained by the Property Custodian.
1.10 The School Buildings account should also be transferred to the DepED as the same is under their full control and the beneficial ownership resides in them.

2.7 We have recommended that in order to establish accurately and reliably the balances of local road networks and the related existence and condition thereof, management should undertake the following:
The Municipal Engineer and the Property Custodian conduct a yearly physical count of local road network projects and consequently update the local road map of the LGU;
The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian the description and cost segregation of road components of various road infrastructure projects; and
The Municipal Accountant keeps and maintains subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical inventory taking at the end of each year.

4.8 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to regularly update his cashbooks and deposit his collections and henceforth abide by the rules and regulations on deposit of collections pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS, Section 111 of GAAM Vol. 1 and DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81.

5.6 We have reiterated our recommendation that the management religiously observe the rules and regulations on cash advances.  Disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00 shall be made through checks and paid directly to the payee in compliance with Sections 2.2 and 4.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
6.9 We have recommended that the management justify the noted lapses and henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.

7.4 We have recommended that all Memoranda of Agreement entered into by the municipality or Notice of Authority to Debit Account be submitted to the audit team to verify if the specific purpose to which the fund was established was met.

8.8 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer submit duly accomplished supporting documents as required by the afore-cited regulations. Henceforth, transactions should be properly supported with complete documentation in order to establish the validity of claims. Processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented should be discontinued.

9.3   We have reiterated our recommendation that management submit immediately the Accomplishment Reports and other supporting documents for audit.  Henceforth, management should see to it that provisions of the cited regulations be strictly followed/complied with.  Likewise, refrain from processing claims without complete supporting documents.

10.5 We have reiterated our recommendation that copy of the contract documents and Purchase Orders shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days from issuance in the standard form as prescribed by GPPB and as required under COA Circular 2009-001 for auditorial and technical review;

11.4 We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare transmittal of submitted vouchers to the Municipal Accountant, who after recording in the Books of Accounts, will then submit the same to the Auditor for the conduct of audit so as to facilitate timely communication of noted deficiencies. This way, management could easily pinpoint the persons responsible and determine the causes of delay of submission of accounts and reports.

12.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall stamp “PAID” the disbursement vouchers and all their supporting documents after payment and indicate the number of the check issued and the date of actual payment to avert any attempt to use the same supporting documents in payment of future claims, thereby safeguarding government resources against loss or wastage.

13.5 We have recommended the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to locate and submit the Disbursement Vouchers of the above transactions, as well as the supporting documents attached herewith in compliance with Section 4 of PD 1445.

14.7 We have recommended that management prepare APP which shall serve as a guide in the procurement activities.  Every office shall prepare their PPMP for consolidation to APP.  The BAC should observe the date of implementation of the items listed in the APP and see to it that the appropriate mode of procurement shall be applied.

15.5.7 We have recommended that management justify the purchase of second hand motor vehicles without the benefit of public bidding. We further recommend that the Municipality comply with the requirements of the DBM on the purchase of second hand vehicles, submission of Purchase Orders/Contracts as required under COA Circular 2009-001 and complete documentation of claims as provided by Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

16.5 We have recommended that progress payments should be subjected to the ten percent (10%) retention or substituted with other allowable form of guarantees to protect the interest of the municipality in case of defects in implemented projects or from third party liabilities.

17.9 We have recommended that management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to prioritize Public Bidding as the main method of Procurement of the Agency, in compliance to the provisions of RA 9184.

18.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Accountant immediately submit all the necessary documents to support the payment of development projects. Henceforth, strictly adhere with the policies and procedures on the documentary requirements for all disbursements/purchases before payments are made.

19.4 We have recommended the following:
prepare and submit the Report on SEF Utilization in compliance with the provisions of Item 6.0 of DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and
Ensure the full implementation of the activities in the SEF budget to maximize the benefit to the constituents.

20.6 We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Accountant immediately submit all the necessary documents to support the above stated payments.  Henceforth, strictly adhere with the policies and procedures on the documentary requirements for all disbursements/purchases before payments are made.

21.4 We have recommended that management form a Solid Waste Management Board that will prepare Solid Waste Management Plan to fully support the objectives of the State in compliance with Republic Act 9003.

22.6 We have recommended that management establish their Local Council for the Protection of Children who shall prepare plans and ensure implementation of these in compliance with the provisions of Section 15 of RA 9344 and DILG MC 2012-120.

23.1.3 We have recommended that management submit monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF sourced from GAA and Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations of the municipality to NDRRMC through the OCD on or before the 5th day following the end of each month and furnish copy to the audit team for audit/evaluation.
23.2.3 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Mayor ensure that CDUR are attached to the DV before the utilization of cash donation for DRRM for proper monitoring and control. Also, the Accounting Unit shall prepare and maintain separate subsidiary ledger (SL) for “Trust Liabilities- DRRMF” for cash donations received amounting to ₱100,000.00 and above per donor per purpose while those below ₱100,000.00 be grouped and posted in one SL Labeled as “Various Donors” per purpose.
23.3.6 We have recommended that management justify the noted findings. Henceforth, ensure the existence of an appropriation in accordance with Section 7 of R.A. No. 7160 and the afore-cited jurisprudence, before transactions are made or contracts are entered into by the municipality. Fund availability is, as it has always been, an indispensable prerequisite to the execution of any government contract involving the expenditure of public funds by all government agencies at all levels.
23.4.4 We have recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and the related subsequent provisions on LDRRMF Utilization. Personal services, maintenance and other operating expenditures, and capital outlay of the LDRRMO as well as the regular MOOE and capital outlay of the municipality shall be sourced from General Fund. The LDRRMF shall be strictly utilized for the implementation of DRRM programs.

23.5.4 We have recommended the following:
That the Municipal Accountant take up under the appropriate expense account the inventory items upon distribution to end-users/beneficiaries and not at the end of the year;
See to it that these are properly supported with RIS and listings of recipients/beneficiaries which are duly signed acknowledging receipt of the items and quantities actually received.
That the Municipal Accountant submit the RIS and the list of recipients/beneficiaries (with their address and other necessary personal information for audit purposes) duly signed/acknowledged, showing the items and quantity of items distributed)

23.6.5 We have recommended that management comply with the regulations on the transfer of unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to Trust Fund. We recommend that the Sanggunian Bayan Members pass a board resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate trust fund bank account for LDRRMF. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant maintain a special account and complete set of subsidiaries for the said funds.

23.7.9 We reiterate our recommendation that an Investment Plan for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund be prepared and utilized with the prescribed format presented in Annexes of COA Circular 2012-002.  Also, compliance to the guidelines on the utilization of the LDRRMF as prescribed under Republic Act 10121 should be strictly adhered to.

23.8.3 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose all the necessary information pertaining to LDRRMF. Henceforth, strictly comply with the disclosure requirements on LDRRMF as provided by COA Circular No. 2014-002.

24.6 We have recommended that Management, in cases of lump sum appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB appropriation ordinance/resolution be secured by the Municipal Mayor to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government in accordance to Section 22 (c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the afore-cited jurisprudence. To eliminate the need for a separate authorization and in order to expedite the implementation of future development projects, we strongly recommend that the PPAs to be included in the Annual Investment Plan be specifically identified and be fully described in detail in the AIP.

26.5 We have reiterated our recommendation that utilization of the 20% Development Fund should be limited to programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure that yields greater benefit to the people in accordance with DBM DILG Memorandum Circular 2017-1.

27.4 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant record these projects to their proper account.  Also, subsidiary ledgers should be maintained in order to easily account these assets in case of physical inventory.  We also propose the following journal entry:
Repairs and Maintenance – Infrastructure Assets	₱1,683,000.00
Information and Communication Technology Equipment	        96,000.00
Infrastructure Assets – Road Networks				₱1,683,000.00
Other Supplies and Materials Expenses				        96,000.00
To reclassify projects based on their nature.

28.12 We have reiterated our recommendation that the municipality’s GAD Focal Point prepare the annual and performance-based LGU GPB in response to the gender issues and or concerns of the locality and in the context of the LGU mandate; and consolidate the same following the form and procedures prescribed in the Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC).

29.4 We have recommended that the Municipality establish a GAD database to serve as basis for planning, programming and formulation of gender related issues or concerns before the GAD Plan is finalized and submitted for review to the Municipal Planning and Development Office.

30.10 We have reiterated our recommendation that the management
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases and contracts as mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid impositions of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue.
Minimize payments through cash or reimbursement in the procurement of goods and services.

We recommended that a) the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (i)  create  an
Inventory Committee; (ii) require each department head  to  fast  track  the
inventory taking of  all  properties  under  their  office  and  submit  the
inventory report to the committee for validation  and  verification  of  the
correctness and existence of the property and; (iii) in the  event  that  an
inventory committee is created, require it to (1)  check  the  condition  of
the property, and any damaged, obsolete and unserviceable property shall  be
reported; (2) prepare the Report on the Physical Count  of  Property,  Plant
and Equipment (RPCPPE) and; (3) immediately perform  the  required  disposal
of unserviceable  property  b)  the  GSO/Municipal  Treasurer  (i)  maintain
Property Cards per class and  category  of  property,  plant  and  equipment
indicating acquisition date, the brief descriptions; (ii) revisit   previous
years’ records and reconcile with the information at the accounting  office,
and (iii) update the property issue slip/PAR to  fix  the  custodianship  of
the  property;  and  c)  The  Municipal  Accountant  to  drop  the  cost  of
unserviceable and obsolete properties items from  the  books  following  the
prescribed process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor a.)  instruct  the  Municipal
Planning Development Officer, Budget Officer and the Accountant  to  adhere
strictly with the guidelines on the appropriation and  utilization  of  the
20% economic development fund prescribed under  Joint  Memorandum  Circular
No. 2017-1 of the DILG and  DBM;  and  b.)  utilize  the  fund  to  finance
priority development  projects  and  programs,  as  embodied  in  the  duly
approved Local Development plan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the  Head  of
LDRRMO (i) to monitor monthly utilization of LDRRMF and  corresponding
Accomplishment Report and (ii) to observe strictly the laws rules  and
regulations on the use of calamity fund to  ensure  that  the  desired
services and benefits can be delivered to the constituents in times of
calamities; (b) instruct the  Municipal  Accountant  to  (i)  prepare,
certify and submit monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF
in prescribed format under Annex E of COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommended that Management: (i) require  the  LSB  to  follow  and
perform the activities and preparation and approval of the SEF  Budget  with
indicative timelines as prescribed by DepEd- DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.  1,
s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 or the  Revised  Guidelines  on  the  Use  of
Special Education Fund (SEF); and (ii) require the Municipal  Accountant  to
prepare the Report of SEF  Utilization  quarterly  and  annually  using  the
prescribed from for submission to appropriate  offices.  (iii)  require  the
Local School Board coordinate with the Property Offices of both the LGU  and
the DepEd in order to strengthen the controls on property accountability  to
accurately monitor the custodianship of government property.

We  recommended  that  Management  allocate  the   mandatory   minimum
appropriation or funding for LCPC and instruct the council members  to  (i)
ensure the proper implementation of the formulated PPAs in accordance  with
the above law and regulations; and (ii) consider outsourcing or partnership
with  private  organizations  and  entities  that  provide  sponsorship  or
financial assistance for the implementation of LCPC PPAs.

While we took cognizance of the relentless  efforts  of  the  LGU  to
support gender equality and women  empowerment,  we  recommended  that  the
Executive Committee  and  the  Technical  Working  Group  (i)  Endeavor  to
capacitate themselves through attendance to trainings under  GFPS  for  more
responsive planning; and  (ii)  Update  the  GAD  Code,  if  necessary,  in
coordination with the Sanggunian and disseminate  the  same  in  accordance
with the guidelines provided under PCW Memorandum Circular No. 2009-1 dated
September 16, 2009.

We recommended that the  management  consider  the  negotiation  with
government bank to enroll its employees in  the  Automated  Teller  Machine
(ATM) payroll system in order to  lessen  the  risks  of  loss,  strengthen
controls in handling cash for payroll, and improve efficiency  in  treasury
operations.

We recommended that  the  LCE  prioritize  the  hiring  of  mandatory
positions to enhance its  services  in  attaining  full  implementation  of
programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the benefit
of the Municipality and its constituents. Further, personnel  movement  has
to  be  considered  in  order  to  acknowledge  the  exemplary  performance  of
employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby allowing the entry  of
new applicants for lower level positions.

We  recommended  that  Management  (i)  immediately  facilitate  the
transfer of ownership of these motor vehicles in favor of the  Municipality
in order to obtain the required government plates (red  plates)  and;  (ii)
initiate the application of renewals for registration in the  LTO  for  the
seven motor vehicles.

We  recommended  that  the  Management  require  the  Office  of  the
Accountant to submit the required financial records, reports and supporting
documents within the prescribed period.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief  Executive
(a) coordinate the efforts of the public and private sectors  and  implement
the  environmental  laws  on  preserving  and  protecting  the   environment
required  under  R.A.  No.  9003;  (b)   work  on   the   promulgation   and
implementation of “No Littering” Ordinance; (c) maximize  the  operation  of
the  MRF  and  implement  the  Municipality’s  plan  of  producing   organic
fertilizer and CHB Pavers  from  biodegradable  wastes  and  adopt  specific
revenue-generating measures to promote  and  ensure  the  viability  of  its
Solid Waste Management Plan; and (d) encourage  the  full  participation  of
the barangays in the implementation of the R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended that Municipal  Engineer  strictly  comply  with  the
provision cited under DPWH Department Order No. 197 series of 2016  in  the
preparation of ABC for infrastructure projects.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the  Bids  and  Awards
Committee to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of  RA  No.  9184
and its IRR in the procurement activities and submit immediately  the  above
required documents.

We recommended that  all  taxes  withheld  be  fully  remitted  intact
within the prescribed period.

We recommended the management to continuously remit the GSIS  premiums
within the prescribed period.

We recommended that HDMF premiums and  employees’  loan  amortizations
be deducted and fully remitted intact within the prescribed period.

We recommend that the Inventory Committee be required to conduct a complete physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality so that it can prepare and submit the required physical inventory reports to the Auditor and thereafter, regularly submit such reports to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.  Likewise, provide depreciation on those properties that were not depreciated.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant obtain the records from the Municipal Assessor, and immediately thereafter, record the unrecorded real properties by using its appraised value, if the acquisition costs are not known nor available.  We also recommend that the Municipal Assessor be directed to prioritize the titling of those untitled lands in order to establish absolute ownership thereof, and for the Municipal Budget Officer to consider the funding requirement for the titling of lands in the budget preparation for the ensuing year.

We recommend that management file the request for authority to write-off dormant/ accounts totaling P173,105.40 to the COA Audit Team pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.

We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) be required to strictly observe the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as provided for in DILG-DBM JMC no. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.  Likewise, maximize the utilization of appropriated fund in implementing the development projects indicated in the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommend that the SEF Budget be prepared by the District Supervisor and approved by the Local School Board (LSB), and duly supported with programs, projects, and activities that are aligned with Section 100(b) of RA 7160, and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017.  Limit the use of the SEF for purposes for which it was intended.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of the taxpayers, as well as its total amount due and collectible at the beginning of every year.  Accordingly, we also recommend that the Municipal Accountant record immediately the receivables as soon as the Municipal Treasurer provide such list.  Further, we require that the Municipal Assessor submit to the Municipal Treasurer on or before the 31st day of December each year, an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties.

We recommend that management submit at the beginning of the year a list of on-going and to be implemented projects/programs/activities (PPAs) for the current year, and inform COA within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices are already posted as required in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommend that Municipal Treasurer and Accountant strictly observe the timely submission of accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445.

Require the employees to refund immediately the unexpended balance of the cash advances and enforce the immediate liquidation of overdue advances by sending demand letters to the concerned otherwise, the Treasurer is advised to withhold or suspend the salaries of the erring accountable officers which had not settle their cash advances as of December 31, 2017 without prejudice to the other sanctions as provided for under the aforementioned regulations.
Strengthen measures to demand and assure the recovery of prior year cash advances made by officers and employees in and out of service.
Strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Cir. No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to gather all serviceable and inventoriable properties which were funded by the LGSF since the start of its implementation, and shall recognize them in the books to their proper asset accounts. The existing and the future projects funded by the same fund source shall always be properly recognized using the criteria provided in the prevailing accounting standards.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary correcting entries regarding the deficiencies noted and to affix her signature in every journal entries to testify the correctness thereof. Each transactions must be properly corresponded with the appropriate accounts to produce a more reliable and fairly presented financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to invite observer from duly recognized private group in all stages of the procurement process and to acquaint themselves to the prescribed period for every stages of the procurement process and to strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184.

We are reiterating our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant and other Head of Offices concerned to submit the accounts and other financial reports of the Municipality regularly, on every 10th day of the succeeding month.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to refrain from selling the bidding documents prior to the appointed time and to strictly adhere to the provisions of The Revised IRR of RA 9184, in the issuance of the bidding documents to the bidders to have equal access of information.
We recommended that the procuring entity should submit the lacking documents peculiar to the contract which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial and legal review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

We are reiterating our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same, so that the Management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the Chairman of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board shall provide for the schemes that would improve the dump site operation and prevent unwanted individuals to dump their garbage in the final disposal site. Further, segregation of wastes at source must be strongly implemented and the collection thereof must be properly handled in consonance with Article 2 and 3 of RA 9003. The board is also encouraged to convert its open dump site to a sanitary landfill as its final disposal of wastes and must include the required individuals to be members of the board.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to provide the necessary appropriation representing one percent (1%) of the IRA for programs, projects and activities for the protection of children and to submit the required documents.

To strictly observe the provisions of Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and the Policies and Guidelines of DILG MC 99-65; and
To refrain from using the appropriations for Peace and Order for purposes other than those authorized under Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and the Policies and Guidelines of DILG MC 99-65.

To strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 10121 in planning and implementation of the projects, programs and activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office; and
To refrain from using the LDRRMF for purposes other than those authorized under Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to review all disbursements for GSIS, HDMF and PhilHealth to make sure that the monthly contributions and loan amortizations of all employees have been withheld; that no more employees have outstanding balances from the previous years; and that all of the contributions and loan amortizations withheld were remitted. Prompt remittance of these monthly contributions must be observed, in consonance of the aforementioned laws.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Supply Officer to submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of government motor transportation for CY 2017 and hereafter, to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed for the period.

We recommended that Management direct those involved in planning and implementation to monitor closely and hasten the implementation of the projects. Likewise, optimally utilized the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development so that the intended beneficiaries can avail the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to withheld the amount of Php887,840.17 during the payment of the contract, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued on the same amount.  Moreover, instruct the Municipal Engineer, the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to meticulously review all the dsocuments and the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract before proceeding to the procurement process. Further, require the concerned officials to update themselves with the rules and regulations relative to the new issuances issued by the DPWH on the implementation of the Infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Local Sanggunian shall revisit the locally-enacted resolutions if the same are still relevant/reasonable nowadays considering the cheaper, prevailing rates offered by the mobile networks. Otherwise, the enjoined individuals should decrease the communication allowance to a more conscionable rate. A detailed report must also be submitted to justify the excess, indicating therein the breakdown of expenses covered by the monthly communication allowance given. The principle of judicious utilization of government funds as emphasized in Section 2 of PD 1445 must be considered at all times.

We recommended that Management direct those involved in planning and implementation to monitor closely and to hasten the implementation of these projects/activities funded from National Government Agencies especially those releases in earlier years, so that the constituents can benefit from said projects. Likewise, please submit justification on the non-utilization of the funds.  Return to source agency any unused balance of grants/fund transfer upon completion of the project.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management:

-Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to establish existence and determine the nature of the accounts.  Measures which include sending demand letters to debtors reminding them of their overdue obligations should be made;

-If the receivables would be found uncollectible and unrecoverable, consider requesting for its write-off pursuant to existing COA rules and regulations.  The request for authority to write-off the accounts outstanding for more than 10 years should be forwarded to the COA for evaluation as required by existing rules and regulations; and 

-Accounting unit should maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The management initiated the submission of the request for write off, however other documents cannot be provided.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that:

Management promptly conduct an inventory of its road network system and the GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and

The Municipal Accountant trace the recorded road, highways and bridges which were closed to government equity in the prior years and subsequently record in its books of accounts the amount of road works equivalent to the required transition provision.

We reiterated our recommendation to the management that cash advances be supported with estimate/breakdown of expenses to be paid, in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and Annex B of COA Circular 2012-03.  Also, we reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer should be granted cash advances only for salaries, wages, honorarium and petty operating expenses.

We reiterated our recommendation to the management to instruct the treasurer to pay disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00 directly to the payee through checks in compliance with Sections 2.2 and 4.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We have recommended that management instruct the accountant to submit the Approved DTR, Letter of authorization, and other documents that supports the payment of salaries and wages of regular employees pursuant to PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012 – 001.

Job Orders

We have recommended the management to instruct the accountant to submit the Approved DTR, Letter of authorization, Accomplishment Reports, Sangguniang Bayan Resolution, and other documents that supports the payment of salaries and wages of casual employees in compliance with the above provisions.

Other Professional Services

We have recommended that the management instruct the accountant to submit the complete documentary requirements of paid Day Care Workers and the Doctor to the Barrios.  The hiring of these personnel shall likewise be authorized through a Contract of Service pursuant to existing rules and regulations.

Gasoline, Oil and Fuel Consumption

We have recommended that the respective personnel in charge of the subject disbursements/activities submit the required documents.

Repairs and maintenance of Motor Vehicle/Construction and Heavy Equipment

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer verify whether all the necessary supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before the processing of the claims. Henceforth, all disbursements not supported with the required supporting documents should not be process and should be returned to the respective employee/personnel.
Supplies and Materials Expenses -₱1,955,373.83

We have recommended that management: 

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary supporting documents for evaluation in compliance with the above stated regulations;

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record purchases of supplies and materials to their respective inventory asset account and record these to their respective expenditure account upon issuance.  All issuances should be supported with Requisition Issue Slips (RIS) and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) should be prepared and submitted to COA to support charges made to expenditure accounts; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Perpetual Records /Supplies Ledger Cards per stock as provided by NGAS to ensure fair presentation of inventory accounts in the financial statements. The Supply or Property Unit or the Municipal Treasurer as the case maybe, should maintain Stock Cards to record all receipts and issuances.

Meals and Snacks - ₱1,703,490.00

We have recommended that management:

Instruct the concerned offices to submit necessary documents to prove the validity of claims for meals and snacks in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001;

Instruct the department heads to make purchases to registered suppliers and that payments made to them should be subjected to applicable withholding taxes in compliance with existing revenue regulations; and

Procurements exceeding the thresholds for shopping and/or small value procurements shall be done through public bidding in compliance with RA 9184.

Aid/Cash Donations to PNP - ₱195,000.00

We have recommended that the management submit to COA the necessary documents for evaluation in order to establish the validity of the financial assistance given to PNP.  Also, explain the purpose of the financial assistance in order to ascertain the proper classification in the books of the agency.

We have recommended that all Memorandum of Agreement entered into by the municipality be submitted to the audit team to verify if the specific purpose for which the funds was established was met and to determine whether the terms and conditions were adhered to by the parties.

We have recommended that the management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record these projects to their proper account.  Also, subsidiary ledgers should be maintained in order to easily account these assets in case of physical inventory.  In line to that, we propose the following journal entry:

Infrastructure Assets – Road Networks		         ₱3,528,900.47
Infrastructure Assets – Flood Control Systems			2,475,000.00
Infrastructure Assets – Water Supply Systems			718,185.95
Repairs and Maintenance – Infrastructure Assets			₱6,722,086.42
To record transfer of capitalized Infrastructure assets to their proper account.
observations.managementAction: Adjusted.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted a certificate of review by the DILG Provincial Focal Point Person.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the  accountable officers the immediate   settlement of outstanding cash       advances for fair presentation of the    affected accounts in the financial   statements which had remained        unliquidated as of December 31, 2016 otherwise cause the withholding of   salaries of accountable officers     concerned.

We recommended and management agreed that the Petty Cash Custodian or     Municipal Treasurer (a)  prepare     report of disbursement to support the  replenishment/liquidation of PCF; (b)  secure an approval of the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution first before        establishment of PCF or increase of  amount thereof; and (c) avoid using  PCF for regular operating and        maintenance expenses except in       emergency cases.
observations.managementAction: Not

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the  designated Property Officer and the  Municipal Accountant to prepare and  maintain reports/property records    such as RPCPPE and Property Cards for  proper monitoring of properties      purchased, donated, transferred or     disposed so that reconciliation of     accounting records with physical       count will be made. Likewise,        facilitate the conduct of complete     physical count of all the properties   of the Municipality with the Auditor  as witness.
observations.managementAction: Not

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that Municipal Mayor and other      officials stop the practice of       utilizing 20% Development Fund to other  purposes for which the fund was      created. We further recommended that  the preparation of the Annual        Investment Plan be compliant with the  provisions enumerated in DBM-DILG  Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1  and ensure the utilization of the      Fund in accordance with the plan.

No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: Partially

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Local School Board (LSB)  strictly comply with the provisions of  R.A. No. 5447, R.A. No. 7160, LOI     1462 and relevant DepEd-DBM-DILG     Joint Circulars in the preparation of  the SEF Annual Budget and identify the  priority expenditures pursuant thereto   treating on the utilization of the      Special Education Fund to avoid       disallowances in audit.

We recommended that management comply with the above cited provisions of   R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR and COA      Circular No. 2012-001. In addition, the  management is required to submit the  lacking documents/justifications noted  to prove the legality and validity of   the procurement of infrastructure.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the municipal general services  officer in close coordination with   the Inventory Committee, expedite the  disposal of its unserviceable motor     vehicles not only to ascertain their   actual conditions but most             importantly to determine the         appraised realizable value which are  prerequisites to final disposal and      require the submission of the Report   of Unserviceable Property, in         accordance with Section 79 of PD     1445. Likewise, to avoid their        further deterioration especially       those exposed to the elements of     nature and thereby realizing higher   proceeds from the disposal.
observations.managementAction: Not

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor and       Municipal Accountant comply with COA-  DBM-DILG-GCG-DND JC No. 2015-01      prescribing the guidelines on the      entitlement, release, use, reporting   and audit of the confidential and/or   intelligence funds.

We recommended and management agreed that the provisions of the Revised   Implementing Rules and Regulations of  the Republic Act No. 9184, or the Act   providing for the Modernization,       Standardization and Regulation of      Procurement Activities of the        Government be strictly adhered to by  the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Partially

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant require  the Supplier/Contractor to post the   necessary warranty security in any     acceptable form for goods delivered    and infrastructures implemented as     prescribed under Section 62 of the    IRR-A of RA No. 9184 before final     payment is made in order that the     Municipality is assured of             compensation in case of faulty or      defective projects.
observations.managementAction: Partially

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the  Municipal Treasurer and Budget      Officer to insure all insurable        properties with the General Insurance  Fund as required by R.A. 656 and     Administrative Order No. 33.
observations.managementAction: Not

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and the  Municipal Treasurer (a) strictly     comply with the regulation on the     rendition of accounts and financial    reports in order not to delay the      verification and post-audit          activities; and (b) observe the due    dates of submission of the required   financial reports and documents to     the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Not

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned LGU officials comply with the        provision of COA Circular No. 2009-001  to avoid suspension of the           transactions and the penalty          prescribed by law under 4.1 of this    Circular.
observations.managementAction: Partially

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

Henceforth, the Inventory Committee undertake an annual inventory of all insurable property of the agency and submit the report to GSIS and furnish a copy to the COA Auditor on or before October 31 of ensuing year.

We recommend that:

 The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the former Municipal Accountant and the Accounting Personnel in-charge of recording in the books, in tracing the original accounting entries made and if warranted, prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries, to de-recognize the accounts from the books.

Management, through the Municipal Accountant, strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommend that:

The Municipal Accountant review the transactions and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Necessary adjusting entries to be done during year end Closing of Books and Accounts

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records as basis in preparing adjusting journal entries.

The Municipal Accountant make an adjusting journal entry to restore the amount of accountable forms written off amounting to P146,574.00, if warranted, after the reconciliation.

We recommend that:

The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Local Accountant a copy of the Inventory and the Statement of Accountability for Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers at the end of the month and at year-end.

The Municipal Accountant record properly in the books the procurement and issuance of all accountable forms.

The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant coordinate with each other to reconcile their records on all accountable forms.

We recommend that:

Management, through the Municipal Treasurer, General Services Officer, and LDRRMC insure yearly with the GSIS the LGU’s insurable properties based on the annual inventory of insurable properties, using the unexpended/unobligated QRF and DRRMF-MOOE funds.

Management appropriate in its budget for the payment of premiums for adequate insurance coverage with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS of the LGU’s insurable assets.

We recommend that, management, through the LDRRMO, implement immediately the projects indicated in the LDRRMFIP, as preventive measure to secure the constituents in times of emergencies caused by disasters.

We recommend that management:

Send immediately at least three (3) consecutive demand letters to the concerned Accountable Officers whose whereabouts are known, including those who are still in service, and furnish the COA Auditor with certified copies duly received by the Accountable Officers.

b.	File the appropriate charges against those erring Accountable Officers in item (a) who failed to liquidate the cash advances despite the 3 demand letters and furnish the COA Auditor with certified copies.

c.    Exhaust all possible means to find the addresses of those whose whereabouts are unknown and update immediately the COA Auditor with a written report on the actions taken to locate them.	
d.	Send at least three (3) consecutive demand letters to those Accountable Officers in item (c) once their addresses are obtained, and furnish the COA Auditor with copies duly- received by the Accountable Officers.

e.	File the appropriate charges against those erring Accountable Officers in item (c) who failed to liquidate the cash advances despite the 3 demand letters and furnish the COA Auditor with certified copies.
observations.reason: Not  all AOs were given demand letters

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

a. 	Send demand letters monthly to all Accountable Officers requiring the full settlement of unliquidated cash advances and furnish the COA Auditor with certified copies duly received by the Accountable Officers.

Suspend the salary of those erring Accountable Officers in item (a) who failed to liquidate the cash advances despite at least 3 written demand until such time that they could have fully settled their cash advances, and furnish the COA Auditor with certified copies of order of suspension.

Submit to COA Auditor every 10th day of the ensuing month the Monitoring of All Cash Advances as of the end of each month.
observations.reason: Not  all AOs were given demand letters

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management: 

a. Require the Disbursing Officer-Designate to update completely her cashbook.

Relieve the Disbursing Officer-Designate of her other assignments for her to be able to personally discharge her functions as Disbursing Officer.

We recommend that management: 

a. 	 Require the Municipal Accountant to enlighten those personnel concerned with the rules and regulations on cash advances and henceforth, require them to strictly observe the provisions on the liquidation of cash advance in items 5.1.3, 5.2 and 5.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. 

b.  	Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salary of those with unsettled cash advances for travel beyond the prescribed period until such time that they have fully settled their cash advance and refrain from granting new cash advances to those with unliquidated balances.

c. 	Require the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters monthly to all agency officials and personnel with unliquidated cash advances for local travel which are unliquidated beyond the prescribed 30-day period and furnish the COA Auditor with a copy thereof.
observations.reason: Not  all AOs were given demand letters

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management: 

a. 	Maintain adequate internal control on cash by providing the Disbursing Officer-Designate with a safe to keep her cash advances and checks for cash advances granted to her.

b. 	 Create a team to conduct an inventory of the contents of the safety vault in the custody of the former Disbursing Officer-Designate in the presence of the latter so that the safety vault can be used by the present Disbursing Officer-Designate.
observations.managementAction: Safety vault was purchased for exclusive use of the DO

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

Management pay the salaries, wages, and other monetary benefits of the agency’s elective officials, regular, casual, contractual and job order personnel through Automated Teller Machine (ATM) instead of cash.

Management coordinate with nearby agencies and with the AGDB for the installation of ATMs nearby.

observations.recommendation:

Management immediately procure a Biometric Finger scanner to be immediately procured and be made available in the different agency offices, and pending its successful installation, to make available a functioning electronic Bundy clock in each Office for agency personnel’s use.

Management, through the HRMO, devise a system to protect the Biometric Finger scanner or electronic Bundy clock against loss or damages due to improper use.
 
 All officers and employees including department heads and Job Order personnel keep a daily record of attendance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

 The GAD Focal Point System meet monthly in order to update the GAD Fund utilization and accomplishment.

Management strictly adhere with Section 36 of R.A. No. 9710 and Section 2.3 of the Joint Circular No. 2012-01 of the PCW, NEDA, and DBM and other rules and regulations on GAD.

We recommend that management:

a.  	Exercise prudence in the disbursement of government funds.

b.  Require the concerned agency officials and personnel to familiarize with COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 in order to guide them on the different types of irregular, unnecessary, extravagant, excessive and unconscionable expenditures.

c.  	Require the concerned participants to refund the transportation expenses and cost of polo shirt aggregating P4,600.00 per participant.
We recommend that management strictly adhere to the ceiling set in and the other provisions of the DBM National Budget Circulars and other related rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

 The Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all collections to authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.




Management assign additional personnel to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommend that the Collectors/Tellers turn over their collections daily to the Liquidating Officer in strict adherence to Sections 60(A) and 61(B) Of the Local Treasury Operations Manual and other pertinent rules and regulations.

We recommend that management: 

a. 	Reduce the Petty Cash Fund from P50,000.00 to P5,000.00 or P10,000.00.


b.       Require the PCF Custodian to keep the Petty Cash Fund and to personally disburse the fund.
We recommend that the individual Collectors/Tellers maintain and update the Cashbook – Teller/Collector (LTO Form 9) prescribed in Section 63(F) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommend that:

The Municipal Accountant update the subsidiary accounting records and furnish the Municipal Treasurer with a certified copy thereof at the end of each month.

The Municipal Treasurer reconcile his cashbook balances with the subsidiary accounting records every end of the month in strict adherence to the provisions of Section 63 (F) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Auditor the Reconciliation Statements monthly.

We recommend that:

Henceforth, management strictly adhere to the provisions of the annual General Appropriations Act on the minimum monthly net take-home pay.

Management require the concerned officials involved in approving personal loans to control the loan amount that an employee could avail of by considering the net monthly net take-home pay.

Management require the personnel in charge of preparing the payrolls and disbursement vouchers for the payment of claims for Personnel Services to use as guide the provisions of the annual General Appropriations Act in prioritizing the authorized deductions that can be charged against the employees’ claims for Personnel Services, subject to the monthly net take-home pay limitation.

Management, through the Office of the Municipal Accountant, inform in writing the concerned employees on authorized and other deductions that can no longer be deducted from their salaries due to monthly net take-home pay limitation.

We recommend that:

The Municipal Treasurer submit to the Auditor on the fifth day of the following month the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and the supporting Reports of Accountability for Accountable Forms.

The Municipal Treasurer include all accountable forms including checks in the RAAF and CRAAF.

Management assign additional competent personnel to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer, or capacitate the existing incompetent personnel through training and monitoring with closer supervision.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer did not submit the RAAF and CRAAF and supporting reports of accountability for Accountable Forms to the Auditor on the 5th day of the following month

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Record all purchases of supplies and materials in an Inventory Account both in the Accounting Office and in the General Services Office. As such, the General Services Office is responsible in the safe keeping and issuance of these supplies and materials by requiring the end-users to fill up a Requisition and Issue Slip duly approved by the approving official, as well as the preparation and maintenance of a Supplies Ledger Card for each type of supplies.
 
Enjoin all department heads to submit to the General Services Office their usage of the various supplies and materials thru the Requisition and Issue Slip, so that the latter can prepare the required Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued monthly which will become the basis for recording the expenses in the books and direct the General Services Office to properly maintain the supplies cards/stock cards.

Require the Inventory Team to conduct Inventory Supplies and Materials semestrally and to submit the corresponding report using the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories.   Instruct the Accounting Office to regularly reconcile this Inventory Report submitted by the General Services Office as against their own record and to verify discrepancies in the process and to adjust records accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Instructed Accounting office and GSO to record all purchases of materials and supplies in an Inventory account and maintain a Supplies Ledger card
observations.reason:
Direct expense method is used. 
GSO still did not maintain the Supplies Ledger cards and no stock room is available for the storage of their purchases.

Department heads did not submit to GSO their usage of various supplies and materials.

No inventory conducted for supplies and materials because these are immediately released to the requisitioning department/
office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend Management to:

a) 	Require the employees to refund immediately the unexpended balance of the cash advances and enforce the immediate liquidation of overdue advances by sending demand letters to the concerned otherwise, the Treasurer is advised to withhold or suspend the salaries of the erring accountable officers which had not settle their cash advances as of December 31, 2016 without prejudice to the other sanctions as provided for under the aforementioned regulations.

b) 	Strengthen measures to demand and assure the recovery of prior year cash advances made by officers and employees in and out of service.

c) 	Strictly adhere with the provisions of Sec. 89 PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 97-002 and EO No. 298 and other rulings mentioned above.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum Order has been issued to all accountable officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances and settle their financial accountabilities with the local government. Every one of them were called personally by the Municipal Accountant to help them settle their cash advances. Others were able to settle but some were not. We will strengthen our measures to demand and assure the recovery of prior years' cash advances made by officials and employees in and out of service. Demand letters were issued and withholding of their salaries/wages and other emoluments were instituted. We will look into writing off other accounts.
observations.reason: It has been noted for the current year that Management has a decrease accumulation of cash advances. However, cash advances from prior years are barely liquidated

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same, so that the Management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum Order has been issued to the BAC Chairman and BAC Secretariat to strictly comply the timely submission of copies of government contracts and its supporting documents.
observations.reason:
Not all contracts were submitted to COA. 
There is still delays in submission of contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Technical Working Group (TWG) to strictly follow the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184, and disqualify participating bidder with incomplete documents/requirement. Likewise, prices of Bid documents should be uniformed to be fair with other participating bidders.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum Order has been issued to cause compliance to the provisions of the Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.reason: There are still procedural lapses in the procurement process of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management ensure that procurement of goods and services should be undertaken in pursuant to Rule IV, Section 10 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in order to have a wide dissemination of bid opportunities and participation of different private contracting parties and to obtain the most advantageous price. Likewise, indicate in the Attendance Sheet the Designation/Position of the attendees in order to determine if they are the official participants of the said activity.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum Order has been issued to cause compliance to Rule IV, Section 10 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.  We already indicated the designation/position of the attendees in the Attendance Sheet.
observations.reason: Several procurement transactions are still not done in accordance with RA 9184. Most of the procurements are through Cash Advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:

a) 	To strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 4 (6) of PD 1445. Moreover, cause the immediate submission of the documentary deficiencies and the lacking details of the transactions.

b) 	To strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 10121 in planning and implementation of the projects, programs and activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.

c) 	To submit to the auditors updated list of serviceable vehicles of the Municipality with their corresponding plate numbers for verification of fuel consumption and validation purposes.

d) 	To exercise due diligence in the preparation of required documents to support government transactions to ensure accuracy, completeness and reliability of the same.
observations.managementAction: A memorandum had been issued to MDRRMO Head to observe Disaster Risk Reduction Management provisions and cause compliance thereto as well as the preparation of payments and its supporting documents forming part of the transactions should be done accordingly with the General Services/Procurement Unit In-charge.
observations.reason: Nonpriority expenditures are charged against the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to follow the provisions stated in Joint Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 8, 2015, to ensure the submission on time the documentary requirements needed in the granting of cash advances for Confidential Fund.
observations.managementAction: This was already settled. Disbursement Voucher for the payment of Cash Advance for the 4th Quarter of CY 2016 Confidential Fund was submitted to COA within 7 days for post audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management should:

•	Formulate a policy on the granting of financial assistance systematically determine the extent of aid to be given to individuals in need. Beneficiaries should be properly screened giving priority to the less privileged constituents.

•	Coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to assess request for medical and hospitalization assistance by way of Social Case Study Reports so
observations.managementAction: MSWDO were already instructed to completely require eligible clients to submit supporting documents and to assess client's eligibility guided by the DSWD Memo Circular No. 04 Series of 2015 and Section 5 (6) of PD 1445. Rest assured that compliance to the Departments' Circular will be strictly observed.
observations.reason: Still no systematic determination on the granting of financial assistance.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommend that Management follow the limitations imposed under COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and CSC and DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2015 dated November 25, 2015. Likewise, instruct the concerned officials to refund the excessive/unnecessary payments, otherwise, Notices of Disallowance will be issued on the same.
observations.managementAction: Cash refund/cash return will start this August 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board:

a)	prepare a budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and

b)	request for inputs from the DepEd officials/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum Order has been issued to the Local School Board to observe compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to review the granting of cash advances intended for the payment of payroll for salaries and wages before the issuance of check and follow strictly the provision of Section 4.2.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

Starting September 2016, we already stop granting cash advances for salaries and wages of more than the net amount of the payroll.
observations.reason: Not all payrolls were checked/verified.

observations.recommendation: We recommend therefore that Management follow the provisions stated in Section 34.8 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and to review the post-qualification report prepared by the BAC TWG.

observations.managementAction: A directive has already been given to the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports together with the supporting documents within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommend that the Management observe the prudent use of scarce resources and follow the limitations imposed under COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and DILG Memorandum Circular No.2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 in order to minimize the cost of the conduct of these activities and save funds for other relative development projects. Travelling expenses which are found to be excessive and extravagant shall be disallowed in audit.

Moreover, follow strictly the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement which exceeds the threshold for shopping and small value item of Php100,000.00, should be done thru public bidding to be competitive and to obtain the most advantageous price.
observations.managementAction: We requested an authority from the DILG Regional Director Lilibeth A. Famacion, CESO III to conduct our CDP & ELA Formulation Workshops cum 2017 Budget Preparation outside our territorial jurisdiction. According to her, authority is no longer necessary because it is within the purview of the Capability Development Agenda of the LGU. But with this observation, rest assured that we will observe prudent use of scarce resources and follow the limitations imposed in order to minimize the cost of the conduct of any succeeding activities.
observations.reason: Notice of Disallowance was issued.
