observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our recommendation that the management create an
inventory committee to conduct a complete physical inventory taking of
property to establish the existence and actual condition of the assets of the
agency. Also, the Municipal Accountant is advised to maintain PPELC to be
reconciled with the balances of PPE per General Ledger and Property Cards
to be maintained by the Property Custodian.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management create an inventory
committee to conduct a complete physical inventory taking of property to
establish the existence and actual condition of the assets of the agency. Also,
the Municipal Accountant is advised to maintain PPELC to be reconciled
with the balances of PPE per General Ledger and Property Cards to be
maintained by the Property Custodian.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management:

a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to establish existence and
determine the nature of the accounts. Measures which include sending demand
letters to debtors reminding them of their overdue obligations should be made;

b. If the receivables would be found uncollectible and unrecoverable, consider
requesting for its write-off pursuant to existing COA rules and regulations. The
request for authority to write-off the accounts outstanding for more than 10
years should be forwarded to the COA for evaluation as required by existing rules and
regulations; and

c. Accounting unit should maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for
Receivables.

We have recommended that:

Management promptly conduct an inventory of its road network system and the
GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and

The Municipal Accountant trace the recorded road, highways and bridges which
were closed to government equity in the prior years and subsequently record in
its books of accounts the amount of road works equivalent to the required
transition provision.

We reiterated our recommendation to the management that cash advances be
supported with estimate/breakdown of expenses to be paid, in accordance with
Section 89 of PD 1445 and Annex B of COA Circular 2012-03. Also, we
reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer should be granted
cash advances only for salaries, wages, honorarium and petty operating
expenses.

We reiterated our recommendation to the management to instruct the
treasurer to pay disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00 directly to the payee
through checks in compliance with Sections 2.2 and 4.3.2 of COA Circular No.
97-002.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer
to deposit all collections regularly and to deposit the balance of ₱147,682.34 as of
December 31, 2015and henceforth abide by the rules and regulations on deposit of
collections pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS, Section 111 of GAAM
Vol. 1 and DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81.

We have recommended that management instruct the accountant to submit the
Approved DTR, Letter of authorization, and other documents that supports
the payment of salaries and wages of regular employees pursuant to PD 1445
and COA Circular 2012-001.

We have recommended the management to instruct the accountant to submit
the Approved DTR, Letter of authorization, Accomplishment Reports,
Sangguniang Bayan Resolution, and other documents that supports the
payment of salaries and wages of casual employees in compliance with the
above provisions.

We have recommended that the management instruct the accountant to submit
the complete documentary requirements of paid Day Care Workers and the
Doctor to the Barrios. The hiring of these personnel shall likewise be
authorized through a Contract of Service pursuant to existing rules and
regulations.

We have recommended that the respective personnel in charge of the subject
disbursements/activities submit the required documents.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer verify whether all the necessary supporting documents are attached
to the disbursement vouchers before the processing of the claims. Henceforth,
all disbursements not supported with the required supporting documents
should not be process and should be returned to the respective
employee/personnel.

We have recommended that management:

a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary supporting
documents for evaluation in compliance with the above stated regulations;

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record purchases of supplies and
materials to their respective inventory asset account and record these to
their respective expenditure account upon issuance. All issuances should
be supported with Requisition Issue Slips (RIS) and Summary of Supplies
and Materials Issued (SSMI) should be prepared and submitted to COA to
support charges made to expenditure accounts; and

c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Perpetual Records
/Supplies Ledger Cards per stock as provided by NGAS to ensure fair
presentation of inventory accounts in the financial statements. The Supply
or Property Unit or the Municipal Treasurer as the case maybe, should
maintain Stock Cards to record all receipts and issuances.

We have recommended that management:

a. Instruct the concerned offices to submit necessary documents to prove the
validity of claims for meals and snacks in compliance with Section 4(6) of
PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001;

b. Instruct the department heads to make purchases to registered suppliers
and that payments made to them should be subjected to applicable
withholding taxes in compliance with existing revenue regulations; and

c. Procurements exceeding the thresholds for shopping and/or small value
procurements shall be done through public bidding in compliance with RA
9184.

We have recommended that the management submit to COA the necessary
documents for evaluation in order to establish the validity of the financial
assistance given to PNP. Also, explain the purpose of the financial assistance
in order to ascertain the proper classification in the books of the agency.

We recommend that all Memorandum of Agreement entered into by the municipality be submitted to the audit team to verify if the specific purpose for
which the funds was established was met and to determine whether the terms and
conditions were adhered to by the parties.

Copy of the contract documents shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor
within five (5) working days from issuance thereof and it shall be in the standard
form as prescribed by GPPB and as required under COA Circular 2009-001 for
auditorial and technical review; and

BAC should submit written justification/s for non-adherence to provisions of
R.A. No. 9184.

We have recommended that management, especially the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC), explain the reasons for not abiding with the rules on procurement. Purchases
beyond the threshold on shopping and small value items should be done through
competitive bidding as mandated by RA 9184. In all instances, ensure that the most
advantageous price for the Government of the Philippines is obtained.

We have recommended that progress payments should be subjected to the ten
percent (10%) retention or substituted with other allowable form of guarantees to
protect the interest of the municipality in case of defects in implemented projects or
from third party liabilities.

We have reiterated our recommendation to management:

3 that the Municipal Engineer verify the reported accomplishments before
certifying the Statement of Work Accomplished submitted by the contractor. He
shall measure the value of the works actually in place in accordance with the
contract; and

3 Municipal Officials should oblige the contractors to implement the projects
otherwise, the payments will become the personal liability of the municipal
officials who certified the correctness and validity of the claims.

We have recommended the management to included PCCP in the program of
works if additional funds could have been obtained, also implement approved
projects in accordance with the objectives of the fund stated in RA 7171.

We have reiterated our recommendation that an Investment Plan for the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund be prepared and utilized with the
prescribed format presented in Annexes of COA Circular 2012-002. Also,
compliance to the guidelines on the utilization of the LDRRMF as prescribed
under Republic Act 10121 should be strictly adhered to.

We have recommended that the management follow the programmed timelines for
period of implementation in the AIP to ensure the full implementation of the
development projects. Project implementation schedule shall be made in order to
effectively implement and monitor all the projects of the municipality.

We have recommended that the management amend or update the Annual
Investment Plan in case a realignment/supplemental of appropriation is made.
The AIP must contain specific projects in accordance with DBM DILG
Memorandum Circular 2011-1.

Wehave reiterated our recommendation that utilization of the 20% Development
Fund be limited to programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the
attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management
outcome and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure and
yields greater benefit to the people in accordance with DBM DILG Memorandum
Circular 2011-1.

We have recommended that the management instruct the Municipal Accountant
to record these projects to their proper account. Also, subsidiary ledgers should
be maintained in order to easily account these assets in case of physical inventory.
In line to that, we propose the following journal entry:

Infrastructure Assets – Road Networks ₱3,528,900.47
Infrastructure Assets – Flood Control Systems 2,475,000.00
Infrastructure Assets – Water Supply Systems 718,185.95
Repairs and Maintenance – Infrastructure Assets ₱6,722,086.42
To record transfer of capitalized Infrastructure assets to their proper account.

We have recommended that the management instruct the Municipal
Treasurer/Accountant to immediately submit all the necessary documents to
support the payment of development projects. Henceforth, strictly adhere with
the policies and procedures on the documentary requirements for all
disbursements/purchases before payments are made.

We have recommended that the municipality’s GAD Focal Point person should
prepare the annual and performance-based LGU GPB in response to the gender
issues and or concerns of the locality and in the context of the LGU mandate and
consolidate the same following the form and procedures prescribed in the Joint
Memorandum Circular (JMC).

We reiterate our recommendation that the management:

a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deduct, withhold and remit the
corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases and contracts as
mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid impositions of sanctions by
the Bureau of Internal Revenue;

b. Prevent/minimize paying/disbursing through cash or reimbursement basis in
the procurement of goods and services;

c. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to continue to locate the
documents in order to support validity of the long outstanding balance of Due
to BIR account;

d. If documents are found, remit the taxes immediately. If not, make a
representation with the BIR on how to settle the account; and

e. Always withhold and remit taxes on time to prevent penalties and sanctions, in
compliance with the above-stated revenue regulations and other relevant
provisions.

1.8	We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the accountable officers the immediate settlement of outstanding cash advances for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements which had remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2016 otherwise cause the withholding of salaries of accountable officers concerned.

We recommended and management agreed that the Petty Cash Custodian or Municipal Treasurer (a)  prepare report of disbursement to support the replenishment/liquidation of PCF; (b) secure an approval of the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution first before establishment of PCF or increase of amount thereof; and (c) avoid using PCF for regular operating and maintenance expenses except in emergency cases.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the designated Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain reports/property records such as RPCPPE and Property Cards for proper monitoring of properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed so that reconciliation of accounting records with physical count will be made. Likewise, facilitate the conduct of complete physical count of all the properties of the Municipality with the Auditor as witness.

We recommended and management agreed that Municipal Mayor and other officials stop the practice of utilizing 20% Development Fund to other purposes for which the fund was created. We further recommended that the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan be compliant with the provisions enumerated in DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 and ensure the utilization of the Fund in accordance with the plan.

We recommended and management agreed with the following:
Observe strictly the laws rules and regulations on the use of calamity fund to ensure that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to the constituents in times of calamities;
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to make thorough examination of the transaction prior to approval of payment of t-shirts, uniforms and water dispenser to avoid disallowance of the same; and
Instruct the Municipal Deputized DRRMO to utilize the LDRRMF only for the specific purpose for which it has been appropriated, particularly programs and activities that support pre-disaster and disaster risk management as mandated under R.A. No. 10121.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local School Board (LSB) strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 5447, R.A. No. 7160, LOI 1462 and relevant DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto treating on the utilization of the Special Education Fund to avoid disallowances in audit.

We recommended that management comply with the above cited provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR and COA Circular No. 2012-001. In addition, the management is required to submit the lacking documents/justifications noted to prove the legality and validity of the procurement of infrastructure.
The management agreed to comply and submit the required documents and justification for the issuance of Notice of Award within the prescribed period.

We recommended and management agreed that the municipal general services officer in close coordination with the Inventory Committee, expedite the disposal of its unserviceable motor vehicles not only to ascertain their actual conditions but most importantly to determine the appraised realizable value which are prerequisites to final disposal and require the submission of the Report of Unserviceable Property, in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445. Likewise, to avoid their further deterioration especially those exposed to the elements of nature and thereby realizing higher proceeds from the disposal.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant comply with COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND JC No. 2015-01 prescribing the guidelines on the entitlement, release, use, reporting and audit of the confidential and/or intelligence funds.

We recommended and management agreed that the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184, or the Act providing for the Modernization, Standardization and Regulation of Procurement Activities of the Government be strictly adhered to by the Municipality.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant require the Supplier/Contractor to post the necessary warranty security in any acceptable form for goods delivered and infrastructures implemented as prescribed under Section 62 of the IRR-A of RA No. 9184 before final payment is made in order that the Municipality is assured of compensation in case of faulty or defective projects.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Budget Officer to insure all insurable properties with the General Insurance Fund as required by R.A. 656 and Administrative Order No. 33.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the concerned LGU officials comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-001 to avoid suspension of the transactions and the penalty prescribed by law under 4.1 of this Circular.
The management will be using checklist to comply for the submission of approved contracts and purchase orders within the prescribed period.

While we took cognizance of the relentless efforts of the LGU to support gender equality and women empowerment, we recommended that the Executive Committee and the Technical Working Group:
Endeavor to capacitate themselves through attendance to trainings under GFPS for  more responsive planning;
Facilitate the establishment of the GAD Database to aid in gender analysis and provide baseline data on the status of women and men in the LGU; and
Assess the extent of compliance through a compliance checklist of the elements of the institutional mechanisms in place and intensify efforts on those areas that should be in place.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) coordinate the efforts of the public and private sectors and implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; (b)  work on the promulgation and implementation of “No Littering” Ordinance; (c) maximize the operation of the MRF and implement the Municipality’s plan of producing organic fertilizer and CHB Pavers from biodegradable wastes and adopt specific revenue-generating measures to promote and ensure the viability of its Solid Waste Management Plan; and (d) encourage the full participation of the barangays in the implementation of the R.A. No. 9003.
We recommended that management continue remitting the GSIS contributions on time.
We recommended that management continue remitting the taxes withheld as required by the BIR Revenue Regulations.

We recommend that:

a. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer immediately
transfer the unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and
DRRMF-MOOE in CY 2016 totaling P1,722,555.22 from the General
Fund to Trust Fund Books.

b. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer, LDRRMO and the Municipal
Accountant coordinate with each other in updating monthly the
LDRRMF utilization in order to determine and transfer the
unexpended/unobligated balances of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE from
the General Fund to the Trust Fund Books on or before the last
banking day of the current year.

This observation does not require the issuance of Notice of Suspension,
Notice of Disallowance, or Notice of Charge, as this pertains to deficiency in the
accounting system and cash management.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant correct the books of
accounts by making an adjusting journal entry in the General Fund books as
follows:

Accounts Debit Credit
Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Fund 1,055,070.84

To correct the erroneous entries made for LDDRMF-CO balances in CY
2014 and CY 2015

This observation does not require the issuance of Notice of Suspension,
Notice of Disallowance, or Notice of Charge, as this pertains to deficiency in the
accounting system.

We recommend that:

a. The Municipal Accountant correct the books of accounts by making
the following adjusting journal entries in the agency books:

b. Management submit to Caloocan City and other Grantor LGUs a
report on the utilization of the funds received in strict adherence to
Item 5.2.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and
furnish the Auditor a copy of the report.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of
Disallowance, if warranted, upon post-audit of subject transactions.

We recommend that:

a. Management pay the salaries, wages, and other monetary benefits of
the agency’s elective officials, regular, casual, contractual and job
order personnel through Automated Teller Machine (ATM) instead of
cash.

b. Management coordinate with nearby agencies and with the AGDB for
the installation of ATMs nearby.

This observation does not require the issuance of Notice of Suspension, Notice
of Disallowance, or Notice of Charge, as this pertains to deficiency in the internal
control system over human resources and cash.

We recommend that:

a. The Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all collections to authorized
depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.

b. Management assign additional personnel to the Office of the
Municipal Treasurer.

This observation does not require the issuance of Notice of Suspension, Notice
of Disallowance or Notice of Charge as this pertains to deficiency in the timeliness in
the deposit of collections.

We recommend that the Collectors/Tellers turn over their collections daily
to the Liquidating Officer in strict adherence to Sections 60(A) and 61(B) Of the
Local Treasury Operations Manual and other pertinent rules and regulations.

This observation does not require the issuance of Notice of Suspension, Notice
of Disallowance or Notice of Charge as this pertains to deficiency in the collection
system.

2.9  	We recommend Management to:
Require the employees to refund immediately the unexpended balance of the cash advances and enforce the immediate liquidation of overdue advances by sending demand letters to the concerned otherwise, the Treasurer is advised to withhold or suspend the salaries of the erring accountable officers which had not settle their cash advances as of December 31, 2016 without prejudice to the other sanctions as provided for under the aforementioned regulations.
Strengthen measures to demand and assure the recovery of prior year cash advances made by officers and employees in and out of service.
Strictly adhere with the provisions of Sec. 89 PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 97-002 and EO No. 298 and other rulings mentioned above.

3.7  	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same, so that the Management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.
4.6	With the foregoing, it is recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant be guided with the observation above in the succeeding disbursement of CF.

5.11	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Technical Working Group (TWG) to strictly follow the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184, and disqualify participating bidder with incomplete documents/requirement. Likewise, prices of Bid documents should be uniformed to be fair with other participating bidders.

6.10	We recommend that the Management ensure that procurement of goods and services should be undertaken in pursuant to Rule IV, Section 10 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in order to have a wide dissemination of bid opportunities and participation of different private contracting parties and to obtain the most advantageous price. Likewise, indicate in the Attendance Sheet the Designation/Position of the attendees in order to determine if they are the official participants of the said activity.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend that Management:
To strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 4 (6) of PD 1445. Moreover, cause the immediate submission of the documentary deficiencies and the lacking details of the transactions.
To strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 10121 in planning and implementation of the projects, programs and activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.
To submit to the auditors updated list of serviceable vehicles of the Municipality with their corresponding plate numbers for verification of fuel consumption and validation purposes.
To exercise due diligence in the preparation of required documents to support government transactions to ensure accuracy, completeness and reliability of the same.

8.4	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to follow the provisions stated in Joint Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 8, 2015, to ensure the submission on time the documentary requirements needed in the granting of cash advances for Confidential Fund.

9.8 	We recommend that Management should:
Formulate a policy on the granting of financial assistance systematically determine the extent of aid to be given to individuals in need. Beneficiaries should be properly screened giving priority to the less privileged constituents.
Coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to assess request for medical and hospitalization assistance by way of Social Case Study Reports so that only the deserving indigents can be assisted and served.
Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to require the applicants for AICS a complete submission of supporting documents for the assistance being requested as a basis of assessment and recommendation to make sure that only the deserving ones were provided with the necessary assistance to help them cope during crisis situations.

10.9	We therefore recommend that Management follow the limitations imposed under COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and CSC and DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2015 dated November 25, 2015. Likewise, instruct the concerned officials to refund the excessive/unnecessary payments, otherwise, Notices of Disallowance will be issued on the same.

11.5	We recommend that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board:
Prepare a budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and
Request for inputs from the DepEd officials/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund.

12.4	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to review the granting of cash advances intended for the payment of payroll for salaries and wages before the issuance of check and follow strictly the provision of Section 4.2.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

13.6	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the BAC Secretariat, the copies of the required preparatory documents and subsequently, for the BAC Secretariat to file intact all the pertinent documents that form part of a contract. Likewise, the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contract within five (5) days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents and such other documents peculiar to the contract which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
14.7	We recommend therefore that Management follow the provisions stated in Section 34.8 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and to review the post-qualification report prepared by the BAC TWG.

15.6	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant and other Head of Offices concerned to submit the accounts and other financial reports of the Municipality regularly, on every 10th day of the succeeding month.

16.5	We recommend that the Municipality should implement the provisions of COA Circular 2007-001 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of fund transfers.
16.6	Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should review all outstanding accounts and record adjustments for fund transfers that actually partake of the nature of donations.
16.7	Further, the Municipality should institute action against NGO/PO’s and their officers/organizers that failed to implement the programs and projects funded by the Municipal government pursuant to the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement covering these transactions.

17.8	We therefore recommend that the Management observe the prudent use of scarce resources and follow the limitations imposed under COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and DILG Memorandum Circular No.2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 in order to minimize the cost of the conduct of these activities and save funds for other relative development projects. Travelling expenses which are found to be excessive and extravagant shall be disallowed in audit.
17.9	Moreover, follow strictly the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement which exceeds the threshold for shopping and small value item of Php100,000.00, should be done thru public bidding to be competitive and to obtain the most advantageous price.

18.12	We recommend that the Management:
Set a limit to the mobile communication allowance granted to the employees and provide justification/supporting documents on the reasonable basis/limit to the allowable amounts indicated in the Memorandum Order such as the result of study of the frequency of official calls, the name/office of the persons being contacted by LGU official and employees on official business in order to determine the average communication allowance needed for each individual.
Require the Accounting Office as an internal control unit to attach complete supporting documents for the claims (i.e. justification/supporting notes, reasonable basis/limit, certification from the officials & employees that the communication allowance was used for official business only, and other documents in needed in support of the claims) and proper classification of the account used in the charging of expenses.
Show the breakdown/detail of the amount stated in the Resolution No. 167 s. of 2015.

19.5	We recommend that Management direct those involved in planning and implementation to monitor closely and hasten the implementation of the projects. Likewise, maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilized the 20% Development Fund to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

20.5	We recommend that Management direct those involved in planning and implementation to monitor closely and to hasten the implementation of these projects/activities funded from National Government Agencies especially those releases in earlier years, so that the constituents can benefit from said projects. Likewise, please submit justification on the non-utilization of the funds.

We reiterated our recommendation to the management that cash advances be supported with estimate/breakdown of expenses to be paid, in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and Annex B of COA Circular 2012-03. Also, we reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer be granted cash advances only for salaries, wages, honorarium and petty operating expenses.
We reiterated our recommendation to the management to instruct the treasurer to pay disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00 directly to the payee through checks in compliance with Sections 2.2 and 4.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction:
The municipal treasurer was granted cash advance only for salaries, wages, honorarium and petty operating expenses; still pay through cash for purchases/disbursements above ₱15,000.00.
The list/ estimate of expenses was not stated in the face of disbursement vouchers for MOOE.
The municipality was able to establish their Petty Cash Fund on July 2016.
The municipality paid Infrastructure projects funded by 20% DF and Trust Funds through checks.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended:
 that the Municipal Engineer verify the reported accomplishments before certifying the Statement of Work Accomplished submitted by the contractor. He shall measure the value of the works actually in place in accordance with the contract; and
 the fund received from RA 7171 should have been used for the purpose stated in the supplemental budget; officials already made supplemental budget for RA 7171 this 2016.
 Municipal Officials should oblige the contractors to implement the projects otherwise, the payments will become the personal liability of the municipal officials who certified the correctness and validity of the claims.
observations.managementAction:
The municipality still pay projects for 2016 which have lower percentages of accomplishments stated in SWA of the contractor.
The municipality was able to make supplemental budget for RA 7171 this 2016.
